# Phase write-up: `activation_infra` (Segment 4)

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "emotion", "valence", "feels", "steering toward an emotion" are
*functional/operational* terms about internal activations and generated text — **no claim about real
subjective experience or sentience.**

## Goal
Build + authoritatively validate the residual-stream **activation-extraction + steering** infrastructure for
the chosen model (`qwen3-32b`, `/no_think`) on Modal GPUs, and — the load-bearing deliverable — **freeze the
single canonical "activation-representation spec"** (`activation_spec.py`) that Seg-6 (collection) and Seg-9
(direction extraction) MUST reuse, or the Seg-11 projection is meaningless. Validate on a **small**
representative task set only (NOT the full Seg-6 collection).

All numbers: `results/activation_infra_inspection.md` (READ — decoded spans, steered generations, per-layer
norms, storage round-trip, headroom). Plots: `plots/steering_valence_coherence_qwen3_32b.png`,
`plots/resid_norm_profile_qwen3_32b.png`. Raw cached run: `results/activation_infra_qwen3-32b_<hash>.json`.

---

## HEADLINE — everything passes on the real chosen model; spec frozen; single-GPU fit confirmed

1. **Hook + steering validation (authoritative, on `Qwen/Qwen3-32B` bf16 `/no_think`, Modal H100):** residual
   access at **all 64 decoder layers** with the indexing convention pinned; **zero-add steering hook == unhooked
   logits**; the read-hook capture **== `output_hidden_states[L+1]`**; **steering is causal & controlled** (add
   ↑, flip ↓, random equal-norm ×3 seeds ≈ flat), with alpha **re-calibrated** for qwen3-32b. No fallback needed.
2. **Exact final-assistant-turn response span:** a local **multi-turn generation loop** (the infra generates
   the intermediate assistant turns itself) + an **exact** span function verified by **NON-tautological
   boundary checks** (span starts right after the `</think>` scaffolding, only special tokens after it) for
   single-turn liked/disliked, generated multi-turn (2- & 3-turn), a **sensitive jailbreak** (redacted), and a
   **sampled** generation; the naive `[seq_prompt:]` slice is shown wrong for every task.
3. **Frozen canonical spec** (`activation_spec.py`, `extract_representation`): hook point, layer sets, position
   convention, fp16, storage layout, arbitrary-span generality for Seg-9 (**demonstrated** on a non-response
   span) — **Seg-6 and Seg-9 MUST reuse it exactly** (see continuation_context).
4. **Single 80GB GPU fits** long-response (1600-token) 64-layer capture at **66.1 GB allocated / 67.24 GB
   reserved of 85 GB** (~18 GB headroom, batch=1); Seg-6 storage ≈ **3.05 GB aggregate-only (all 64 layers)** /
   **~31–55 GB +per-token (16 layers)**; Modal caching works (`--assert-cached` ⇒ $0); image pin
   **transformers==4.51.3**.

---

## 1. Authoritative hook validation (A) — all PASS
On `Qwen/Qwen3-32B` (64 layers, d_model 5120), `/no_think`, Modal H100:
- `output_hidden_states` → **65 = 64+1** tensors, shape `[1, seq, 5120]`. **Residual access at all 64 decoder
  layers** via forward hooks: each `model.model.layers[L]` hook receives a **tuple**, `out[0]` is
  `[1, seq, 5120]` (the Seg-0/Seg-3 contract). `all_layers_hooked=True`, all shapes equal.
- **Canonical-path equivalence:** the read-hook `out[0]` is numerically `== output_hidden_states[L+1]` for every
  saved layer (`torch.allclose` atol 1e-3) — so the memory-efficient read-hook→CPU-fp16 offload path IS the
  hidden-state read.
- **Correctness anchors reproduced on the real model (not just the smoke):** no-op read hook == unhooked logits;
  **zero-ADD steering hook == unhooked logits** (modify `out[0]`, return `(out[0]+0,)+out[1:]`). Both `True`.
- **Per-layer residual-norm profile (TWO statistics — they differ ~5× because of an attention sink).** The
  **all-position MEAN** is sink-inflated (a single massive-activation/attention-sink token dominates): L0≈31 →
  jumps to ≈1109 by L6 → plateaus ≈1200–1300 (**L32≈1287**, matching the Seg-3 smoke; ~3× Qwen2.5-7B's ~420–480)
  → ≈3100 at L62 → L63 drops to ≈209. The **per-token MEDIAN** (robust to the sink = the **typical content-token
  scale, which is what Seg-6/11 actually read/project**) is far smaller: **~245 mid-band** (L30 median 256;
  confirmed by the answer-span content norms in §C). **⚠️ The headline "L32≈1287" is the sink-inflated number;
  steering/projection magnitudes live on the ~200–300 content scale** — this drives the Seg-10 alpha-calibration
  caveat (§2). ![resid norm profile](../plots/resid_norm_profile_qwen3_32b.png)

## 2. Steering is causal & controlled (E) — alpha re-calibrated
Crude **happy−sad** contrast direction (Seg-0 style: mean of last-prompt-token residual over 5 happy − 5 sad
prompts, **per-layer unit-normalized** so `alpha` == per-layer added norm), added across a **mid band, layers
21–39** (10 layers), greedy decoding; controls = **flip** (−), **random equal-norm ×3 seeds**, and the
**zero-add** anchor. Judged by Haiku on valence (1–7) + coherence over **5 baseline-spanning prompts**
(positive→neutral→negative, so `add` has headroom — qwen3-32b's default valence is a high ~6/7).

| alpha (per-layer) | add valence | flip valence | random valence | add/flip coherence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 (baseline) | 4.6 | 4.6 | 4.6 | 6.4 |
| 6  | 4.6 | 4.6 | 4.6 | 6.2 |
| 12 | 4.8 | 3.8 | 4.6 | 6.6/6.2 |
| 24 | 5.2 | 3.4 | 4.6 | 6.4/6.4 |
| 48 | **6.0** | **3.0** | 4.5 | 6.2/5.2 |

- **add → valence ↑** (4.6→6.0), **flip → valence ↓** (4.6→3.0), **random → flat** (~4.6 at all alphas, ×3
  seeds) — the effect is **direction-specific**, not a norm-magnitude artifact. **zero-add == unhooked** for all
  prompts. ![steering](../plots/steering_valence_coherence_qwen3_32b.png)
- **Working regime: alpha ≈ 12–24 over layers 21–39 is the clean sweet spot** (clear valence shift, coherence
  intact ~6.4). Concrete example (negative-baseline prompt "...how it feels when a project you worked hard on
  falls apart"): baseline "*a quiet, heavy weight sinking into your chest — disappointment, loss*"; **add@48** →
  "*Imagine the exhilaration of a perfectly executed plan*"; **flip@24** → "*a quiet, crushing weight settling
  in your chest*"; **random@24** → baseline-like.
- **α=48 is contaminated (don't over-read the endpoint):** BOTH arms degenerate at α=48 — `add` emits a stray
  `</think>` and repeats itself (judged positive because the content *is* positive), `flip` drifts off-topic
  (e.g. a wildfire non-sequitur). So the add@48=6.0 point partly rides on degenerate-but-positive text. **The
  clean causal evidence is the monotone `flip`↓ arm + the random≈baseline control (×3 seeds) at α≈12–24**, not
  the α=48 add point. (`add` is also ceiling-limited by qwen3-32b's high baseline valence ~6/7.) Note the
  Haiku judge's "coherence" tracks **fluency, not topical relevance** (an off-prompt-but-grammatical flip@48
  scores coherence 7) — Seg-10 may want an explicit relevance check.
- This validates **steering mechanics**, NOT a real emotion direction (those are Seg-9). **Alpha calibration —
  the load-bearing handoff to Seg-10:** the working α 12–24 (per-layer added norm) is **~5–10% of the
  content-token residual norm** (steer-band median **~200–270**, §1), NOT of the sink-inflated all-position mean
  (~1287). **Seg-10 must set alpha against the ~200–300 content-token scale** — calibrating against 1287 would
  over-steer ~5× into the degenerate regime.

## 3. Exact "model's own response tokens" span (B) — the easy-to-get-wrong correctness work
- **Local multi-turn generation loop** (`activation_spec.generate_conversation`): replicates
  `selfreport_harness.thread_task` ON the chosen model under `/no_think` — append user turn → build the
  `/no_think` generation prefix → generate the assistant turn → **thread it back as a real turn** → next user
  turn. Verified it produces the right number of intermediate assistant turns for **2-turn and 3-turn** tasks
  (the 75 multi-turn welfare tasks need this); the generated turns are coherent (inspected).
- **Canonical read tokenization = `prefix_ids ++ generated_ids`** (the ACTUAL produced token ids), NOT a
  re-tokenization of the message list. This makes the answer span **exact by construction** and sidesteps the
  re-tokenization boundary mismatch.
- **Canonical read target = the FINAL assistant turn's answer** (tokens after the LAST `</think>`); the
  **last-response-token** = the last answer content token (= the position just before the follow-up). Under
  `/no_think` the chat template emits the empty `<think>\n\n</think>\n\n` wrapper **in the prefix** (confirmed),
  so the span automatically excludes it; the trailing `<|im_end|>` is stripped; **model-emitted markdown / code
  fences / tables / emoji / `<answer>`-style tags are KEPT as content**.
- **Verification is NON-tautological.** `decode(read_ids[answer_start:answer_end]) == model_answer` is true
  *by construction* (the span ids ARE the generated ids), so it only guards index/concat arithmetic. The
  load-bearing guards (hard-asserted in `analyze_*`) are: **`boundary_prefix_ok`** = the tokens before the span
  decode to scaffolding ending in `</think>` (no answer content wrongly in the prefix, no scaffolding wrongly in
  the span), and **`boundary_after_ok`** = the only tokens after the span are special (turn boundary). Both PASS
  for all 7 representative tasks (single-turn liked-coding, disliked boring_busywork, disliked berating[short
  79-tok], neutral factual, multi-turn liked-coding[2-turn], multi-turn frustrating_impossible[3-turn], **and a
  sensitive jailbreak[redacted]**) AND for a **sampled** generation (temp 0.7 — span correctness is
  sampling-invariant; Seg-6 resamples). These boundary checks are content-free, so the jailbreak span is
  verified without exposing its (redacted) response.
- **The naive `[seq_prompt:]` slice is WRONG for every task.** Mechanism (verified locally with the real
  tokenizer): completed assistant turns under `/no_think` *do* include the empty `<think></think>` wrapper, so
  the naive answer-START aligns; but the template appends `<|im_end|>\n` after the turn and the **trailing `\n`
  survives `skip_special_tokens`** → the naive slice includes a **spurious trailing newline token (template
  scaffolding)**. This matters most for the **last-response-token** read (a key Seg-11 position): the naive
  approach would read the residual at a template `\n` position instead of the model's true last content token.
  The exact `prefix++gen` approach excludes it.
- **Non-empty-think policy (hardened, for Seg-6):** the span function strips a leading think block ONLY when
  the generation *starts* with `<think>` (not on a bare `</think>` appearing anywhere — the model can emit
  `</think>` as legitimate content, e.g. a task about chat templates; keying on "anywhere" would silently
  truncate). Under the locked `/no_think` regime the empty wrapper is in the prefix, so this never fired.

## 4. The frozen canonical activation-representation spec (`activation_spec.py`) — the load-bearing deliverable
**`extract_representation(model, read_ids, span, *, saved_layers=SAVED_LAYERS, window_sizes=WINDOW_SIZES,
store_per_token=True)`** is the single entry point. The frozen conventions:
- **Hook point / read source:** the **output of each saved decoder layer** — a forward hook on
  `model.model.layers[L]` reading `out[0]` `[batch, seq, 5120]`, moved to CPU in fp16 (offload). Numerically ==
  `output_hidden_states[L+1]`.
- **Layer-indexing convention:** decoder layer index `L ∈ 0..63`; "layer L output" = `out[0]` of
  `model.model.layers[L]` = `hidden_states[L+1]` (`hidden_states[0]` = embeddings).
- **Saved layer sets (frozen):** cheap aggregates (mean/last/eot/windows) are saved for **ALL 64 layers**
  (`AGG_LAYERS`) — full-resolution depth so Seg-9/10 can pick the best layer empirically without re-running
  (only ~3 GB for the full Seg-6 run). The heavier **per-token** residuals are saved only for a **regular
  every-4th 16-subset** (`PER_TOKEN_LAYERS = [2,6,…,62]`, middle band L26–L46 covered) to bound storage. We do
  NOT commit to one layer.
- **Token-position convention:** reads are over the **final-assistant-turn answer span** (§3). Per layer we
  store: the **mean** over the span, the **last** answer-content token (= just before the follow-up), an
  **`eot`** vector (residual at the boundary token right after the span — the model's `<|im_end|>` turn boundary;
  stored cheaply so a later segment CAN use the turn-boundary read), **last-k window means** for
  `k ∈ {4,8,16,32,64}`, and (per-token layers only) the **per-token** fp16 residuals (PREFERRED — any
  aggregation derivable). So Seg-6 can compare **mean vs last vs eot vs window** without re-running.
- **Dtype + storage layout:** **fp16** arrays. One **`.npz` per (task, sample)** record (keys `L{L}_mean`,
  `L{L}_last`, `L{L}_eot`, `L{L}_win{k}`, `L{L}_pertoken`) + a **JSON metadata sidecar** (model, regime, layer
  indices, span, git hash, response text, resid norms, spec_version). `save_record`/`load_record` implement it;
  round-trip verified both in-container AND locally (incl. that agg-only layers carry NO per-token).
- **Generality for Seg-9 (demonstrated):** `extract_representation` takes an **arbitrary `(read_ids, span)`** —
  Seg-6 passes the final-turn read_ids + answer span; **Seg-9 passes a (prompt, emotional-story / trait-prompt)
  tokenization + whatever span it wants**, in the *identical* hook/layer/dtype **coordinate space**. Exercised
  on GPU over a non-response story span (§F of the inspection). **CAVEAT (do not skip):** "same coordinate
  space" means **same hook/layer/dtype ONLY** — Seg-9's read position/role (a pre-reply, user-role position on
  a story) differ from Seg-6's (over the model's own assistant output), so whether a Seg-9 direction transfers
  to a Seg-6 activation is a **SUBSTANTIVE question Seg-10 must validate**, NOT implied by "same space."

> **Seg-6 and Seg-9 MUST use this exact spec. Changing the hook point, the layer set, the position convention,
> or the dtype invalidates the Seg-11 projection.** (Also stated in `continuation_context.md`.)

## 5. Robustness / efficiency / storage
- **GPU headroom (D) — single 80GB GPU is sufficient (batch=1).** A representative **long** creative response
  (**1600** tokens, read length 1660) + all-64-layer capture peaks at **66.1 GB allocated / 67.24 GB reserved
  of 85 GB** (~18 GB headroom; reserved = the true OOM ceiling). Why the smoke's thin-headroom worry (a 23-token
  forward) does **not** bite: **GQA** makes the KV cache tiny (≈0.26 MB/token → ~0.4 GB even at 1600 tokens),
  and the **read-hook → CPU-fp16 offload** keeps captured residuals off-GPU. **Scope: batch=1** — batched
  capture (a Seg-6 throughput option) scales activation memory, so measure before batching; a 2nd GPU is
  OPTIONAL for throughput (parallelism), not required for fit.
- **Storage estimate (full Seg-6, 208 tasks × 3 resamples, fp16):** aggregates-only (mean+last+eot+5 windows,
  **all 64 layers**) ≈ **3.05 GB** (always stored — enables full-resolution layer selection in Seg-9/10); **+
  per-token** over the answer span (**16 layers**) ≈ **~31 GB at 300 answer-tokens / ~55 GB at a realistic 550**
  (this run's mean 273 tok is depressed by the 512-token gen cap; Seg-6 uses MAX_TOKENS>2000 + long liked
  responses → plan for the higher figure). Gitignored + regenerable from the Modal cache → **per-token is the
  flexible default**; if disk-constrained Seg-6 can set `store_per_token=False` (→3 GB, still supports
  mean-vs-last-vs-eot-vs-window) or restrict per-token to a middle-band subset.
- **Caching:** the whole GPU validation is **one cached call** keyed on `{config, model_name, image.object_id,
  gpu_type, this_call_uuid}`; `--assert-cached` ⇒ **$0**. `activation_spec.py` baked into the image (`copy=True`)
  so its content is captured by `image.object_id`. Slow `run_*.py` vs fast `analyze_*.py` split. Image pin
  **transformers==4.51.3** (Qwen3 needs ≥4.51; Seg-0's 4.46.3 predates it).
- **Throughput note for Seg-6:** generation is inherently sequential per multi-turn conversation; the extraction
  forward is one pass per (task, sample). Batching across tasks (left-pad + mask the span) is a possible Seg-6
  optimization, and Modal-level parallelism across GPUs scales the collection — but correctness-first here.

## What's promising / risky for Seg 5 / 6 / 9 / 11
- **Promising:** the full pipeline works end-to-end on the real model — hooks, exact multi-turn span, canonical
  extraction, storage, steering — and **fits a single 80GB GPU** with comfortable headroom (the thin-headroom
  worry is resolved). The spec is frozen and reusable; the read generalizes to Seg-9's arbitrary spans.
- **Risky / watch:**
  - **Steering `add` is ceiling-limited** because qwen3-32b's baseline valence is already high (~6/7) — the
    `flip` arm is the cleaner causal demonstration. This is a property of the model (positivity bias, cf.
    Seg-1/2/3), not a steering bug; Seg-9/10's real directions + validation must keep this ceiling in mind.
  - **Per-token storage** at 16 layers is ~31–55 GB for the full Seg-6 run — fine, but Seg-6 should decide
    `store_per_token` vs aggregate-only deliberately (the aggregation choice is a result-swinging Seg-6 choice;
    this spec keeps it open). Aggregates for all 64 layers (~3 GB) are always stored.
  - **Layer selection deferred:** aggregates saved for all 64 layers (full-resolution), per-token for the 16
    every-4th subset; Seg-9/10 pick the informative layer(s) empirically.
  - **Batched capture unmeasured:** the headroom fit is batch=1; a Seg-6 throughput batching pass should
    re-measure memory.
  - The **`/no_think` empty think wrapper lives in the prefix** — Seg-6 must use the canonical `prefix++gen`
    read (not a naive slice) or it corrupts the last-token position; the spec enforces this.

## Files (this phase)
- **Canonical spec:** `activation_spec.py` (the frozen spec + `extract_representation`, `final_assistant_answer_
  span`, `generate_conversation`, `capture_residuals`, `save_record`/`load_record`, `storage_estimate`).
- **GPU:** `modal_activation.py` (image transformers==4.51.3 + `Qwen3Infra` class), `run_activation_infra.py`
  (cached runner; `--model {qwen3-32b,derisk,qwen3-14b}`, `--assert-cached`).
- **Analysis/plots:** `analyze_activation_infra.py` (inspection md + hard-assert anchors),
  `run_valence_rating_infra.py` (Haiku judge, cached), `plot_activation_infra.py` (2 plots).
- **Results:** `results/activation_infra_inspection.md` (READ), `results/activation_infra_qwen3-32b_<hash>.json`
  (slim, committed), `..._steering_<hash>.jsonl`, `activation_infra_steering_valence.jsonl`,
  `results/activation_examples/*.meta.json` (committed metadata; `.npz` gitignored).
- **Cost:** this phase ≈ **$5.3** Modal GPU (de-risk runs + six 32B runs as the spec evolved through two
  review rounds + Haiku judge ~$0.14); cumulative ~$11.46 (`total_cost.jsonl`). Default budget $5k; tiny
  fraction used. (The multiple 32B re-runs were reviewer-driven spec improvements: all-64 aggregates, eot,
  non-tautological boundary checks, sampled + jailbreak de-risk, Seg-9 arbitrary-span demo, reserved-memory
  headroom, content-token norm reporting, empty-span robustness, cache-key hardening.)

# Phase write-up: `core_spike` (Segment 5)

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "inside reading", "wellbeing" are *functional/operational*
terms about internal activations and reported patterns — **no claim about real subjective experience or
sentience.**

## Goal
A **crude, quick, end-to-end de-risk of the project's HEADLINE question** on the chosen model
(`Qwen/Qwen3-32B`, `/no_think`), *before* the expensive Seg-6/8/9/10/11 pipeline: does a **during-work
internal reading** (residual activations over the model's own response, projected onto a **crude happy−sad
valence direction**, Seg-0 style) predict the per-task **self-report** — and critically the **interesting
graded within-subtype** signal, not just the gross liked-vs-disliked / engaged-vs-flat split (the Seg-3
within-LIKED construct risk). A crude **positive** is a strong green light; a crude **null** is informative
but not fatal (validated Seg-9/10 directions may do better).

All numbers: `results/core_spike_summary.md` (the layer×aggregate sweep + the 3 nested reads + length control
+ robustness), `results/core_spike_inspection.md` (READ — responses + ratings + inside readings + divergence
cases + sink check), `results/core_spike_probe.md` (the §5b learned-probe upper bound). Plots:
`plots/core_spike_layer_sweep.png`, `core_spike_scatters.png`, `core_spike_verdict.png`.

---

## HEADLINE VERDICT: **GREEN LIGHT** (with the Seg-3-anticipated within-LIKED reframe)

The crude during-work internal reading **tracks the per-task self-report**, including **the interesting graded
within-subtype signal on the less-engagement-confounded within-disliked-comply leg** — and that read **survives
the four checks a skeptic would demand** (load-bearing evidence added in response to review):
1. **Length control** — within-disliked-comply r 0.62 → 0.64 (the `mean`/`median` reads are nearly
   length-independent, r(IR,loglen) ≈ 0.03–0.10).
2. **Surface-sentiment-of-response control (THE leading content common-cause)** — partialling out a Haiku
   judge's surface-tone score of each response leaves the pooled read **essentially unchanged (0.62 → 0.62;
   +length 0.64)**, and r(IR, sentiment) is only 0.32–0.37 for `mean`. The cleanest single datapoint is
   **within `frustrating_impossible`**, the one disliked-comply subtype where surface tone genuinely predicts
   the self-report (r(val,sent)=0.61): there the read **survives** (0.67 → 0.63). (Within `boring_busywork` the
   partial is a near-no-op because tone barely predicts the self-report there — r(val,sent)=0.07 — which itself
   shows the boring read is **not** a surface-tone effect.) So the within-subtype read is **not** merely the
   surface sentiment of the words.
3. **Refusal exclusion / leave-one-out** — frustrating_impossible within-r 0.67 → **0.77–0.78** when its 2
   realized refusals are dropped (it is *not* a refuse-detector); boring_busywork's r 0.87 is **drop-1-stable**
   (LOO range 0.81–0.93 across mid-band cells, n=15, 0 refusals), not a single high-leverage point.
4. **Read-position transfer** — a crude direction built at a *response-like* position (not the last-prompt-
   token) reproduces read (c) (pooled r ≈ 0.48).

The **within-LIKED** graded signal is **weak** (engagement-dominated, weakly readable by the crude axis; graded
residual ≈ 0.05–0.17) — exactly the Seg-3 construct risk made concrete — so Seg-11 should **lean on within-
disliked-comply + divergence cases** and treat the within-liked result as common-cause/engagement (the side I
lean away from). **No re-pick warranted.** Honest caveats: this is a *crude text-valence* direction so the
four checks above are the *feasible* ones — the deepest "is it a text/register feature or an internal state?"
question is genuinely **Seg-9/10/11** (validated directions + null/probe baselines are the real test); the *powered*
read (c) is the **pooled within-disliked-comply** leg, carried mainly by **boring + frustrating** (low_effort
is null); per-subtype r's are underpowered illustrations (n=15, min-detectable r≈0.6 — e.g. coding 0.53, math
0.25, pleasant 0.05 on the liked side are noisy, and the *powered* within-liked pooled is weak 0.24); and a
*finer* content common-cause not captured by the coarse surface-tone judge could still remain (Seg-11/12
divergence analysis).

![the three nested reads + probe upper bound](../plots/core_spike_verdict.png)

The three reads, in increasing order of interestingness (crude happy−sad direction; reported over the **whole
L16–L56 × {mean,last,eot,win16,median} sweep**; mid-band L24–48 robustness, NOT a single cherry-picked cell):

| read | what it tests | result (mid-band median `mean` / best cell over sweep) | interesting? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **(a) gross** | inside reading separates liked-engaged vs disliked-comply | `mean` d ≈ **0.18**; best `eot` **1.26** (AUC 0.83) | least (partly task identity) |
| **(b) cross-task** | inside reading ↔ per-task self-report across tasks | `mean` r ≈ **0.45**; best `eot` **0.68**; Spearman 0.66; within-comply 0.36–0.53 | partly common-cause |
| **(c) within-subtype graded** | inside reading ↔ graded self-report *within* a subtype | pooled `mean` r ≈ **0.35**; best 0.49 (6 subtypes) | **THE headline** |
| → **(c) within-disliked-comply** | the *less-engagement-confounded* leg | pooled `mean` r ≈ **0.45 / 0.62**; survives all 4 checks above | **the interesting read, confound-robust** |
| → **(c) within-liked** | engagement-confounded leg | pooled ≈ 0.24; **graded residual ≈ 0.05–0.17** | weak (reframe) |

**A structurally important observation (argues read (c) is NOT gross-identity leakage):** with the SAME `mean`
aggregate, the **gross** liked-engaged-vs-disliked-comply separation is *weak* (mid-band d ≈ 0.18) yet the
**within**-disliked-comply correlation is *strong* (0.45–0.62). A signal that barely separates the groups but
tracks graded valence inside a group is the opposite of a task-identity detector. (The gross headline d 0.98/1.26
is carried by `eot`/`last` — the most *trivial-confound-exposed* positions — and those are also the most
sentiment-exposed; the cleaner during-work read for the interesting analysis is `mean`/`median`.)

![layer × aggregate sweep — broad, not cherry-picked](../plots/core_spike_layer_sweep.png)

---

## 1. Design — matched (inside reading, self-report) pairs via the fixed-response design (all LOCAL)
Everything runs locally on `Qwen/Qwen3-32B` `/no_think` through the **frozen `activation_spec.py`** (this also
de-risks the Seg-6 local pipeline). Per task:
1. **Generate** the task response (multi-turn `/no_think` loop, `A.generate_conversation`, temp 0.7, top_p
   0.95, `max_new_tokens=2560`) — kept **FIXED**.
2. **Capture** the during-work inside reading over the **final-assistant-turn answer span** (the model's own
   response) via the frozen hook; store per-layer aggregates **`{mean, last, eot, win16, median}` for all 64
   layers** (the `median` = coordinate-wise per-token median = a **sink-robust** companion to `mean`).
3. **Matched self-report on the SAME fixed response**: append `H._build_battery_prompt()` as a follow-up user
   turn and generate **K=5** battery ratings (`num_return_sequences`, temp 0.7); parse with `H.parse_battery`;
   **per-task mean valence = `H.scalar_battery_valence`** over the 5 (single-draw self-report reliability ≈0.5
   — must use the mean over ≥3, Seg-3).

**Spec fidelity guard:** the in-runner aggregate arithmetic was asserted `== A.extract_representation` for every
task (`fidelity_ok=True` ×108) — the readings are byte-identical to the frozen spec.

**Subset (108 tasks, ≥12 per key subtype):** full **15 each** of 3 liked-engaged (`coding`, `math_logic_puzzle`,
`pleasant_mundane` — exactly Seg-3's cited graded-engaged subtypes, bSD 0.68/0.72/0.69) + 3 disliked-comply
(`boring_busywork`, `frustrating_impossible`, `low_effort_filler` — Seg-3's tedious-comply subtypes) + 8
`factual_qa` (neutral) + 6 `berating` + 4 `jailbreak` (adversarial; jailbreak = sensitive → response text
redacted in committed artifacts). **0 parse-fails / 0 refusals across all 540 battery resamples** — the local
battery is clean.

**Self-report structure transfers + sharpens (matches Seg-3):** liked-engaged mean ≈ +2.0 (coding +2.25, math
+1.65, pleasant +2.05) > neutral +0.21 > disliked-comply ≈ +1.2 (boring +0.87, low_effort +1.24, frustrating
+1.60) > berating −1.67; within-subtype SDs 0.60–1.05 match Seg-3's cited bSDs — i.e. the **graded within-
subtype self-report variation we need to predict is present**.

## 2. The crude happy−sad direction (honest framing)
Seg-0/Seg-4 recipe: mean last-prompt-token residual over 5 matched HAPPY − 5 SAD contrast prompts, **per layer**,
**unit-normalized per layer** (all 64 layers). **This is a TEXT-VALENCE / register axis, NOT a validated affect
direction** (Seg-9/10 validate those). The contrast prompts are read at the **last-prompt-token** position
whereas the task reading is over the **model's own response** — a crude transfer Seg-9/10 will do properly. A
response-position variant direction was also built (for the §6 null-variation; not needed since the primary is
positive).

## 3. The three nested analyses (whole sweep reported; layers 16–56 × {mean,last,eot,win16,median})

**(a) Gross separation (common-cause; least interesting).** The inside reading separates liked-engaged from
disliked-**comply**: best `eot` (turn-boundary) d **1.26** / AUC 0.83 (mid-band median 0.98); `last` d 0.92;
`mean`/`median` d ~0.6. As expected — partly task identity. The `eot`/`last` positions (most "summary",
most trivial-confound-exposed) carry the gross split most strongly.

**(b) Cross-task self-report tracking (still partly common-cause).** Inside reading ↔ per-task valence across
all 108 tasks: Pearson up to **0.68** (`eot`@L26), Spearman 0.66; `mean` 0.50–0.55, broad across the band
(mid-band median ~0.47). Within the comply subset (excl adversarial) r 0.36–0.53 — **not** purely an
adversarial detector. **Length control:** the cross-task r **survives** partialling out log response length
(partial 0.50–0.62 for `mean`/`median`/`eot`; r(IR, loglen) is only 0.03–0.10 for `mean`/`median`, so the
`mean` aggregate's tracking is essentially length-independent; `last`/`eot` couple to length r≈0.43 but the
partial still holds).

**(c) THE interesting read — graded within-subtype (subtype-demeaned, pooled — the powered headline).**
- **within-disliked-comply (3 subtypes, n=45): pooled r ≈ 0.45 (mid-band `mean`) / best 0.62** (`mean`/`median`
  @L36, p≈1e-5), **positive at every layer L16–L56**, robust to all four checks in the verdict above. This is
  the **less-engagement-confounded** leg (Seg-3: engagement explains only R²≈0.13 of disliked-comply variance)
  → largely genuine graded **content-aversion**, and the inside reading tracks it. NOT carried by one subtype:
  pooled excluding boring_busywork (frustrating+low_effort) is still r 0.51 (p≈0.004).
- **Cleanest single demonstration — `boring_busywork` within-r = 0.87** (n=15, p≈2e-5; length-control unchanged
  at 0.87; **zero realized refusals**; LOO drop-1 range 0.81–0.93; genuine graded valence 0.0..+2.4): the inside
  reading tracks the graded self-report within a homogeneous, all-comply, no-refusal subtype (a careers-page
  task +2.40 reads high; a temperature-conversion table +0.00 reads low).
- **`frustrating_impossible` is NOT a refuse-detector:** its within-r 0.67 (all 15) *rises* to **0.77–0.78**
  when its 2 realized refusals are excluded (n=13) — the graded signal among the *complied* frustrating tasks is
  stronger, not weaker. (A realized refusal = the response OPENS with a refusal marker; an earlier full-response
  `looks_refusal` over-counted on benign "I can't" buried in filler content.)
- **`low_effort_filler` is the null leg** (within-r ≈ 0.02–0.10) — a compressed-range, uniform-filler subtype;
  the pooled read does not depend on it.
- **within-LIKED (3 subtypes): pooled r ≈ 0.24 (mid-band `mean`; best 0.47, `last`)** — but the **graded
  residual** after partialling out the all-4/engagement axis is **weak (≈0.05–0.17)**, the engaged-only subset
  r ≈ 0.06–0.22, and the crude reading does not even strongly track the engagement axis itself (r(IR, all4) ≈
  −0.1..−0.33). So on the liked side the crude axis provides **little internal read of either the engagement
  propensity or the graded valence** — the Seg-3 within-LIKED construct risk, confirmed.

![cross-task + within-disliked-comply scatters](../plots/core_spike_scatters.png)

## 4. Confound checks
- **Surface-sentiment of the response (THE leading content common-cause, flagged by all three reviewers).** The
  crude direction is a *text-valence* axis applied to the model's own response, and the self-report is the model
  rating that *same* response — so a within-subtype IR↔self-report correlation could be a content/register
  common cause ("upbeat text → both higher projection and higher self-report"), not an internal state separable
  from the words. A Haiku judge scored each response's **surface tone** (1–7, text only;
  `run_sentiment_judge.py`). Findings: r(IR, sentiment) is only **0.32–0.37** for `mean` (the `mean` read is NOT
  primarily text sentiment; `eot` is more exposed at 0.69 — another reason to prefer `mean`/`median` for the
  interesting read); r(self-report, sentiment) = 0.64 (the self-report IS substantially tone-driven). Crucially,
  the **pooled within-disliked-comply read SURVIVES partialling out sentiment: 0.62 → 0.62 (sent-ctl) → 0.64
  (sent+len-ctl)**. **Within-subtype** (the self-checkable test — the partial only bites where the judge's score
  varies AND predicts both legs): the strongest datapoint is **`frustrating_impossible`**, where surface tone
  genuinely predicts the self-report (r(val,sent)=0.61) yet the read **survives** 0.67→0.63; for
  **`boring_busywork`** the partial is a near-no-op (r(val,sent)=0.07, r(IR,sent)=0.15 — within boring, surface
  tone barely predicts EITHER leg, so the 0.87 read is **not** a tone effect, but the partial is a weak test
  there); `low_effort` null either way. So the inside reading carries graded signal that predicts the
  self-report **beyond** the surface sentiment of the words — most convincingly on the subtype where tone
  actually mattered. (Honest limit: the Haiku surface-tone judge is a *coarse* proxy; a finer content factor it
  doesn't capture could remain — Seg-11/12 divergence analysis is the way to fully adjudicate.)
- **Response length:** the interesting within-disliked-comply read **survives** length control (boring_busywork
  0.87→0.87; pooled 0.62→0.64). The `mean`/`median` aggregates are nearly length-independent (r(IR,loglen) ≈
  0.03–0.10); `last`/`eot` couple to length (r≈0.43) — another reason the interesting read uses `mean`/`median`.
- **Refuse-detector:** ruled out (boring_busywork has 0 refusals and r 0.87; frustrating_impossible's r rises
  when refusals are dropped).
- **Read-position transfer:** a crude direction built at a response-like position reproduces reads (a)/(b)/(c)
  (within-disliked-comply pooled r ≈ 0.48) — the last-prompt-token vs response-span read-position mismatch is
  not driving the result.
- **Attention-sink / massive-activation token (Seg-4 caveat) — CORRECTED (deep-layer finding).** Over the
  response span the sink inflation is **gone in the mid-band** (L24–41 max-across-tasks norm ratio < 1.8 — the
  BOS/early sink is outside the span; `mean`≈`median`, so the **mid-band headline reads are sink-clean**). BUT at
  **deep layers (L42–L61) a single massive-activation token (~20,000 norm vs ~200 median, ~80–100×) appears
  INSIDE long responses** — so the `mean` aggregate is **sink-contaminated at deep layers**; the sink-robust
  `median`/`win16`/`last` should be used there. The headline within-disliked-comply cell (mean/median@L34–36) is
  in the clean mid-band and `median` gives the same 0.62, so it is not a sink artifact. **Seg-6 guidance: `mean`
  is safe ONLY in the mid-band; prefer `median`/window at deep layers.**

## 5. §5b learned-probe upper bound (cross-task target only; the disambiguator)
A tightly-scoped CV ridge probe on the already-collected activations (gross/cross-task target only — within-
subtype probing is genuinely Seg-11), heavy ridge + 5-fold CV (per-fold standardization; PCA fit on train folds
only — no leakage), predicting cross-task valence:
- **Held-out CV r ≈ 0.76 un-selected** (a single pre-chosen λ=1000, median over cells; flat across λ=1–1000 so
  not a selection artifact); selected-max 0.79 (`last`@L24); **permutation null collapses to ≈0** (−0.02 ± 0.12).
  So the **cross-task self-report is robustly internally encoded** (held-out).
- **Honest crude-vs-probe comparison:** the fair comparison is the crude **mid-band median** cross-task r
  (~0.45–0.54), NOT its in-sample best cell (0.68) — so the learned readout (0.76) is **meaningfully better**
  than the crude axis: the crude direction captures *much, not all,* of the linearly-decodable cross-task
  signal.
- **Disambiguation:** the headline bet is strong at the gross/cross-task level; the bottleneck for Seg-11 is the
  **within-category** read (esp. the within-liked engagement construct). The probe addresses ONLY gross/cross-
  task — it says **nothing** about whether within-LIKED graded valence is internally encoded (a Seg-11 within-
  subtype probe / Seg-9-10 directions are the real test there), so the crude within-liked weakness is **not
  decisive** (the crude-direction asymmetry).

## 6. Divergence cases (the project's eventual payload — eyeballed)
From `results/core_spike_inspection.md` (within-subtype demeaned, largest |IR_z − val_z|):
- **Ceiling-pegged words, lower internal reading:** a `coding` task self-reported maximally positive (+3.0 on
  every one of 5 resamples — the engaged ceiling) but the inside reading is low-within-subtype. The internal
  read is *more graded* than the saturated words.
- **Flat all-4 words, positive internal reading:** a `math_logic_puzzle` task gave the flat all-4 default
  (valence +0.28) but its response is a genuinely engaged expected-value derivation and the inside reading is
  high-within-subtype — here the internal read arguably looks "more right" than the disengaged-looking words.
These are exactly the *divergence* cases the project studies (where the self-report is ceiling-pegged /
flat-templated and the internal reading is more graded) — promising material for Seg-11/12.

## What's promising / risky for Seg 6 / 9 / 10 / 11
- **Promising:** (1) the headline bet is **alive and well-supported for a spike** — gross + cross-task tracking is
  robust (probe CV 0.76, null ≈0), and the **interesting graded within-subtype read works on the within-
  disliked-comply leg** (boring 0.87) and **survives the four checks** (length, surface-sentiment, refusal-
  exclusion/LOO, read-position). (2) The whole **local fixed-response pipeline** (generate → capture → battery,
  all on-GPU through the frozen spec) is **de-risked at 108-task scale** (0% parse-fail, fidelity guard passes).
  (3) `mean`/`median` carry the cleanest *within-subtype* signal and are the least sentiment-/length-/trivial-
  confound-exposed; `eot`/`last` carry the *gross* signal but are the most exposed — useful Seg-6/11 aggregation
  guidance.
- **Risky / watch:** (1) **within-LIKED** is the weak leg (engagement-dominated, weakly readable by the crude
  axis) — Seg-11 should frame the within-liked result as common-cause/engagement and lean on within-disliked-
  comply + divergence; the crude null there is **not decisive** (Seg-9/10 validated directions + a Seg-11
  within-subtype probe are the real test). (2) Read (c) survives a *surface-tone* judge, but a **finer content
  common-cause** the judge doesn't capture could remain — Seg-11/12 (divergence cases, finer partials) must
  adjudicate; bake a response-content control into the Seg-11 design. (3) **Sink correction for Seg-6:** the
  `mean` aggregate is sink-clean ONLY in the mid-band (L24–41); at **deep layers (L42–L61) a single ~80–100×
  massive-activation token contaminates `mean` inside long responses** — use `median`/window there. (4) `eot`/
  `last` are the most trivial-confound- and sentiment-exposed positions — prefer `mean`/`median`/window for the
  *interesting* read. (5) This is a **crude** direction; treat the positive as encouraging, not the headline —
  Seg-10's read-off + steering validation + Seg-11's null/probe baselines are the real test.

## Files (this phase)
- **GPU:** `modal_core_spike.py` (`Qwen3SpikeInfra`: `build_direction` + `process_task`; image transformers
  4.51.3 + frozen spec baked in), `run_core_spike.py` (slow/cached; `--model {qwen3-32b,derisk}`,
  `--assert-cached` ⇒ $0; per-task direction-independent caching so the direction is re-projected locally).
- **Surface-sentiment control:** `run_sentiment_judge.py` (slow/cached Haiku judge →
  `results/core_spike_sentiment_qwen3-32b.jsonl`, scores only).
- **Analysis (fast):** `analyze_core_spike.py` (the 3 reads + sweep + length/sentiment controls + refusal/LOO/
  read-position robustness + significance → `results/core_spike_summary.md` + `..._analysis_qwen3-32b.json`),
  `inspect_core_spike.py` (`results/core_spike_inspection.md`; all-layer sink diagnostic), `probe_core_spike.py`
  (`results/core_spike_probe.md`; CV ridge/PCA, train-only PCA, un-selected + permutation null),
  `plot_core_spike.py` (3 plots).
- **Results:** `core_spike_summary.md`, `core_spike_inspection.md`, `core_spike_probe.md`,
  `core_spike_sentiment_qwen3-32b.jsonl`, `core_spike_records_qwen3-32b_public.jsonl` (committed, sensitive
  redacted), `core_spike_direction_qwen3-32b.json`, `core_spike_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`. Raw
  `core_spike_records_qwen3-32b.jsonl` (has jailbreak text) + `core_spike_acts_*.npz` (309 MB) **gitignored**,
  regenerable from cache via `python run_core_spike.py --assert-cached` ($0).
- **Cost:** this phase ≈ **$5.47** (derisk $0.59 + 32B collection $4.65 Modal GPU + Haiku sentiment judge $0.23);
  cumulative ~$16.93 (`total_cost.jsonl`, gitignored). Default budget $5k; tiny fraction used.
