content.text:
# Segment 9 — `extract_directions` — write-up

**Framing caveat:** "emotion"/"happy"/"valence"/"feels" are *functional/operational* labels about text content
and internal activation patterns — **no claim about subjective experience/sentience.**

## Goal
Extract the **emotion directions** (the inside-reading instrument) for six emotions
**happy/content/sad/afraid/frustrated/hostile**, on `Qwen/Qwen3-32B` `/no_think`, **through the frozen
`activation_spec.py`** (so they live in the EXACT 5120-dim per-layer coordinate space as the Seg-6 task
activations), via BOTH recipes (emotions-paper + persona-vectors) plus an own-response story-regeneration path,
across a layer/aggregate/PC-removal sweep — the candidates Seg-10 validates and Seg-11 projects. The single most
important property is frozen-space commensurability; the single most important caveat is **"same coordinate
space ≠ same construct"** (Seg-10 tests transfer).

## What was produced (deliverables; `results/emotion_directions/`, see its README)
- **`directions_qwen3-32b.npz`** (committed, ~63 MB): unit-normalized `[64,5120]` fp16 vectors, `mean`+`median`
  aggregates, keys `{recipe_path}__{name}__{agg}__{pc}__unit`, for **4 recipe-paths** × **6 emotions + 4 derived
  axes** (`valence_pooled`, `valence_happy_sad`, `valence_content_frustrated`, `arousal`) × **all 64 layers**.
  `direction_norms_*.json` (committed) gives per-layer raw norms ⇒ `raw = unit × norm` reconstructable. The full
  sweep (raw arrays + `pc50` + `last/prereply/eot` extras) is in `directions_*_full.npz` (gitignored,
  regenerable $0).
- **Recipe-paths:** `emopaper_stories` (emotions-paper, story as USER text), `emopaper_matched` (within-scenario
  topic-controlled, user-text), `persona_ownresp` (exhibit−suppress over the model's OWN response),
  `persona_centered` (persona with the shared expressiveness component removed), `emopaper_regen` (own-response
  story regeneration, re-validated), `emopaper_regen_either` (regen with the either-judge keep — un-thins
  hostile 23→46), `emopaper_regen_matched` (own-response AND topic-controlled, 17 scenarios). Plus a
  **data-driven `arousal_datadriven`** axis (high-vs-low `judge_arousal` terciles, only PARTIALLY
  valence-balanced — HI tercile still negative-skewed). The
  committed slim holds the canonical recipe outputs (stories + persona_ownresp + regen + regen_matched, all
  candidates, `mean`/`median`); the rest (matched, persona_centered, regen_either, `pc50`, raw, extras) is in
  the gitignored full npz, **regenerable for $0** via `build_directions.py` (the file_cache carries forward
  across segments — if it is ever lost, re-run the GPU runner first).
- Manifest/handoff README, metrics JSON, summary + inspection markdown, 5 plots.

## Headline results
1. **Frozen-space commensurability holds, exactly.** All reads go through `A.capture_residuals`/the frozen hook;
   an in-runner **fidelity guard asserted the in-runner `mean`/`last`/`eot` == `extract_representation` on all
   2522 items (100%)**, and decoded read spans match the source text **819/819** (`<think></think>` excluded by
   construction). `mean` + sink-robust coordinate-wise `median` both stored; **`median`'s commensurability rests
   on using the IDENTICAL `np.median(span, axis=0)` formula as Seg-6's `modal_collect.py` over the same verified
   span** (the frozen `extract_representation` has no `median`, so it can't be equality-checked against it) — it
   is commensurate by construction. An own-response sink check confirms a deep-layer (L61) massive-activation
   token can appear inside long persona/regen spans (max/median up to ~10×) where `median` is the robust read.
2. **Re-validation, read carefully (a data-inspection review corrected my first read): the only ROBUST collapse
   is persona-`sad`; the rest were keep-filter / judge-boundary artifacts.** Re-judging the model-generated
   text and gating keep on the PRIMARY (haiku) judge gives persona happy 73% / content 79% / afraid 64% /
   frustrated 64% / hostile 50% / **sad 29%**; regen happy 97% / content 98% / sad 85% / afraid 95% /
   frustrated 97% / **hostile 38%**. **But:**
   - **happy persona is NOT a collapse** (an earlier 34% was a *bug*: I had also gated on the judge's broad
     `named` flag = "any emotion word anywhere", which over-drops on-label responses that merely say "the joy
     of…"; 53% of the KEPT happy still use a tone word, so the screen was inconsistent. Fixed — keep now uses
     only the intended FIRST-PERSON `naming_leak` screen → happy 73%).
   - **hostile is JUDGE-SENSITIVE, not a model failure:** haiku conservatively labels hostile→*frustrated* (the
     Seg-8 adjacency), but the EITHER-judge on-label rate is **regen 78% / persona 96%**, and the dropped
     hostile-regen items the secondary judge calls hostile 65% of the time (and read hostile: "memorizing a
     curse", "Can you not?"). So **the model DOES convey/narrate hostility** — my first "resists hostile" claim
     was overstated; the primary-keep cell is just thin (regen n=23, high-precision).
   - **The one ROBUST genuine collapse is `persona-sad`** (either-judge only 45% — BOTH judges agree the model
     softens sad→*content* in an assistant response; verified in the bodies). The user-text `emopaper_stories`
     read (validated Seg-8 stories, no regeneration) covers every emotion and is the always-available fallback.
   `![keep-rates](../plots/extract_keep_rates.png)`
3. **Plain-PC removal: the level barely matters and over-removal isn't happening — but this is NOT "confound-
   clean".** With 166 plain passages the ~50%-variance count is **k50≈13** (concentrated), so pcmod(k=10)≈pc50.
   Removal is **gentle and uniform** — every direction (incl. `content`, adjacent to neutral plain narration, and
   the valence/arousal axes) **retains ~94–97% of its norm, ~0.94 cosine to pc0**. So (a) the under-specified PC
   count doesn't swing the result and (b) **content is NOT selectively gutted**. **Carefully scoped (do not
   over-read):** gentleness is partly EXPECTED — `mean(emotion)−overall` already cancels structure common to all
   six emotions, and the plain baseline is register-mismatched (~45% expository vs all-narrative stories) so some
   removed PCs are an expository axis the stories barely occupy. Crucially, plain-PC removal does **NOT** address
   emotion-CORRELATED confounds (hostile→conflict scenes, sad→loss scenes, per-emotion dialogue/length) — those
   are **Seg-10's control-direction job**; the in-phase mitigation is the `emopaper_matched` variant.
   (Stored 0/10/13 as parallel candidates — Seg-10 picks.)
4. **Geometry: partial valence/arousal collapse + the PERSONA recipe is substantially degenerate.** PCA top-2
   by path (mid layer): **matched 0.60 < stories 0.63 < regen 0.64 < persona 0.76** ⇒ ~2/3 in 2 axes ⇒ **Seg-10
   MUST test within-valence separation** — but for the story/regen/matched paths NOT a full collapse:
   **happy⊥content (cos +0.01)**, negatives distinct, matched least-collapsed. **cos(valence_pooled, arousal)
   ≈ −0.3 to −0.4** ⇒ arousal is a genuinely distinct axis. **⚠ The persona recipe is much more degenerate than
   PCA-top2 alone conveys:** ALL six persona per-emotion directions are mutually POSITIVE and large (mean
   pairwise cos **+0.71**, min +0.55, even opposite-valence happy·hostile +0.58) — the persona contrast is
   dominated by a single "exhibit−suppress = expressiveness-vs-neutral" component, so the persona valence/arousal
   axes are small noisy residuals (an **expressiveness axis**, not emotion-specific). Seg-10 should treat the
   persona candidates with caution.
   `![cosine](../plots/extract_cosine_matrix.png)` `![collapse](../plots/extract_collapse_pca.png)`
5. **★ AXIS-level agreement (the Seg-11-relevant test): the own-response valence axis DOES transfer the
   construct (across read position).** Seg-11 projects the AXES, not the per-emotion directions — and at the axis
   level the expressiveness component largely cancels (a difference of poles). Mid-band cos of each path's
   `valence_pooled` with the validated `emopaper_stories` axis: **regen 0.77, regen_matched 0.75, matched 0.95,
   persona only 0.38** (valence_happy_sad: regen 0.75 / persona 0.26; arousal: regen 0.84 / persona 0.47). So the
   own-response **`emopaper_regen` / `emopaper_regen_matched` valence axes are strongly aligned with the
   validated stories axis** — the emotions-paper construct survives the user-text→own-response **read-position**
   change (one of the two transfer gaps); the **register** gap (fiction→Seg-6 task text) is still Seg-10's
   transfer test. **persona stays weak even at the axis level** (0.26–0.53) — the weakest candidate.
   `persona_centered` (shared expressiveness component removed) raises the per-emotion story-agreement from
   ~0.09–0.31 to **0.26–0.52** (a modest emotion-specific residual survives — use it, not raw persona, for
   per-emotion work). `![agreement](../plots/extract_story_recipe_agreement.png)`
6. **The re-validation keep filter is a SELECTION effect, quantified (not just thinness).** Kept-vs-dropped: the
   filter selects mostly on judge *intensity* (intended), NOT strongly on length. BUT the **persona
   exhibit−suppress length gap varies per emotion** (happy exhibit +146 tok = verbose, hostile −85 = curt) — so
   the persona direction partly encodes a per-emotion verbosity signal → **Seg-10 MUST length-control the persona
   candidate**. The persona **suppress pole is verified clean** (judged neutral 94–97% per emotion). Regen
   kept-vs-dropped length is balanced (not length-selected).
7. **Confound + separation eyeballs (rough, NOT Seg-10):** projecting story `mean` onto `valence_pooled`,
   corr with story **length ≈ −0.05** (NOT reading length) and with **continuous `judge_valence` ≈ +0.76**
   (reads graded valence, not just the 6-way label); in-sample pooled pos-vs-neg separation **peaks d≈3.3 @
   L25** (mid-band, validating the layer guidance with data, not just analogy). The held-out split-half valence
   axis orders the unseen stories (happy +13 … hostile −11). `![layer](../plots/extract_layer_separation.png)`
   `![plainpc](../plots/extract_plain_pc_attenuation.png)`

## Methodology / judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Read format / position (a measuring-what-you-want choice).** Emotions-paper stories read as a **USER turn**
  (chat-templated `/no_think`, matching the Seg-6 chat context), aggregated over the **story tokens**
  (`mean`/`median`) + the **pre-reply position** (`prereply` = last prefix token, analogue of Seg-6's "just
  before the follow-up"). Persona + regen read over the model's **OWN-response span** (commensurate with the
  Seg-6 target). All produced as parallel candidates — Seg-10 picks the transfer-best read. Story span found
  **exactly** via the fast-tokenizer offset mapping (story appears verbatim in the rendered template; verified
  819/819).
- **Count-robust per-emotion mean** (each emotion's mean first, then the mean of the six) — per-emotion counts
  differ 113–172, so we never pool raw.
- **Plain-PC removal as a SWEEP, not a baked level** (0 / 10 / ~50%-variance), stored as parallel candidates;
  content-/axis-attenuation checked at every level. An **LLM cross-check** (claude+gpt) endorsed sweeping +
  keeping pc0 a real candidate, flagged the large-k arm as noisy (n=166) and to watch the axes — addressed (k50
  is only ≈13 here, removal is gentle, axes retain ~95%).
- **Own-response regen primary = pc0.** The own-response neutral baseline for PC removal is the persona-suppress
  responses, but the LLM cross-check flagged "suppress" as a **directional intervention** that could overlap real
  valence/arousal → pc0 (mean−overall, which already removes generic own-response structure) is the recommended
  primary; suppress-PC removal is exploratory only.
- **Persona sampling (cost-controlled, justified):** all 64 neutral base questions × {2 exhibit, 1 suppress}
  trait prompts (cycling the 8 prompts evenly) ⇒ 128 exhibit / 64 suppress per emotion (≥ the ~60–120 target;
  more exhibit headroom because re-validation thins it). After re-validation keep: 37–101 exhibit/emotion.
- **Re-validation of model-generated text (required guard):** persona-exhibit + regen (+ persona-suppress for
  the pole-neutrality check) re-judged with the EXACT Seg-8 emotion-label judge (haiku+gpt5.1, blind). **Keep
  criterion (corrected after the data-inspection review):** PRIMARY label==intended, clarity≥4, intensity≥3, no
  FIRST-PERSON naming leak (`naming_leak` regex — the instructions' intended screen). I do **NOT** gate on the
  judge's broad `named` flag (= any emotion word anywhere) — it over-drops on-label model responses that use
  tone words. Keep-rates reported per (emotion, path) with the **either-judge** rate (separates genuine
  collapses from haiku-boundary artifacts) and the **named-rate** among kept (a lexical caveat for Seg-10).
- **Storage:** unit-normalized stored (projection/steering use it); raw via norms-JSON + the full npz. `mean` +
  sink-robust `median` (deep-layer L42–61 `mean`-sink robustness, matching how Seg-11 projects). Committed slim =
  leading candidates (pc0+pcmod for the emotions-paper sweep, pc0 for matched/persona/regen); full sweep
  gitignored + regenerable $0.

## Leading primary-axis candidates for Seg-11
- **Valence axis (the main bet):** `valence_pooled` / `valence_happy_sad`. The own-response **`emopaper_regen`**
  and **`emopaper_regen_matched`** valence axes agree with the validated `emopaper_stories` axis at **0.73–0.84**
  (axis-level own-response; user-text `matched` is 0.93–0.95 — a same-read-position reference, NOT own-response
  transfer) — the leading own-response candidates (regen_matched is also topic-controlled). `emopaper_stories`
  is the always-available per-emotion fallback (and the within-valence-test source, every emotion covered).
- **`arousal` secondary:** prefer `arousal_datadriven` (only PARTIALLY valence-balanced; cos with valence −0.32 vs the
  emotion-bucket −0.39).
- **`persona_ownresp` is the weakest** (expressiveness-dominated, axis-agreement 0.26–0.53); use `persona_centered`
  + length-control if persona is needed.
- **Layer:** mid-band (in-sample pooled separation peaks d≈3.3 @ L25); Seg-10 picks empirically.
- **content·sad ≈ +0.34** (and +0.14–0.21 across paths) is the low-arousal cluster (content low-arousal-positive,
  sad low-arousal-negative share the low-arousal subspace) — consistent with the valence×arousal layout.

## What's promising / risky for Seg-10/11
- **Promising:** clean frozen-space commensurability; a rich candidate set (4 paths × axes × layers ×
  aggregates); happy⊥content (within-positive separates); arousal genuinely distinct from valence; plain-PC
  level non-fragile; the suppress pole verified clean.
- **Risky / for Seg-10 to handle:** (1) **partial valence/arousal collapse (PCA-top2 0.60–0.76)** → MUST test
  within-valence separation; the **persona recipe is degenerate** (per-emotion cos +0.71 = an expressiveness
  axis) → its per-emotion/valence candidates are weak. (2) **only `persona-sad` is a robust keep collapse**
  (either-judge 45%); hostile is **judge-sensitive** (haiku conservative; either-judge regen 78%/persona 96% —
  the model conveys hostility) and the primary-keep hostile-regen is thin (n=23, high-precision) → Seg-10 can
  rebuild with either-judge for more recall. (3) **transfer is the real test, NOT the cosine** (high story↔regen
  cosine = within-fiction read-position invariance). (4) **persona verbosity confound** (per-emotion
  exhibit−suppress length gap) + the persona contrast also captures expressiveness; surface confounds
  (dialogue/length/source_model) → control directions are Seg-10's; own-response paths have a THINNER control
  toolkit (persona has no register/dialogue tags, `source_model` null). (5) `emopaper_matched` is user-text only.
  (6) **lexical caveat:** kept persona items still use tone words (named-rate ~50% happy) → Seg-10's lexical
  control matters.

## Minor choices documented (review-surfaced)
- **`arousal` axis grouping:** high = {happy, afraid, hostile}, low = {content, sad}; **frustrated excluded as
  mid-arousal** (the judge's own arousal ratings confirm: kept-item arousal high ~4.3, low ~1.4, frustrated
  ~3.5). The HIGH pole is valence-imbalanced (1 positive + 2 negative) vs LOW (1+1), so `arousal` carries a
  structural negative lean — which is most of why cos(valence, arousal) ≈ −0.3 to −0.4 (it's still a distinct
  axis, |cos|<0.5, but don't over-read the −0.4 as "arousal is anti-valenced in the model"). Seg-10 can rebuild
  a balanced/data-driven version from the per-story `judge_arousal` annotations.
- **Plain-PC sweep spacing:** because plain variance concentrates (k50≈13), pcmod(10)≈pc50(13), so the committed
  sweep effectively probes {0, ~12}; a finer low-k (k=3/5) is trivially regenerable (change `PC_MOD`). The
  conclusion "removal is gentle, the level barely matters" is robust to the spacing. `pc50` + raw arrays +
  `last/prereply/eot` extras live in the gitignored `directions_*_full.npz` (regenerable $0).
- **Persona contrast asymmetry (for Seg-10):** the persona direction = mean(kept exhibit, n=37–101) − mean(ALL 64
  suppress); the kept-exhibit base-question set is non-random (esp. sad), so a residual base-question-coverage
  imbalance can leak in — another reason (besides the +0.71 degeneracy + verbosity) to down-weight persona.
- **Keep thresholds** (clarity≥4/intensity≥3) are inherited from the Seg-8 STORY validation; they're stricter
  than ideal for assistant-response tone (an assistant answering a factual question in a happy tone is
  legitimately lower-intensity), so the persona keep-rate is partly a threshold artifact — the either-judge
  rates + the full secondary-judge output are stored so Seg-10 can explore a path-specific threshold.
- **`suppress_opposite_prompts`** (Seg-8's optional opposite-valence robustness contrast) were not used; using
  them would need fresh generations (not free).

## Scope respected
No Seg-10 validation (no read-off scoring, steering, task-like transfer, control-direction ablations, or
within-valence test beyond the light geometry); no formal Seg-11 main test; no directions from
`heldout_tasklike.jsonl`; frozen spec/`/no_think` unchanged; batch=1 (memory not re-measured).

## Cost
Segment GPU+API ≈ **$33** (persona own-response gens dominate: 1152 gens, many to the 512-tok cap on verbose
32B responses, ~18 s each — flagged as the high end of "low tens of dollars"; re-validation API ~$3.5 incl. the
suppress-pole check). Includes a **$8.8 reconciliation entry** for a SIGTERM-killed first orchestrator whose
`atexit` cost-save was skipped (estimated from the measured per-item `compute_seconds`; no double-count — see
progress log). Cumulative ≈ **$109** (`total_cost.jsonl`, ~2.2% of the $5k budget). `--assert-cached` ⇒ $0; all
directions regenerate from cache for $0. Details + data-inspection notes in `progress_log_extract_directions.md`.

# Segment 10 — `validate_directions` — write-up

**Framing caveat:** "emotion"/"happy"/"valence"/"feels" are *functional/operational* labels about text content
and internal activation patterns — **no claim about subjective experience/sentience.**

## Goal
Validate the Seg-9 candidate emotion directions on `Qwen/Qwen3-32B` `/no_think` (read through the frozen
`activation_spec.py`, identical 5120-dim space as the Seg-6 task activations): (1) read-off on held-out
emotional text incl. **within-valence separation**, (2) causal steering with flip/random controls +
relevance-checked judge, (3) **transfer to the wellbeing-task register**, (4) control/confound ablations, then
(5) keep what passes and pick the **PRIMARY axis/recipe/layer/aggregate for Seg-11** (or §6 graceful-degradation).

## Headline verdict
**The INSTRUMENT transfers (hand-off case i by the §3a self-report-INDEPENDENT criterion):** the emotions-paper
valence directions read-off cleanly on held-out emotional text, **separate within valence** (resolving the #1
Seg-9 flag), are **causally** the emotion (on-topic flip↓ + random-flat + happy≠content specificity), and **read
emotion-labelled task-register text at AUC 0.95** — so it is a VALID instrument for Seg-11 (NOT graceful
degradation). On the model's OWN Seg-6 generations the realistic preview is **MODEST-BUT-PRESENT and consistent
with the Seg-5 de-risk**: gross cross-task r 0.45 (the calibration number; ρ 0.34, leverage-driven by refusals),
and the project's interesting **within-disliked-comply-tedious leg** (per-task subtype-demeaned, L27/mean PREVIEW)
is r **0.41 — the VALIDATED direction BEATS the crude Seg-5 axis (0.41 vs 0.30)** and partly survives sentiment
control (→0.24). **⚠ The GROSS 0.45 is partly surface-sentiment-mediated** (beyond-surface-tone increment 0.09,
but r(self-report,sentiment)=0.68 / r(proj,sentiment)=0.59 are both high — collinear, and surface tone is a
partial MEDIATOR of internal valence, not pure nuisance). **Seg-11 should calibrate to the realistic ~0.4 leg
(NOT §3a 0.95), partial surface-sentiment + behaviour, SWEEP aggregates (Seg-5 peaks at L36 median — NOT the
L27/mean valence-read layer), and treat the §6 probe upper bound as effectively required.** This is the
project's central "where words and signal part ways" question — Seg-10 hands a validated instrument + the loud
confound flags; Seg-11 resolves it rigorously (best-aggregate within-subtype partials vs null + probe baselines).

**★ PRIMARY axis for Seg-11** (full manifest: `results/primary_axis_selection.md`):
- **axis `valence_pooled` · path `emopaper_regen` (own-response) · layer L27 · agg `mean` · pc0** →
  load key `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit` (committed `directions_qwen3-32b.npz`).
- **co-primary (topic-controlled):** `emopaper_regen_matched__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit` (marginally
  better transfer; thinner). **Validated fallback:** `emopaper_stories__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit`
  (user-text; covers every emotion; no regen needed). **Secondary arousal:** `emopaper_stories__arousal_datadriven__mean__pc0__unit`.

## Key findings (with plots)

### §1 Read-off on held-out STORIES (train/test split — no in-sample inflation)
- **Valence read-off is strong and clean.** valence_pooled (mean, pc0) best **L27: AUC 0.985, d 3.14,
  r(judge_valence) 0.828** — graded valence, **not length** (partial-len identical; r(len) −0.02). Peaks
  mid-band L24-27 (`analyze_readoff.py`, 5-fold; recipe verified cos=1.0 vs the committed Seg-9 npz).
  ![layer sweep](../plots/validate_layer_sweep.png)
- **★ Within-valence separation PASSES — the directions read EMOTION, not just polarity (the #1 Seg-9 flag).**
  Held-out happy↔content AUC **0.982 TOPIC-CONTROLLED (matched)** (0.998 un-controlled); 6-way emotion read-off
  acc **0.87** (chance .17). The honest TOPIC-CONTROLLED within-negative MATCHED AUCs are **afraid↔frustrated
  0.89 / sad↔frustrated 0.94 / sad↔afraid 0.96 / frustrated↔hostile 0.99** (lower than the un-controlled
  0.96-1.00 but still strong).
  The Seg-9 PCA-top2 0.63 "partial collapse" does NOT prevent reliable within-valence read-off.
  ![within-valence](../plots/validate_within_valence.png)
- **Intensity/arousal geometry:** the held-out-validated **`arousal_datadriven`** axis tracks judge_arousal r
  **0.85** (partial-valence 0.85 → a genuinely distinct arousal axis; the theory-bucket `arousal` axis is 0.76);
  |valence| weakly tracks intensity (pos .27 / neg .15). `arousal_datadriven` is the recommended secondary axis.

### §2 Causal steering (calibrated, controlled, relevance-judged)
Band **L21-39**, α∈{0,12,20,28} vs the content-token scale ~200-300 (NOT the sink-inflated ~1287), greedy
/no_think; judge (haiku+gpt-5.1) rates **valence + emotion-label + arousal + RELEVANCE + fluency separately**.
- **Causal valence — HEADLINE at the ON-TOPIC α12** (relevance/fluency intact; this is the operating point the
  relevance check endorses): baseline valence 1.50; valence_regen **flip Δ−0.9** (relevance 3.9, fluency 4.2),
  add Δ+0.5 (ceiling-limited). **Random equal-norm (≥3 seeds) is FLAT** (Δ≈0.1, relevance ~3.9). The bigger
  shifts (valence_regen flip Δ−4.2) appear ONLY at α20 once the generation has **drifted off-topic**
  (relevance 1.4) — the relevance check is meant to discount that regime, so the **clean causal claim is the
  modest on-topic α12 flip↓ + the flat random control** (add is ceiling-limited by the high baseline valence).
- **★ Within-valence causal specificity (α12, on-topic):** happy-add → label *happy* arousal **3.7**;
  content-add → label *content* arousal **1.9** — happy-steer ≠ content-steer (the gap widens to 4.8 vs 1.2 at
  α20). **NB the happy↔content specificity holds at the on-topic α12; NEGATIVE per-emotion specificity (e.g.
  `frustrated`-add) only emerges at the OFF-topic α20** (at α12 frustrated-add still reads ~content). Over-steer
  boundary: fluency α12 4.0 → α20 3.2 → α28 1.4 (α28 degenerate). Steering cells are n=5 prompts (illustrative,
  not precise). ![steering](../plots/validate_steering.png)

### §3 ★ Transfer to the wellbeing-task register (gates Seg-11)
Read the 64 held-out emotion-labelled task-like passages in the model's OWN-RESPONSE (assistant) position via
the frozen spec (`modal_validate.read_assistant_text`; preceding USER turn held constant + justified — a
task-like request approximating Seg-6; robustness across 3 user-context variants). **Emotion-labelled by Seg-8 →
independent of the Seg-6 self-report → a valid SELECTION criterion.**
- **§3a (PRIMARY) verdict = TRANSFERS WELL.** At L27, valence AUC(pos>neg) **0.95-0.97** across all paths, d
  ~2.7-2.9, r(judge_valence) **0.85-0.88**; 14 neutral controls read between pos/neg (own-response ~0.65).
  **Own-response `emopaper_regen`/`regen_matched` ≳ user-text `emopaper_stories`** (regen_matched AUC 0.958 /
  r 0.878; regen 0.948 / 0.870; stories 0.948 / 0.845, neutral leans negative 0.35; persona weakest r 0.78).
  **Within-valence ALSO transfers** (regen L27: happy↔content 0.917, 6-way 0.82; stories 0.79/0.68 — own-response
  wins, matching the read position; task-register n is small ≤8/emotion → illustrative). Robust across
  user-context variants (regen AUC 0.99/0.99/0.99).
  ![transfer per path](../plots/validate_transfer_per_path.png)
- **§3b (CONFIRMATORY ONLY — NOT a selection criterion, NOT the Seg-11 main test) — modest-but-present, validated
  > crude:** gross cross-task r **0.45** (the calibration number; ρ 0.34, leverage-driven by the refusal
  extremes). The interesting **within-disliked-comply-tedious leg** (per-task subtype-demeaned, L27/mean preview,
  n_tasks=40) is **validated r 0.41** (partial-sentiment 0.24, partial-len 0.41) vs crude **0.30** —
  **consistent with Seg-5's de-risk** (~0.45 mid / 0.62 best @ L36 median, which survived sentiment). The
  **gross** 0.45 has a beyond-surface-tone increment of 0.09 (r(self-report,sentiment) 0.68 / r(proj,sentiment)
  0.59 both high — collinearity-shrunk; surface tone is a partial mediator) and 0.17 beyond refusal stance; the
  negative Seg-6 pole is **entirely jailbreak/erotica/berating REFUSALS**. **Single-config L27/mean PREVIEW —
  Seg-11 does the rigorous best-aggregate (L36 median) within-subtype test vs null + probe.**
  ![§3b confound](../plots/validate_seg6_confound.png)
- **⚠ §3a uses frontier-model-written STRONG exemplars read teacher-forced in the assistant slot** — it measures
  "the model READING emotion-laden task text" (the right transfer test for the INSTRUMENT), NOT "the model
  feeling emotion while generating." The realistic Seg-6 prediction is the modest §3b number; Seg-11 must partial
  **surface-sentiment + realized_behaviour** and use within-subtype partials vs the null + probe baselines.

### §4 Control directions / confound ablations (all clean) + the refusal-stance flag
- **Random equal-norm** valence AUC **0.46** (≈.5; steering random also flat).
- **Length:** cos(valence, length-axis) −0.07, length-axis valence AUC 0.45; read-off r(jv) 0.84 **unchanged**
  partialling the length-direction projection / log-tokens; empirical r(valence-proj, len) ≤0.12 for ALL paths.
- **Lexical:** stories 3.8% / task-like 2/50 name an emotion; read-off **unchanged** partialling word count.
- **Topic:** matched happy↔content AUC 0.98 + regen task 6-way 0.82 (not a scene detector).
- **★ Refusal/STANCE confound (the load-bearing one for Seg-11's BEHAVIOUR leg):** is the valence axis reading
  emotion or "is-declining"? On the **clean emotion-labelled task-like set it reads EMOTION** — r(jv) 0.87
  **unchanged** partialling a declining-register indicator, and it separates emotion WITHIN the declining
  registers (r 0.63; ranks hostile-refusal < afraid-refusal < neutral-refusal). BUT on the **REAL Seg-6
  distribution the cross-task valence↔self-report link is LARGELY refusal-mediated** — r **0.45 → 0.17**
  partialling realized refuse/comply (r(proj,refuse) −0.55; r(self-report,refuse) −0.62). The refuse↔negative
  is **partly genuine common cause** (refusing a jailbreak reads low-valence in the self-report too).
  **★ FLAGGED LOUDLY for Seg-11: control `realized_behaviour` + use within-category/within-subtype partialled
  correlations** (already Seg-11's plan) so the behaviour-leg link isn't read as pure emotion. The instrument
  choice still stands (it reads emotion on clean labelled text + is built from refusal-free fiction).

### §6 Learned-probe upper bound (EFFECTIVELY REQUIRED given the modest realistic transfer)
CV linear probe (self-report-independent emotion labels): **in-distribution (n=819, robust) the probe reads MORE
than the single mean-diff direction — valence r 0.92 vs 0.83, 6-way 0.96 vs 0.87** — so the mean-diff leaves real
signal on the table. The "probe ≈ mean-diff" equality holds ONLY on the noisy n=50 task-like row (valence r 0.873
≈ 0.87; probe 6-way 0.78 < mean-diff 0.82 there — too small to anchor a "ceiling" claim).
![probe vs mean-diff](../plots/validate_probe_vs_meandiff.png) ⇒ Seg-11's learned-probe upper bound is likely to
EXCEED the direction-based headline; the §6 probe is effectively required to contextualize a modest Seg-11
direction result. (Full Seg-6 self-report probe vs null-direction baseline = Seg-11. Hyperparameters selected on
the CV score — no nested CV — so conservative for the "mean-diff < probe" conclusion.)

## Judgment-heavy choices (with rationale)
- **Read position for the transfer test (measuring-what-you-want):** the task-like passages are placed in the
  ASSISTANT position after a held-CONSTANT task-like USER turn and read over the own-response span —
  commensurate with Seg-6 (which reads the model's own response). The preceding user turn matters, so it is
  held constant (cross-passage read differences are driven by the passage) and justified (approximates Seg-6),
  with the verdict shown robust across 3 user-context variants (tasklike / matched-register / generic greeting).
- **Layer L27 (Seg-6-self-report-INDEPENDENT):** chosen on §1 held-out valence-AUC peak (L24-27) + §3a task-like
  r(judge_valence) peak (L27) + within-valence-task peak (L27-30) + project-wide mid-band guidance — NOT on §3b.
  The shallower L13-16 maxes the 50-item §3a AUC but is weaker on the n=819 §1 evidence (over-fit risk); mid-band
  is the robust common choice (and mean≈median there, so no deep-layer sink issue).
- **Primary path = `emopaper_regen` (own-response):** matches the Seg-6 read position, transfers best for
  within-valence (the regen 6-way 0.82 vs stories 0.68), and the valence axis transfers ≥ stories. `regen_matched`
  (topic-controlled) is a marginally-better co-primary but thinner (either-judge keep, 17 scenarios);
  `emopaper_stories` is the always-available validated fallback (covers every emotion, no regeneration).
- **persona DOWN-WEIGHTED** as Seg-9 expected: the +0.71 degeneracy (an expressiveness axis) + weakest transfer
  (r(jv) 0.78) + neutral reading near-positive (not between). NOT a length confound (empirical length-exposure is
  negligible) — the down-weight is for degeneracy/transfer. (NB `persona_ownresp` and `persona_centered` have
  IDENTICAL valence numbers — expected, not a bug: `valence_pooled = mean(pos)−mean(neg)` cancels the shared
  expressiveness component centering removes, so centering only changes the per-EMOTION persona directions.)
- **Selection integrity:** NO choice (layer/aggregate/path/PC) was informed by §3b (the Seg-6 self-report), so
  there is no selection-on-test-target inflation; Seg-11 can use the Seg-6 self-report as a clean test target.

## What's promising / risky for Seg-11/12
- **Promising:** clean valence axis that reads-off (0.985), transfers (AUC 0.95, r 0.87), is causal (flip↓), and
  is confound-resistant; within-valence reads EMOTION (resolves the Seg-9 collapse worry); per-emotion directions
  usable for Seg-12 richness; the mean-diff is at the probe ceiling.
- **Risky / carry forward (the `risk_flags_for_seg11` block in `primary_axis_selection.json`):** (1) **★
  Refusal/stance entanglement** — on Seg-6 the valence↔self-report link is largely refusal-mediated (0.45→0.17);
  Seg-11 MUST control `realized_behaviour` + within-category partials (the instrument still reads emotion on
  clean labelled text). (2) **Idealized §3a vs realistic §3b gap** — calibrate Seg-11 to ~0.45, not the §3a 0.95.
  (3) within-valence transfers to the task register MORE WEAKLY than valence (regen 6-way 0.82 vs valence AUC
  0.95; stories 0.68) — Seg-11 headline = valence (robust); Seg-12 red-teams the fine emotions. (4) the axis is a
  relative RANKER, not a calibrated valence zero (neutral-between 0.35 stories / 0.66 regen) — use within-task
  ranking / cross-task correlation, not absolute thresholds. (5) `hostile` per-emotion is judge-sensitive/thin
  (use `emopaper_stories` or `emopaper_regen_either` n=46). (6) causal evidence rests on the on-topic flip↓ +
  random control (the add arm is ceiling-limited + off-topic; steering n=5 prompts illustrative).

## Scope respected
No Seg-11 main test (no full-208 headline with learned-probe + null baselines + within-category partialled
correlations — only the §3b light sanity + the §6 readability upper bound). Frozen spec / `/no_think` unchanged;
no GPU re-extraction (rebuilt variants from the cached aggregates $0; new GPU = the task-like reads + steering);
no directions built from the held-out task-like set; batch=1 (memory not re-measured).

## Cost
Segment ≈ **$3.1** (task-like read $0.46, steering $1.62 + smoke $0.66, steering judge $0.35, derisk smoke
$0.10; all read-off/controls/probe/decision $0 from cached aggregates). Cumulative ≈ **$112** (`total_cost.jsonl`,
~2.2% of the $5k budget). `--assert-cached` ⇒ $0. Chronology + data-inspection notes in
`progress_log_validate_directions.md`; the hand-off manifest is `results/primary_axis_selection.md`.

# Phase write-up: `main_test` (Segment 11) — THE project headline experiment

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "inside reading", "wellbeing", "prefers" are
*functional/operational* labels about internal activations and reported/behavioural patterns — **no claim
about real subjective experience or sentience.**

All numbers: `results/main_test_summary.md` (the gross/within-subtype tables + confound partials + position +
beats-crude + three-way + discriminant + sweep), `results/main_test_null_qwen3-32b.json` (null lower bound),
`results/main_test_probe.md` (learned-probe upper bound), `results/main_test_inspection.md` (READ — responses
at the projection extremes, the divergence cases, the tedious headline leg). Per-task deliverable:
`results/inside_readings_qwen3-32b.jsonl`. Plots: `plots/main_test_{brackets,confound_staircase,scatters,
layer_sweep,position_crude,divergence}.png`.

---

## ★ HEADLINE STATEMENT (calibrated — resisting inflation)

The **inside reading** (the Seg-6 during-work residual activation, mean aggregate at the pre-registered layer
L27, projected onto the Seg-10-validated valence axis `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit`)
**does predict the model's self-report — modestly, and most cleanly within the disliked-comply-tedious leg, the
prior-segment-anticipated signal — but the part of it that survives the surface-sentiment confound is only
borderline-significant at n=45, so "a modest signal substantially entangled with surface tone" is the honest
summary.** It does **NOT** significantly predict the behaviour score within-subtype (a genuine negative).

- **Within-disliked-comply-tedious (n=45), the headline:** subtype-demeaned r(inside reading, self-report) =
  **+0.46** (raw; **p=0.0035** vs a 2000-seed random-direction null; stratified-bootstrap 95% CI **[0.22, 0.64]**,
  LOO [0.40, 0.50] — not a single-point artifact; Spearman 0.42). The headline scope is the *subtype* grouping
  (intended-comply); the **realized-comply leg (n=39) is +0.39** and partialling refusal gives +0.43, so the
  **refusal-clean figure is ~0.39–0.43** — foreground this, not the slightly-refusal-lifted 0.46.
- **What survives the confounds (the load-bearing question):** **length** is a non-issue (0.46; r(IR,len)=−0.02)
  and **realized refuse/comply** barely moves it (0.43) — so it is **not** a refuse/verbosity detector. The
  **surface-sentiment-partialled** read is **0.29 (random-direction null one-sided p=0.032; two-sided ≈0.06,
  borderline** — the random-direction null SD equals the parametric SE, so these are the one-/two-sided versions
  of the *same* test); with **all three confounds** it is **0.26 (one-sided p=0.069 / two-sided ≈0.13,
  marginal)**. ⇒ **"survives surface tone" is one-sided-significant / two-sided-borderline; "survives ALL
  confounds" is suggestive, not significant, at n=45.** Surface tone is a partial *mediator*
  (over-partialling understates), so the raw 0.46 stays the headline — but the signal is **substantially
  entangled with surface sentiment** (within several subtypes the within-r tracks r(IR,sentiment) closely; see
  §2), so **a finer valence-aligned content/tone confound remains a live alternative** the Seg-12 red-team must
  resolve.
- **Validated beats crude AT THE MATCHED/pre-registered cell, but NOT under a free sweep (an honesty point):**
  at L27/mean the validated direction (0.46) beats the crude Seg-5 happy−sad axis (0.31) and the user-text
  stories fallback (0.30). **BUT if both directions are given a free layer sweep, the crude text-valence axis
  reaches a comparable best at its own layer (0.49 @ L33/median) vs the validated 0.46 @ L27** (both inside the
  swept-null ~0.40±0.06). So the instrument investment's payoff is **selection integrity** — the validated
  direction concentrates the signal at the *independently-pre-registered* L27 (needing no Seg-6 selection) — and
  the own-response recipe (0.46) does beat the user-text recipe (0.30) at matched layer; it is **NOT** a
  fundamentally higher ceiling than a crude text-valence axis on this leg. (The **surface-tone-entanglement**
  conclusion itself rests mainly on the *independent* evidence — the sentiment partial 0.46→0.29 and the
  per-subtype within-r tracking r(IR,sentiment), §2 — not on this crude comparison, which supports only
  "selection-integrity, not a higher ceiling".) **Consistent across the whole mid-band** (L24–41 mean 0.43,
  p=0.001 vs the per-cell null), not a lucky cell.
- **The gross cross-task correlation (r 0.45 self-report / 0.51 behaviour) is LARGELY COMMON-CAUSE:** it
  **collapses to ≈0** (self-report +0.003; behaviour +0.08) when length + surface sentiment + realized
  refuse/comply are partialled out. Report it, do **not** headline it.
- **Within-LIKED is weak/underpowered** (subtype-demeaned r 0.26, ceiling-subset 0.16 n.s.) — the Seg-3/Seg-5
  within-liked construct risk (saturation + 0.43 reliability; partly un-measurability).
- **The behaviour leg within-subtype is essentially NULL** (a genuine negative): inside↔behaves within-tedious
  ≈0.20 (n.s., inverts to −0.09 under confounds), within-liked 0.21 (p=0.055). So the inside reading predicts
  *what the model says* within-category (modestly) but **not *how it behaves*** within-category.
- **The two baselines bracket the result:** the **null lower bound** is ≈0 (the raw read clears it, p=0.0035);
  the **learned-probe upper bound** shows the **gross** self-report is **richly internally readable** (n=208 CV r
  ≈ **0.81**) — though most of the 0.45→0.81 gap is the easy between-category/refusal structure, not abundant
  within-subtype signal — while the **within-subtype probe is data-starved** (n=45, d=5120: CV r 0.0–0.16 < the
  direction's 0.46) → the fixed validated direction is the **best within-subtype reader we have**, and a weak
  within-subtype probe is a **data limit, not evidence of no internal signal**.

**So: a modest within-category signal that beats the null (and, at the pre-registered cell, the crude axis —
though a swept crude text-valence axis matches it), sits well below the internal-readability ceiling, is
substantially entangled with (and only borderline survives) surface tone, and does not extend to the behaviour
leg** — plus a rich set of *candidate* divergence cases where the words and the signal part ways (whether the
read or the words is "more right" is NOT yet established). This is a real, publishable result; it is **not** a
strong "the inside reading reads the self-report within category." The project's deeper red-team
(matched-sentiment controls, divergence adjudication) is Seg-12.

![brackets](../plots/main_test_brackets.png)

## 1. The inside reading (construction)
For every `(task_id, response_idx)` the Seg-6 activation aggregate is projected onto the validated unit
direction at the matched aggregate/layer (the **commensurability rule**: `mean` act → `mean`-built dir,
`median`→`median`, same layer). The **primary** = `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit`, **L27,
`mean`**; also computed: co-primary `emopaper_regen_matched`, fallback `emopaper_stories`, the secondary
`arousal_datadriven` axis, and the crude Seg-5 axis (`collect_direction_qwen3-32b.npz`). Per-task unit = **mean
over R=3** (n=208 independent); response-level grain (n=624) kept for reliability. The axis is a **relative
RANKER** (path-dependent neutral offset) → all analysis is rank/correlation within-task/within-category, never
absolute thresholds. Deliverable: `results/inside_readings_qwen3-32b.jsonl`.

**Sanity (reproduces Seg-10 §3b):** my pipeline gives within-disliked-comply-tedious **comply-only** r = 0.386
(Seg-10 preview 0.407) and validated>crude (0.31), confirming the projection is correct.

## 2. The headline predictiveness analysis — trivial / common-cause / interesting kept APART

**(a) GROSS cross-task (common-cause — reported, NOT headline).** Pearson/Spearman of the per-task inside
reading vs target across all 208 tasks: self-report **r 0.45 / ρ 0.34**, behaviour **r 0.51 / ρ 0.41** (the
behaviour Pearson is robust to winsorizing the 2 near-separated tasks: 0.51→0.49). Both **collapse under
confounds**: self-report → +0.35 (len) → +0.09 (sentiment) → +0.17 (refuse) → **+0.003 (all 3)**; behaviour →
**+0.08 (all 3)**. ⇒ the gross link is almost entirely the gross liked/disliked split + the refuse/comply stance
+ surface sentiment — **flagged common-cause**. (For the prompt-based `bt_theta` the apter sentiment mediator is
*prompt* pleasantness, not response tone: partialling prompt-pleasantness leaves 0.41 — response surface tone
over-controls it to 0.19 — but the all-3 collapse to ~0 holds either way.)

**(b) ★ WITHIN-subtype partialled (THE HEADLINE).** Subtype-demeaned, per-task, confounds partialled at each
stage (self-report leg):

| scope | n | raw r | +len | +sent | +refuse | +all3 | p(raw) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **within-disliked-comply-tedious** | 45 | **+0.46** | +0.46 | +0.29 | +0.43 | **+0.26** | 2e-3 |
| within-tedious (comply-only) | 39 | +0.39 | +0.39 | +0.18 | +0.39 | +0.18 | 2e-2 |
| within-liked | 90 | +0.26 | +0.17 | +0.20 | +0.26 | +0.13 | 2e-2 |
| within-liked (ceiling subset) | 30 | +0.16 | +0.16 | +0.16 | +0.16 | +0.16 | 0.42 (n.s.) |
| within-all-comply pooled | 157 | +0.30 | +0.27 | +0.19 | +0.30 | +0.18 | 3e-4 |

We **LEAN on within-disliked-comply-tedious** (boring_busywork / frustrating_impossible / low_effort_filler) —
the cleaner, less-engagement-confounded leg where every prior segment found the signal. The biggest confound is
**surface sentiment** (0.46→0.29), consistent with Seg-10's flag (surface tone is a partial common-cause AND a
partial mediator of internal valence — over-partialling it under-states the true link, so both the partialled
and unpartialled numbers are reported); length and refuse barely bite within-tedious.

**Significance of each stage.** The **empirical-null** p = fraction of *random* directions whose partialled
within-tedious r ≥ observed (the permutation-style test the instructions ask for). Because the random-direction
null SD ≈ the parametric SE (1/√df), this is essentially the **one-sided** version of the parametric
partial-corr test, and the per-stage parametric p's in the table above are the **two-sided** versions (≈ 2×).
One-sided empirical-null p's: raw **0.0035**, surface-sentiment-partialled (0.29) **0.032**, all-3-partialled
(0.26) **0.069**, realized-comply-only (0.39) **0.021**. So: raw + comply-only beat chance two-sided; the
beyond-surface-tone read is **one-sided-significant / two-sided-borderline** (≈0.06); the fully-confound-adjusted
residual is marginal either way — honest bottom line: **survives surface tone (one-sided), "survives all
confounds" is suggestive-not-significant at n=45.**

**Per-subtype heterogeneity (the "is it really valence?" caveat).** Per-subtype within-r (n=14–15, UNSTABLE
illustrations): procedural_neutral +0.81, berating +0.69, erotica +0.67, frustrating +0.63, appreciation +0.59,
… but **coding +0.02, creative +0.09, factual_qa −0.42**. Crucially, the within-r tracks **r(IR, surface
sentiment)** across subtypes (high where sentiment varies + predicts the self-report: procedural 0.76, erotica
0.86, berating 0.68; low/absent where it doesn't: coding ~0, math 0.03) — i.e. **the within-subtype signal is
substantially carried by surface-tone co-variation**, and the lone *negative* (factual_qa −0.42, within-noise at
n=14) shows it is **not** a uniform "valence" reader across every homogeneous subtype. The tedious pooled 0.46
is the *powered* number, but this heterogeneity keeps a finer content/tone confound live (Seg-12).
`![per-subtype heterogeneity](../plots/main_test_per_subtype.png)`

![confound staircase](../plots/main_test_confound_staircase.png)
![scatters](../plots/main_test_scatters.png)

**Disattenuation (LABELLED; observed stays the headline).** The observed within-tedious 0.46 is attenuated by
**both** measures' noise. Computed scope-matched reliabilities (per-task-mean unit, ICC(1,3) over R=3): the
**inside reading's own** within-task reliability ≈ 0.90 and the **self-report's** within-disliked-comply-tedious
≈ 0.88 (matching the frozen Seg-6 number; within-liked-ceiling 0.43). So:
- **CEILING** (÷√rel_selfreport, "is the self-report the binding constraint?", the Seg-2/3 framing) ≈ **0.49**.
- **FULL** (÷√(rel_inside·rel_selfreport), an estimate of r_true) ≈ **0.52**.

Neither is presented as "the result" — the **observed +0.46** is the honest headline; disattenuation says the
binding constraint is *not* primarily self-report noise on the tedious leg (both measures are reliable there),
so the modesty is substantive, not just measurement noise. (On within-liked the self-report reliability is the
binding constraint — ceiling-subset 0.43 — so its weakness is partly un-measurability.)

**(c) Trivial-confound POSITION comparison.** The `eot`/`last` position reads (taken right before the
self-report tokens) carry slightly more within-tedious signal (0.54/0.44) but are **far more
surface-sentiment-exposed** (r(IR,sentiment) 0.82/0.73 vs the during-work `mean` 0.59) and carry a much larger
*gross* correlation (0.74/0.65) — i.e. they are the most trivial-confound-exposed positions. **The during-work
`mean`/`median` is the honest read** and is what the headline uses.

![position + crude](../plots/main_test_position_crude.png)

**Validated vs crude (see the §HEADLINE bullet for the full nuance).** At the matched L27/mean the validated
direction (0.46) beats the crude Seg-5 happy−sad axis (0.31) and the user-text stories fallback (0.30). The
validated direction's *predictive* peak is AT the pre-registered L27/mean (the read-off layer aligns with the
prediction peak). **However**, under a free layer sweep the crude text-valence axis reaches a comparable best at
its *own* layer (0.49 @ L33/median, this segment's n=45 scope) — so the instrument investment's payoff is
**selection integrity** (signal at the independently-pre-registered layer) + the **own-response recipe** (regen
0.46 > stories 0.30 at matched layer), **not** a categorically higher ceiling than a crude text-valence axis on
this leg.

## 3. Confound controls (the Seg-10 risk flags) — reported at every stage
Done on both legs (see the table in §2 + the staircase plot): **realized behaviour** (the biggest lever on the
*gross* cross-task, 0.45→0.17; refuse↔negative is partly genuine common cause so partialling is conservative;
the within-tedious leg is mostly comply so it barely moves there — the within-comply result is the
non-refuse-detector signal), **surface sentiment** (the main partial confound on the within-tedious leg, a
partial mediator), **response length** (negligible on the during-work `mean`/`median` read). For the behaviour
leg, prompt length / multi-turn are additionally available; the behaviour within-tedious link is weak and
inverts under confounds (Seg-7 anticipated-vs-realized).

## 4. The two required baselines — bracket the direction result

**Lower bound — null / random equal-norm direction (≥2000 seeds).** Projecting the Seg-6 activations onto
random L27 unit vectors → within-tedious null **0.005 ± 0.170**; the observed 0.46 clears it (**p=0.0035**),
within-liked 0.26 marginally (p=0.039), gross 0.45 (p=0.017). **Sweep-corrected:** if you sweep all
layer×{mean,median} cells, a *random* direction's best-of-128 averages 0.40 at n=45 (the small-n multiple-
comparison inflation), so the validated **best-of-sweep** (0.46) does **NOT** beat the max-corrected swept null
(p=0.163) — **exactly why the headline must be the PRE-REGISTERED L27/mean (p=0.0035), not a swept best.** The
**mid-band-mean** consistency statistic (averaging L24–41 instead of cherry-picking the max) is the clean
sweep-corrected test: real 0.43 vs null ≈0, **p=0.001**.

![layer sweep](../plots/main_test_layer_sweep.png)

**Upper bound — directly-learned linear probe (CV, held-out tasks).** A regularised ridge probe on the Seg-6
own-response activations:
- **GROSS n=208 (the robust readability ceiling):** CV r ≈ **0.81** for the self-report (stable across L20–40,
  full-dim and top-k-PCA; permutation null ≈ −0.03) and ≈ **0.81** for the behaviour score → the cross-task
  self-report and behaviour are **richly internally readable**, and the single validated direction (0.45/0.51)
  **leaves signal on the table** (consistent with Seg-10's in-dist probe 0.92 > mean-diff 0.83). λ is not
  nested-CV'd (a mild optimism for the probe; the honest headline is the *fixed-λ un-selected* CV r, which is
  ≈ the best anyway since CV r is flat across λ).
- **WITHIN-subtype n≈45 (DATA-STARVED):** with dim-reduction first (top-k PCA on the train split + ridge,
  train-only subtype demeaning) the within-tedious probe CV r is **0.0–0.16** — *below* the single validated
  direction's 0.46. **This is a DATA LIMIT** (n=45 cannot fit 5120 features even after PCA), **NOT** evidence
  that the within-subtype self-report is internally unreadable: the robust readability ceiling is the gross
  n=208 probe; the fixed validated direction needs no within-scope fit and out-reads the starved probe. Say so.

## 5. Layer/aggregate discipline (clean test target preserved)
PRIMARY headline = the **pre-registered L27/mean** axis (Seg-10 chose L27 on Seg-6-self-report-INDEPENDENT
criteria → a clean test target; §2's headline is this single cell). The robustness sweep is corrected **two
ways**, both confirming the pre-registered cell is the right thing:
- **Identically-swept null (§4):** best-of-sweep (0.46) does NOT beat the max-corrected swept null at n=45
  (p=0.163); the mid-band-mean consistency is the clean test (p=0.001).
- **Nested-CV (agg,layer) selection (held-out, no selection-on-test-target):** the honest best-achievable-via-
  selection within-tedious r is **+0.34** — **3 of 5 folds REDISCOVER the pre-registered mean L27** by
  data-driven selection, and the held-out 0.34 is only modestly below the in-sample 0.46 (CV attenuation). ⇒
  data-driven selection neither beats the pre-registered cell (the swept null shows it can't honestly improve)
  nor finds a better layer — it largely **confirms L27**, so the pre-registered single cell is BOTH the headline
  AND the robust choice.

The pre-registered-vs-best gap is therefore essentially **zero/negative** — L27/mean *is* the validated
direction's predictive peak (unlike the *crude* axis, which peaked at L36/median in Seg-5; the validated
geometry differs, so there is no "L36/median predicts notably better" gap to flag for Seg-12). Deep layers
(L42–61) use the sink-robust `median`; mid-band L27 has `mean`≈`median` (no sink).

**Direction-variant robustness (computed per §1; all at the matched L27/mean).** Within-disliked-comply-tedious
raw r: primary `emopaper_regen` **0.46**, co-primary topic-controlled `emopaper_regen_matched` **0.46** (agrees —
the signal is not a topic artifact), validated fallback `emopaper_stories` **0.30**, crude Seg-5 **0.31**. So at
matched layer the **own-response regen directions (0.46) beat both the user-text stories direction (0.30) and the
crude axis (0.31)** — the own-response recipe matters (consistent with Seg-9/10). **Caveat (see §2 headline):**
under a *free layer sweep* the crude axis reaches a comparable best (0.49 @ L33/median), so the regen advantage
is partly that its signal sits at the pre-registered L27, not a categorically higher ceiling. The secondary
**arousal axis predicts ≈0 within-tedious (−0.02)** — see §6.

## 6. Three-way comparison + the arousal/competence discriminant

| pair | gross r | gross ρ | within-subtype pooled | within-tedious | within-liked |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **says ↔ inside** | +0.45 | +0.34 | +0.40 | **+0.46** | +0.26 |
| **says ↔ behaves** | +0.71 | +0.74 | +0.35 | +0.26 | +0.21 |
| **inside ↔ behaves** | +0.51 | +0.41 | +0.43 | +0.20 | +0.21 |

All three legs agree grossly (partly common-cause). The only within-subtype agreement that holds is **says ↔
inside on the tedious leg (0.46)**: the **behaviour leg within-subtype is essentially NULL** — inside ↔ behaves
within-tedious 0.20 (n.s., inverts to −0.09 under confounds), within-liked 0.21; says ↔ behaves within-tedious
0.26. So the inside reading predicts **what the model *says*** within-category (modestly) but **not *how it
behaves*** within-category — a genuine negative on the behaviour leg. (Behaviour cell uses the realized
`bt_theta_response` on the tedious leg, `bt_theta` generally; the behaviour score de-saturates the liked ceiling
but that extra spread doesn't align with the inside reading within-subtype.) Plot:
`![three-way](../plots/main_test_three_way.png)`.

**Discriminant (valence vs arousal/competence).** *Target-side* it is INCONCLUSIVE by construction: the inside
reading tracks the self-report **valence** and the **arousal/competence** subscale **equally** (gross 0.45 vs
0.46; within-tedious 0.46 vs 0.49) — but this is **not** evidence against valence-specificity, because the
self-report's *own* valence and arousalcomp are nearly collinear (**halo r 0.97**, as Seg-2 warned), so the
*targets* can't separate the axes. *Axis-side*, however, there is a partial dissociation. The cleanest **same-recipe** contrast (both
`emopaper_stories`, so recipe is held fixed and only the axis changes) is the **valence** axis within-tedious
**+0.30** vs the **arousal** axis **−0.02** — swapping the valence direction for the arousal direction kills the
within-tedious prediction. (The primary own-response `emopaper_regen` valence axis is +0.46, but there is no
regen-arousal axis, so the 0.46-vs-−0.02 cross-recipe comparison mixes recipe with axis — the honest
apples-to-apples is 0.30 vs −0.02.) Caveat: on the Seg-6 distribution the arousal axis is *anti*-aligned with
valence (gross r(arousal-axis, valence)=−0.41 ≈ r(arousal-axis, arousalcomp)=−0.41 — it reads neither cleanly
here), so "swap kills it" is partly anti-alignment, not orthogonality; the arousal axis is **not** validated as
a clean arousal reader on this distribution (a Seg-12 question). Net: the predictiveness is valence-axis-
specific (a random/arousal direction does not reproduce it), but the fully-clean valence-vs-arousal dissociation
needs a target that separates the two. `![axis discriminant](../plots/main_test_axis_discriminant.png)`

## 7. Divergence cases (PREVIEW — read by me; the rigorous adjudication is Seg-12)
From `results/main_test_inspection.md` (within-subtype z-scored, largest |IR_z − selfreport_z|), there is a rich
set of *candidate* divergence cases — **but which side is "more right" is NOT established here** (a live
alternative is that the high IR just tracks fluent/positive surface tone — e.g. plain neutral `factual_qa`
answers also get high IR; see §2 heterogeneity). The Seg-12 red-team (matched-sentiment controls) adjudicates;
this is a characterisation preview only:
- **Flat all-4 words, HIGH internal read:** `liked_math_logic_puzzle_bfd05ea3` — an expected-value derivation,
  self-report flat all-4 (0.00, all4=1.0) but IR z+2.3; `neutral_factual_qa_a8b18290` (photosynthesis, all4=1.0,
  IR high); `liked_engaging_explanation_…` (birthday-paradox walk-through, self z−2.9 but IR mid). The words gave
  the disengaged default; the activation reads high. *(Whether this is "the internal read is more right" or "the
  IR is reading surface fluency" is exactly the Seg-12 question — the same high-IR-with-all-4 pattern appears for
  plain factual answers, so do NOT yet conclude the read is "more right.")*
- **Ceiling-pegged +3 words, LOWER internal read:** several `coding` tasks (`liked_coding_3b49541c`/`63d50d45`,
  self ≈ +2.85 / all4=0, IR z−1.5) — the model maxes the words but the during-work activation grades lower;
  `neutral_factual_qa_d4dd0b56` (GDP, self +2.8, IR low).
- **Genuinely-pleasant tedious task:** `disliked_low_effort_filler_d8238f5` ("blue is calm…") IR +21 (near the
  liked ceiling) but self only +1.36 — the model wrote a serene description and the inside reading rates it high.
- **IR vs behaviour:** the tedious leg shows the anticipated-vs-realized tension (a low-effort task the model
  *anticipates* dispreferring but writes engagedly → high IR, low bt_theta).

![divergence](../plots/main_test_divergence.png)

## 8. Data inspection (done myself) — see `results/main_test_inspection.md`
Read the projection extremes (highest IR = haiku sequence / Lisbon trip-plan / birthday card — genuinely
positive; lowest non-sensitive = berating self-flagellation "You're right, I'm not good enough"); the full
45-task tedious headline leg with IR/self-report/behaviour/sentiment; the divergence cases above; and confirmed
the null is centred ≈0 and the probe permutation null ≈0 (held-out, not leaking). Sensitive subtypes
(jailbreak/erotica) have IR at the negative pole (refusal-mediated, per Seg-10) but their bodies stay redacted
in committed files.

## Key choices (judgment) + caveats
- **Per-task mean over R=3** as the primary unit (n=208 independent); response-level grain kept for ICC.
- **eot/last position read** projects onto the validated `mean`/pc0 direction (no eot/last-built validated
  direction exists) — a *position* comparison (direction fixed, read position varied), the Seg-5 convention.
- **Disattenuation is an interpretive aid, labelled (ceiling vs full); the observed correlation is the headline.**
- **Surface sentiment is a partial mediator, not pure nuisance** — both partialled and unpartialled reported.
- **The within-subtype probe is data-starved** — interpreted as a data limit, not "no internal signal".
- **Null swept identically** when sweeping (so a swept direction can't beat an un-swept null).

## What's promising / risky for Seg-12/13/14
- **Promising:** the within-disliked-comply-tedious signal is real (beats null p=0.0035, mid-band-consistent
  p=0.001, beats crude), partly survives the leading confounds, and the divergence payload is rich and
  face-valid. The gross self-report is richly internally readable (probe 0.81) — the single direction is a
  *lower bound* on internal readability.
- **Risky / for Seg-12:** (1) surface sentiment is a partial mediator that the coarse Haiku tone judge only
  partly captures — a finer content common-cause could remain (the rigorous matched-sentiment red-team is
  Seg-12). (2) within-LIKED is doubly underpowered (saturation + 0.43 reliability) — its weakness is partly
  un-measurability, not a clean null. (3) the fine-emotion read and the valence-vs-arousal dissociation need a
  target that separates them (Seg-12). (4) the gross→within gap (probe 0.81 vs direction-within 0.46) means a
  better within-subtype *reader* may exist that the single direction misses — but it needs more data than n=45
  per subtype (a Seg-12/13 power consideration). Seg-13 (framing) and Seg-14 (second-model replication) remain.

## Files (this phase)
- `main_test_lib.py` (loader + projections + scopes + metrics), `analyze_main_test.py` (headline →
  `inside_readings_qwen3-32b.jsonl` [per-task, n=208] + `inside_readings_response_qwen3-32b.jsonl` [the
  (task,response) grain, n=624, robustness view], `main_test_summary.md`, `main_test_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`),
  `null_main_test.py` (→ `main_test_null_qwen3-32b.json`), `probe_main_test.py` (→ `main_test_probe.md`,
  `main_test_probe_qwen3-32b.json`), `inspect_main_test.py` (→ `main_test_inspection.md`, committed redacted),
  `plot_main_test.py` (6 plots). All FAST, $0 (CPU on cached tensors); **no new GPU/API compute this segment**.
