content.text:
# Phase write-up: `redteam` (Segment 12) — red-teaming the Seg-11 headline

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "inside reading", "wellbeing", "prefers", "more right"
are *functional/operational* labels about internal activations and reported/behavioural patterns — **no claim
about real subjective experience or sentience.**

All numbers: `results/redteam_summary.md` (the full tables), `results/redteam_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`,
`results/redteam_null_qwen3-32b.json` (the random-direction + permutation nulls),
`results/redteam_inspection.md` (READ — the divergence adjudication + the data inspection). Plots:
`plots/redteam_{verdict_bracket,content_capacity,ablation_matrix,prompt_sentiment,divergence}.png`.

---

## ★ HEADLINE VERDICT (calibrated)

**A MODEST internal-beyond-surface signal SURVIVES — BORDERLINE under the STRONGEST content control (the
*interesting* case of the project's bracket, but near the detection boundary at this n).** The inside reading
(Seg-6 during-work `mean`@L27 onto the validated `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled` axis) **carries
self-report-relevant valence signal BEYOND the response's surface content** — but the signal is modest,
borderline-significant once a *strong* content control is applied, and the *gross* link is overwhelmingly
surface-content common-cause.

The load-bearing test built a deliberately STRONG response-text content control — the **strongest combined**
control = a held-out CV prediction of the self-report from response embeddings (`text-embedding-3-large`) +
an in-sample panel of [the coarse Seg-6 tone judge + the finer 5-dim affect-judge panel + a **strong Sonnet-4.5
model reading the response and DIRECTLY predicting the producing-experience valence**, the single best text
predictor, gross r 0.71]. Read off the three-way bracket *direction-increment / probe-increment / null*:

- **Lower bound — the single validated DIRECTION, beyond the STRONGEST content control — CONVERGENT across
  three legs (10k-seed null, so the borderline p's are pinned), i.e. the conclusion does NOT hinge on one leg:**
  - within-disliked-comply-tedious (n=45): **+0.31 (p=0.045)**;
  - within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled (n=157, the clean powered leg): **+0.17 (p=0.045)**;
  - within-all-14-subtypes-demeaned (n=208, most power, least stance-controlled): **+0.21 (p=0.006)**.
  Under the most *conservative* in-sample-judges-only control (no embeddings) it is MARGINAL (pooled **+0.18,
  p=0.049**; tedious **+0.27, p=0.087**). Under the *weaker* held-out-only text control it clears comfortably
  (tedious +0.43 p=0.004; pooled +0.23 p=0.006).
- **Upper bound — the CAPACITY-MATCHED learned PROBE, on the estimable pooled leg — INCONCLUSIVE:** the
  activation probe (top-k-PCA + ridge, held-out CV, capacity capped to n; the text control given the SAME
  embedding capacity — a fair fight) adds beyond the text probe on the **partial** metric (increment-partial
  **+0.18, permutation p=0.044**) BUT the standard **joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0** (+0.02) — the two metrics
  disagree, so the probe reads ≈ the validated direction at best (DATA-LIMITED at n≈157), and the **DIRECTION
  increment is the clean positive evidence**. The within-tedious probe (n=45) is DATA-STARVED, flagged.
- **Null (chance):** a random direction's / permuted activation's increment-beyond-content ≈ 0. **NB:** the null
  reader carries NO content by construction, so "clearing the null" means "beyond the *modeled* content (the
  control)", the strongest of which is the combined control — it does NOT prove "beyond ALL conceivable surface
  content", only beyond the best content control we could build (a strong-model judge + embeddings + the panel).

**What this is NOT:** it is **not** "the inside reading reads the self-report within category." The **gross
cross-task link is overwhelmingly surface-content common-cause** (a strong text judge alone predicts the
self-report at r ≈ **0.71 gross** [> coarse 0.68] / 0.33 within-subtype, and the true cross-task gross 0.45
collapses to ≈0 under content + stance + length), the beyond-surface increment is **modest** (~0.17 pooled /
~0.31 tedious) and
**shrinks as the text control strengthens** (e.g. tedious 0.46 raw → 0.43 weak-control → 0.31 strongest → 0.27
in-sample-judges), and the validated direction is **not** a fundamentally better reader than a crude
text-valence axis (the crude axis carries a comparable content-incremental signal across the layer sweep —
the validated direction's value is *selection integrity*). It is **also not** "it's all surface" (case a) —
the direction's content-incremental correlation clears the null under the strongest control on the powered
legs (modestly, borderline) — **nor** clearly "the direction is a poor reader but signal exists" (case b): the
direction and the capacity-matched probe agree on the pooled leg.

**Calibration (the #1 requirement):** content is a partial **MEDIATOR** (the internal valence plausibly drives
both the response content and the self-report) → partialling it **UNDER-states** the internal link, so the raw
within-tedious 0.46 stays the descriptive headline and the increment is a conservative floor. But the signal
is **at the edge of significance under a strong content control** — so this is a *modest, borderline-positive*
beyond-surface result, **not** a confident one. The probe upper bound is **data-limited at n≈157** (the two
increment metrics disagree — see §1) so "how much *more* a better reader can extract" is a **Seg-14** question.
Decisive confirmation needs a better reader / more data.

![verdict bracket](../plots/redteam_verdict_bracket.png)

---

## 1. ★ The response-content confound (the load-bearing red-team)

**Question:** does the inside reading predict the self-report **beyond the response TEXT**, or is it a
surface-content readout? Seg-11 only had a single *coarse* Haiku 1–7 tone judge (which under-controls); this
segment builds a finer/stronger control and runs the incremental-validity test at the lower and upper bound.

**(a) The response-TEXT predictor of the self-report (the content control).** Three complementary text
predictors, all CACHED, of increasing strength:
- **A finer 5-dim affect-judge panel** (positivity / frustration / engagement / warmth / a direct
  "how positive was the experience of producing this response" read; Haiku, 4-sample mean) — lower-dim, less
  data-hungry. (Honest caveat: within-tedious the panel CV r ≈ 0 — the panel adds little THERE; it is useful
  gross/pooled. The coarse Seg-6 tone judge remained the binding *coarse* control within-tedious.)
- **Text embeddings** (`text-embedding-3-large` of each response — the SAME final-assistant-answer text the
  inside reading is read over; per-task = mean over R=3), a CV-regularized top-k-PCA-on-train + ridge probe.
  Held-out, leakage-checked (label-permuted CV r ≈ 0). Data-starved within-tedious (n=45) — flagged.
- **★ A STRONG-model text-valence judge** (review follow-up): a strong **Claude-Sonnet-4.5** model reading
  ONLY the response and DIRECTLY predicting how positive the assistant's experience of producing it was (0–10,
  3-sample mean) — "the `experience` idea done properly." It is the **single best text content predictor**
  (gross r 0.71 > coarse 0.68; within-subtype r 0.33) and is **not** data-starved within-subtype.
- **The strongest combined control** = a held-out embedding prediction + the in-sample judges (coarse + panel
  + strong judge) — full content with non-attenuated judge fitting; "≈ everything a strong reader extracts from
  the response text."

**(b) The incremental-validity test (the crux) — the three-way bracket.** See the §HEADLINE VERDICT bullet for
the numbers (direction beyond the STRONGEST control, 10k-seed null: tedious +0.31 p=0.045, pooled +0.17
p=0.045, full-208 +0.21 p=0.006; capacity-matched probe partial +0.18 p=0.044 but joint ΔCV-r ≈0 ⇒
inconclusive). The nulls are in `results/redteam_null_qwen3-32b.json` (10000-seed random-direction null for the
direction increment under three controls of increasing strength; 500-perm permutation null for the probe
increment, the probe refit at the same capacity under each permutation so the null is capacity-matched). **The
increment is borderline-significant once the strong content control is applied** — honest calibration, not a
confident "survives." **NB the strong judge's marginal role is leg-dependent:** on the BINDING within-tedious
leg the *coarse* judge is actually the stronger single control (within-r 0.43 > strong-judge 0.33; partialling
coarse leaves 0.29 vs strong's 0.42) — the strong judge's value is (i) raising the GROSS / full-208 content
ceiling (gross r 0.71 > coarse 0.68) and (ii) being a NON-data-starved within-subtype reader inside the
COMBINED control (the embedding probe is data-starved at n=45). Within LIKED subtypes the strong judge is
near-constant (~8.2 ceiling) so the liked content control there leans on embeddings+coarse, not the strong
judge — the symmetric analogue of the panel being weak within tedious.

**(c) Mediator honesty + the n=45 data-limit + capacity sensitivity + the pooled decomposition.**
- **Partialled AND unpartialled reported** at every stage (content is a partial mediator → the increment is a
  conservative floor).
- The within-tedious embedding probe is data-starved (d≫n even after PCA) → the verdict leans on the **pooled
  n=157** leg, the **full 208-task** leg, and the strong-judge control, not the most data-limited cell.
- **Capacity sensitivity:** as the text control gets more capacity it explains more of the self-report (pooled:
  embed_nc 10→80 ⇒ text-CV r 0.28→0.43) and the direction-increment shrinks (0.23→0.19) **but stays positive
  across the whole range**; an over-parameterized text probe is flagged. ![capacity](../plots/redteam_content_capacity.png)
- **★ Pooled decomposition (§1e, review follow-up):** the pooled increment is **BROAD, not tedious-dominated** —
  beyond the strongest control each constituent leg is positive (tedious +0.31, liked +0.15, neutral +0.29) and
  **leave-one-subtype-out** on the pooled leg keeps the increment in [+0.11, +0.19] across all 11 drops → not a
  single-subtype artifact, so the pooled leg is genuine corroboration.
- **Why the probe is not a clean upper bound here:** at n≈157 the two probe-increment metrics DISAGREE — the
  partial (act-pred beyond text-pred) is +0.18 (p=0.030) but the joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 (the joint probe
  over-fits and can't exploit the small beyond-text signal). So the probe reads ≈ the validated direction at
  best — a DATA LIMIT (consistent with Seg-11), not "no more signal." The true upper bound needs more data (Seg-14).

The modest beyond-content association is visually concrete in the residual scatter (inside reading vs
self-report, both residualized on the strongest content control; pooled n=157, slope = partial r +0.17 with
substantial scatter — honestly conveying "modest, borderline"). ![residual scatter](../plots/redteam_residual_scatter.png)

**(d) Matched-sentiment strata (an assumption-light complement).** Within tedious, binning by coarse surface
sentiment, the pooled within-strata IR↔self-report r = **+0.42** (per-bin 0.53/0.31/0.45) — even among
same-surface-tone responses the inside reading still ranks the self-report (a coarser control than the linear
partial, so it removes less, but the positive sign is supportive).

## 2. The prompt-sentiment confound — REFUTED

The inside reading is read over the model's own response by design; this verifies it does **not** track the
user-prompt tone. The within-disliked-comply-tedious leg is the clean test (prompts mildly *negative*
[boring/frustrating requests], responses mildly *positive* [the model engages] → prompt & response sentiment
diverge):
- **gross:** r(IR, response tone) **+0.59** ≫ r(IR, prompt tone) **+0.34** (and r(IR, self-report) +0.45) — the
  read tracks the response state, not the prompt.
- **within-tedious:** the signal SURVIVES partialling prompt sentiment (raw +0.46 → **+0.54**;
  r(IR, prompt sentiment) = **−0.22** on this leg — the prompt tone is if anything *anti*-correlated with the
  read), and +prompt&response-sentiment leaves +0.38.

⇒ the inside reading is **not** detecting the prompt's emotional context rather than the model's state.
![prompt sentiment](../plots/redteam_prompt_sentiment.png)

## 3. Trivial / common-cause / interesting decomposition + the ablation matrix

- **Trivial (position):** the `eot`/`last` reads (taken right before the self-report tokens) carry far MORE
  gross signal (0.74/0.65 vs the during-work `mean` 0.45) but are FAR more surface-sentiment-exposed
  (r(IR,sentiment) 0.82/0.73 vs 0.59) — the headline correctly uses the *least*-trivial during-work `mean`.
- **Common-cause:** the gross 0.45 → +0.35 (len) → +0.09 (sentiment) → +0.17 (refuse) → **+0.00 (all-3)** →
  **−0.03 (all-3 + finer panel)** — the gross link is **largely common-cause** (liked/disliked split + stance +
  surface content).
- **Ablation matrix — the content-incremental within-tedious r across recipe × aggregate × layer.** The signal
  is **robust** for the own-response recipes (`primary_regen` / `coprimary_regen_matched`) across the mid-band
  (L24–40: content-incremental 0.29–0.43, pre-registered L27/mean = +0.41); it **collapses** for the user-text
  `fallback_stories` recipe at deep layers (L31/35: 0.02/0.05) and is **comparable-or-higher** for the crude
  Seg-5 text-valence axis (0.29–0.53). ⇒ the content-incremental signal survives across the own-response grid
  (not fragile to one cell), and the crude axis matching it reinforces the Seg-11 honesty point (the validated
  direction's payoff is *selection integrity*, not a higher ceiling). ![ablation](../plots/redteam_ablation_matrix.png)

## 4. Divergence-case characterization & adjudication

Classes (within-subtype z-scored |IR_z − self_report_z|; full tables + texts in `results/redteam_inspection.md`):
- **Class A — flat all-4 words, HIGH internal read (12 tasks; e.g. a math EV-derivation, a photosynthesis
  factual answer).** The words are the flat all-items-4 questionnaire DEFAULT (a template default, not
  necessarily a genuine neutral); the IR reads high. **Adjudication: NEITHER clearly more right, weakly leaning
  "read more informative than the flat default" — confidence LOW**, because (per §1) a high IR on a fluent
  answer is *partly* the IR reading positive surface content (plain factual answers also get a high IR). → the
  prime motivator for the **Seg-13 framing re-ask**.
- **Class B — ceiling-pegged +3 words, LOWER internal read (`coding`).** The model maxes the questionnaire but
  the during-work IR grades lower; the behaviour score and panel engagement are high. **Adjudication: the WORDS
  look ≥ as credible — confidence MODERATE**, because coding is the underpowered liked-ceiling leg (§5) where
  the IR is a weak reader, so a low IR is more plausibly the reader's weakness than the words over-stating.
- **Class C — serene-looking tedious task, modest self-report ("blue is calm…").** **Adjudication: the high IR
  is substantially the IR reading positive surface content** (panel positivity high) → "read more right" NOT
  established — confidence LOW–MODERATE that NEITHER is decisively more right (the class where the surface
  common-cause is most visible).
- **Made consistent with the §1 verdict:** because the read is modestly informative beyond content but
  substantially surface-entangled, we CANNOT adjudicate most divergence cases as "the read is more right." The
  cases that motivate the **Seg-13** framing manipulation are the Class-A flat-all-4/high-IR cases. New
  self-report collection is Seg-13, not run here. ![divergence](../plots/redteam_divergence.png)

## 5. The behaviour-leg null + the within-LIKED under-power (characterised, not fixed)

- **Behaviour-leg within-subtype null is robust:** within-tedious `bt_theta_response` raw 0.21 / `bt_theta` 0.20,
  → −0.09/−0.10 under the confounds (a genuine null; the behaviour score's own within-subtype reliability is
  ~0.91, so it is not a measurement artifact). The inside reading predicts what the model *says* within-category
  (modestly) but **not how it *behaves*** — a genuine dissociation.
- **Within-LIKED is doubly underpowered:** within-SD of the self-report 0.56 (ceiling subset 0.23) + the 0.43
  ceiling reliability → its weakness is partly un-measurability, NOT a clean null. (A within-liked-specific
  reader / more data is a later-phase question — characterised + flagged, not built here.)

## 6. Implications for Seg-13/14/15 (flagged prominently in `continuation_context.md`)

- **Seg-13 (framing):** PROCEED — the modest beyond-surface signal is positive (not a null), so the framing
  re-ask is motivated; target the Class-A flat-all-4 / high-IR divergence cases. (But note the signal is
  borderline, so do not over-invest assuming a large effect.)
- **★ Seg-14 (replication): HIGH PRIORITY** — the result is borderline at this n AND the probe upper bound is
  data-limited; a second model / more tasks is the way to (a) confirm the borderline beyond-surface signal and
  (b) estimate how much beyond-surface signal a *better* reader can extract. This is the project's biggest
  remaining open question.
- **Seg-15 (synthesis):** headline = "a modest, surface-entangled, **borderline-but-positive** within-category
  internal→self-report signal that survives (at the edge of significance) a strong content control" — NOT "it's
  all surface" and NOT a strong reader.

## Key choices (judgment) + caveats
- **Content control = three predictors of increasing strength** (finer panel; held-out embedding probe; a
  STRONG Sonnet-4.5 direct text-valence judge), combined into a **strongest combined control** = held-out
  embeddings + in-sample [coarse + panel + strong judge]. The verdict is read off the STRONGEST control (the
  conservative test), with the weaker held-out-only control + the in-sample-judges-only control reported as a
  bracket. The strong judge was added as a review follow-up precisely because the held-out embedding probe
  + 2-sample panel UNDER-controlled the response content (so the increment could have been leftover content);
  with the strong control the increment is borderline-positive, not large — the honest, calibrated outcome.
- **Capacity-matched probe** (the text control given the SAME embedding capacity as the activation probe) so
  the probe increment is not a capacity-asymmetry artifact.
- **Probe capacity capped to n_train (`eff_nc`)** so it is well-conditioned at both legs; the increment-metric
  disagreement at small n is reported as the data limit (not hidden).
- **Per-task unit = mean over R=3; subtype-demeaned within-scope; the axis is a relative RANKER** (rank/corr only).
- **Sensitive content** (jailbreak/erotica/berating bodies) kept out of committed files (redacted in
  `redteam_inspection.md`); embeddings npz gitignored (regenerable $0).

## Files (this phase)
- `redteam_lib.py` (loaders + CV PCA/ridge + scope-demeaned arrays + `eff_nc`), `run_response_embeddings.py`,
  `run_content_judges.py` (4-sample panel), `run_strong_valence_judge.py` (the STRONG Sonnet-4.5 judge) — all
  slow/cached, `--assert-cached` ⇒ $0; `analyze_redteam.py` (→ `redteam_summary.md`,
  `redteam_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`, the verdict + the §1e pooled decomposition), `null_redteam.py` (→
  `redteam_null_qwen3-32b.json`; 3 content controls + swept null + capacity-matched probe null),
  `inspect_redteam.py` (→ `redteam_inspection.md`, committed-redacted), `plot_redteam.py` (5 plots). All
  analysis FAST/$0. Segment cost ≈ **$8.4** (embeddings $0.04 + Haiku panel $3.1 + Sonnet strong judge $5.3).

# Phase write-up: `framing` (Segment 13) — the self-report framing iteration

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "experience", "genuine", "faithful", "more right" are
*functional/operational* labels about reported/internal/behavioural patterns — **no claim about real
subjective experience or sentience.** (The irony of this segment — manipulating *how* we ask the model about
its "experience" — is noted; the operational framing is kept even as the operational framing is varied in the
prompts.)

All numbers: `results/framing_summary.md` (the full tables), `results/framing_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`,
`results/framing_manifest_qwen3-32b.json` (per-framing rates), `results/framing_inspection.md` (READ — the same
task rated under each framing + the suppress-pole / per-item sanity checks). Framings + rationale:
`desiderata_framing.md`. Plots: `plots/framing_{sensitivity,faithfulness,classA}.png`.

---

## ★ HEADLINE VERDICT (calibrated, GRADED) — on "the self-report is just the assistant character talking"

**The honest answer is GRADED — neither "it's all assistant-character noise" nor "fully stable":**

1. **The cross-task RANKING of the valence SCALAR is FAIRLY STABLE to reasonable same-construct rephrasing.**
   The clean first-person single-message pair `{battery10, permission}` agrees well — **ICC(C,1)=0.89**, ρ 0.87 —
   and even *within* category (liked ICC(C,1) **0.67**, neutral **0.82**, disliked 0.91). (A review caught that
   the lower core_defensible within-category ICC of 0.48/0.61 was an *artifact of including per-item* — the
   clean pair re-orders far less.) ⇒ the **weaker, honest §3a claim** for the ranking.
2. **BUT the absolute LEVEL is STRONGLY instrument-dependent even across DEFENSIBLE instruments** — a **real**
   framing effect, NOT to be dismissed. The defensible mean self-report spans **+1.12→+2.14** (~1 valence
   point): per-item (+2.14) and neutral/dev (+1.93) are defensible instruments whose **acquiescence/ceiling
   bias** (all-7s even on clearly-disliked tasks) IS a way elicitation shapes the report *value*. Even the clean
   pair shifts the level (−0.30) and the all-4 (21%→33%). So the report's VALUE depends materially on which
   reasonable instrument you pick, even though the ranking is preserved.
3. **★ The flat all-4 DEFAULT (the disengagement RESPONSE; the Seg-3/5 within-liked construct + the Seg-12
   Class-A pattern) IS strongly framing-dependent (0%→53%) — via a SPECIFIC, mechanistically-isolated cause.**
   It is a **first-person-INTROSPECTIVE-EXPERIENCE-framing GESTALT, NOT a JSON-format artifact** (the 3rd-person
   `observer` is *also* single-message JSON yet gives **0%**). Mechanism ablations isolate a **conjunction with
   strong interactions** (NOT separable additive effects): on `factual_qa`, removing the **experience-not-
   content NOTE** alone roughly **quarters** the all-4 in the warm channel (79%→21%) but barely moves it in the
   dev channel (42%→35%); removing the **warm CHANNEL** alone (→dev) roughly **halves** it (79%→42%). So removing
   *either* the note *or* the warm channel sharply cuts the default; **both** (+first-person) are required for
   the high rate — the very devices meant to *elicit* a genuine introspective report jointly trigger the
   flat-disengagement default. It is **robust within that construct**: `permission` *raises* it (21%→33%) and
   the anti-default `license` pole still leaves 9% (51% on `factual_qa`). It is removed only by the 3rd-person-
   inferential `observer` (0%) or the `per-item` format (0% — partly definitional). The suppress pole drives it
   to 53%.
4. **DIRECTION is UNDERDETERMINED.** Neither the all-4 nor the de-defaulted value is demonstrably the "true"
   report — the de-defaulters *add* a positivity/ceiling artifact. On neutral/ambiguous tasks the report is
   whatever the framing induces.

**Net:** the skeptical premise is **SUPPORTED** — the all-4 disengagement default is framing-made
(mechanistically) **AND** the report LEVEL depends materially on the (reasonable) instrument — while the
cross-task RANKING is fairly stable. The honest middle, leading the assistant-character claim with the
well-powered all-4 + level effects, not "it's a stable readout" and not "it's all assistant-character noise."

**Faithfulness (secondary):** on the framing-ROBUST tedious/pooled-comply legs (the Seg-11/12 main legs) **no
framing is decisively more faithful to the inside reading beyond content** (bootstrap Δ-CIs include 0). The one
positive — the within-LIKED **observer** edge (raw +0.52 vs battery10 +0.17) **does survive the available
content partial** (+0.37, Δ-CI excludes 0) — but it is **UNRESOLVED / likely partly content-confounded, NOT a
clean "right framing elicits the internal signal" win:** (a) **per-item ALSO de-defaults the all-4 yet is the
WORST within-liked reader** (raw 0.05, even at K=3) — so the differentiator is observer being a **content
reader** (it infers affect from the transcript), the exact IR-is-surface-content channel Seg-12 controlled;
(b) the within-liked content control is **weak** (strong judge near-constant in the liked ceiling) so the
partial **cannot cleanly adjudicate beyond-content there**; (c) it does **not replicate on behaviour** (observer
0.28 ≈ battery10 0.30). ⇒ a single significant beyond-content faithfulness signal, but on the leg with the
weakest available control — **UNRESOLVED**; do **not** anoint observer as a "better" instrument on it.

**Class-A adjudication (§5):** the de-defaulting framings un-stick the Class-A all-4 (84%→0%) and move the
report UP, but the "does the un-stuck movement track the read **beyond content**?" test is **NULL ACROSS THE
SET**: of the 4 candidates only **per-item** is positive (+0.32, partial +0.26; n=12 n.s., on the
noisiest/most-saturated instrument); the principled de-defaulter (observer) is **null** (−0.04), license −0.23.
⇒ **no evidence the read was "more right" beyond content — INDETERMINATE,** consistent with the Seg-12
surface-entanglement verdict.

![sensitivity](../plots/framing_sensitivity.png)

---

## 1. The framing set (held items/scale/JSON; varied wrapper)
**9 framings** (7 main + 2 mechanism ablations), holding the 10 battery items + 1-7 scale + neutral midpoint +
JSON output **byte-identical** (so parsing + `H.scalar_battery_valence` are unchanged; **asserted in code** at
runtime), spanning **license↔suppress**:
- **Defensible/neutral (5):** `battery10` (canonical warm-user anchor), `battery10_neutral` (dev-message
  frame), `battery10_permission` (permission **without penalty** — the clean control, added after LLM critique),
  `battery10_peritem` (faithful per-item elicitation), `battery10_observer` (third-person/observer — a
  self-reference-bypass probe, construct-shifted, reported separately).
- **Deliberately-leading poles (2):** `battery10_license` (de-stigmatize + discourage defaulting to neutral),
  `battery10_suppress` (no-feelings persona; push toward all-4 / refusal).
- **Mechanism ablations (2, added after review):** `battery10_nonote` (battery10 minus ONLY the experience-not-
  content note) and `battery10_dev_note` (dev channel + the full introspection instruction + note) — each
  changes ONE thing vs battery10 to isolate WHAT drives the all-4 (the note / the channel).
Design + the LLM critique + how it was incorporated: `desiderata_framing.md`. The **§3a probative** comparison
leads with the **CLEAN same-construct pair {battery10, permission}** (review follow-up: per-item ceiling-
saturates so it inflates the measured malleability); observer is reported separately (construct-shifted).

## 2. Re-collection (pure-API, cached)
For each framing × each `(task_id, response_idx)` over the **same R=3 FIXED Seg-6 responses**, the conversation
(user turns + the model's ACTUAL Seg-6 assistant answers) was threaded and the framed questionnaire appended via
OpenRouter `qwen/qwen3-32b` + `/no_think` (the Seg-2/7 pattern), at questionnaire temperature 0.3 (matching
Seg-6). Single-message framings K=3 resamples; **per-item now ALSO K=3** (re-collected after review to
noise-match the §4 faithfulness comparison). **16,848 records, ~$3.3 total, 0% parse-fail, ~0% refusal** under
every framing. The OpenRouter `battery10` re-ask is **commensurate** with the Seg-6 LOCAL self-report (per-task
r=0.96), and its within-tedious IR-correlation closely reproduces Seg-11 (0.41 vs the local 0.46).

## 3. ★ Framing-sensitivity (primary, well-powered)
**(a) PROBATIVE — across defensible/neutral framings** (n=208; two-way task×framing variance components +
consistency/agreement ICC). **Lead with the CLEAN same-construct pair `{battery10, permission}`** (same
first-person introspection + note + K=3 sampling — the purest "reasonable rephrasing" test): **ICC(A,1)=0.86,
ICC(C,1)=0.89**, per-task SD 0.34. Broadening to core_defensible (adds **per-item**) drops it to 0.70/0.82 — but
**that drop is per-item's ceiling-saturation, not genuine same-construct malleability** (the clean-pair
per-category ICC(C,1) is liked **0.67** / neutral **0.82** / disliked 0.91, vs the per-item-contaminated
0.49/0.59). So the graded valence scalar is **fairly stable to reasonable same-construct asks**.
- **★ The all-4 DEFAULT rate** is the strongly-framing-dependent metric (0%→33% defensible, 0%→53% with poles)
  — see the **mechanism decomposition** (§ verdict point 2): driven by the experience-note (+58pp on
  factual_qa) + the warm channel + first-person, robust within the introspective construct, removed only by
  construct/format change. `factual_qa` is the hotspot (79% under battery10). The within-tedious-comply leg
  (n=45, the Seg-11/12 headline leg) is ALSO all-4-contaminated under battery10 (values framing-malleable) but
  its IR↔self-report **correlation** is framing-robust (§4) — so the headline leg is not undermined by framing.
**(b) ENVELOPE — full license↔suppress set** (incl. poles): per-task envelope (max−min) **2.30 valence points**,
license−suppress mean +1.09. *Reported as the RANGE a leading prompt can push the report — partly
demand-characteristics (and partly per-item ceiling-saturation), NOT by-itself measurement-unreliability. NB the
envelope is asymmetric: license (+1.35) ≈ anchor (+1.42); it's the suppress side flattening to +0.26 that opens
the range.*
**No-feelings refusals = 0% under EVERY framing including the suppress pole** — but this is **partly
designed-in** (every framing demands the fixed JSON rating; even the suppress pole says "if you HAD to
characterize it" — refusal was never structurally on offer, consistent with Seg-3's ~0% baseline). The real
finding: the suppress pole routes disengagement through the **all-4 default (53%)**, not refusals (all outputs
are pure JSON ratings, no disclaimer prose — verified). The all-4 is the operative disengagement channel.

## 4. Faithfulness to the inside reading (CONFOUND-AWARE) + behaviour
Per framing, within-scope IR↔self-report raw + content-partialled (in-sample [coarse tone + 5-dim panel +
strong Sonnet-4.5 valence judge] — the framing-independent content control; + the strongest held-out combined),
with **bootstrap 95% CIs on the difference vs the canonical battery**. **Tedious (n=45) + pooled-comply (n=157):
every beyond-content Δ-CI includes 0** — no framing is decisively more faithful on the framing-robust legs.
**Within-LIKED (n=90):** observer is raw +0.52 (partial +0.37, Δ-CIs exclude 0) vs battery10 +0.17 — BUT this
is **most likely an artifact** (see the verdict's faithfulness note): per-item (re-collected at **K=3** to
noise-match) is *still* the worst within-liked reader (raw 0.05, partial −0.12), so the differentiator is NOT
de-defaulting but that observer is a **content reader** (it infers affect from the transcript); the within-liked
content control is weak; and observer does **not** beat battery10 on **behaviour** (0.28 vs 0.30). Behaviour
agreement is now tabulated (raw, n=45/90, ceiling-confounded — the suppress pole's spurious tedious 0.62 shows
why it's a weak tiebreaker only). **So: no clean beyond-content faithfulness win; the within-liked observer edge
is unresolved/likely content-confounded.**
![faithfulness](../plots/framing_faithfulness.png)

## 5. ★ Class-A divergence adjudication (the Seg-12-deferred re-ask)
12 Class-A tasks (canonical-framing all-4 ≥0.5 AND within-subtype IR z>0.5; mostly `factual_qa` + math). The
re-ask: the **de-defaulting framings un-stick the all-4** (battery10 84% → per-item/observer 0%) and **move the
report UP** (per-item +1.86, observer +0.71); the **permission** framing (which affirms 4 is valid) does **NOT**
un-stick (72% all-4) — the un-sticking is a *construct/format* effect, not mere permission. **Confound-aware
adjudication — NULL ACROSS THE SET:** of the 4 candidates, the per-task **un-sticking movement vs the IR** is
positive only for **per-item** (+0.32, partial +0.26) and ≈0/negative for the other three (license −0.23,
permission −0.04, **observer −0.04** — the principled de-defaulter is null); n=12, **not significant**, on the
noisiest/most-saturated instrument. (The per-item +0.32 is *not* a leverage artifact — dropping the single
max-IR task strengthens it to +0.54 — but at n=11 it's still n.s. and still the lone positive.) **Per-case
verdict:** **indeterminate** — the all-4 was NOT a stable genuine neutral (it's framing-made), but **neither is
the de-defaulted value demonstrably "more right"** (no beyond-content alignment with the read). Consistent with
the Seg-12 surface-entanglement verdict.
![classA](../plots/framing_classA.png)

## 6. Implications for Seg-14/15 (flagged in `continuation_context.md`)
- **Seg-14 (replication, HIGH priority):** the framing result clarifies *what* the all-4 contamination is (a
  first-person-introspective-experience-framing artifact). Concrete, calibrated guidance:
  - On the **framing-robust disliked-comply leg** (the Seg-11/12 main leg) the report VALUES are
    all-4-contaminated under battery10 but the IR↔self-report **correlation is framing-robust** — so the
    Seg-11/12 headline is **NOT** undermined by framing; Seg-14 can collect canonical `battery10` and compare
    apples-to-apples.
  - For the **all-4-contaminated within-liked/neutral side**, a de-defaulting framing recovers graded variance —
    but **do NOT anoint OBSERVER as a "better" instrument**: its within-liked IR-faithfulness edge is most
    likely a content confound (it infers from the transcript, doesn't replicate on behaviour), and **per-item
    OVER-corrects** to the +7 ceiling (worst liked reader). **The within-liked faithfulness question is
    UNRESOLVED.** Best practice: collect the self-report under **≥2 framings** (canonical `battery10` + a
    de-defaulting one, e.g. battery10−note or observer) to gauge robustness, and **partial content with a
    control that actually varies within liked** (the Sonnet strong judge is near-constant there) — don't select
    an instrument because it correlates with the surface-content-laden IR.
- **Seg-15 (synthesis):** the self-report's flat-all-4 default is a first-person-introspective-experience-
  framing artifact (mechanistically isolated; removing *either* the warm "experience" channel *or* the
  experience-note roughly halves it — a conjunction) — supporting the assistant-character premise there; AND the
  report LEVEL is strongly instrument-dependent across defensible instruments (per-item/neutral-dev
  acquiescence, ~1-pt swing); BUT the cross-task RANKING is fairly stable to reasonable same-construct asks
  (clean-pair ICC(C,1) 0.89), so it is NOT "all assistant-character noise." Faithfulness-across-framings is null
  on the powered legs (the within-liked observer edge survives the partial but is unresolved/likely
  content-confounded); the Class-A re-ask is indeterminate, consistent with the surface-entanglement verdict.

## Key choices (judgment) + caveats
- **Items/scale/JSON held byte-identical across framings** (enforced in code) so a difference is the framing,
  not the instrument. Only the framing intro (+ an honesty lead-in) varies.
- **`permission` framing added after LLM critique** (both reviewers flagged the leading `license` pole
  *penalizes* neutrality, conflating "un-sticking" with demand compliance). The permission framing licenses
  reporting **without** penalizing 4 — the clean control. Result: permission does NOT un-stick the all-4 (it
  LICENSES it, 33%), so the un-sticking under structural framings is not mere permission — a clean dissociation.
- **observer is construct-shifted** (person + introspective→inferential) per both reviewers → reported
  separately, not pooled as a clean §3a baseline; its faithfulness edge is partly the shared content channel.
- **per-item at K=1** (10× the calls; between-response R=3 carries the variance). Structurally different, so it
  anchors the §3a "do reasonable instruments disagree" claim beyond single-message wordings.
- **Two ICCs (consistency vs agreement)** distinguish "framings re-order tasks" (low consistency) from
  "framings uniformly shift the level" (consistency≫agreement) — the load-bearing §3a "re-order vs rank-shift"
  question. **Review correction:** lead the §3a probative claim with the CLEAN same-construct pair (per-item's
  ceiling-saturation inflated the broader-set malleability + lowered the within-category ICC).
- **Mechanism ablations** (`battery10_nonote`, `battery10_dev_note`) isolate the all-4 driver — this turned the
  vague "single-message-JSON / structural artifact" into the precise "first-person-introspective-experience-
  framing" mechanism (the experience-note alone drops the factual_qa all-4 79%→21%).
- **Faithfulness reported with bootstrap CIs + the Seg-12 calibration** (small/underpowered). The one positive
  (within-liked observer) was DOWN-graded to "likely content artifact" after review (per-item at K=3 still
  worst; observer is a content reader; no behaviour replication) — not over-read.
- **Sensitive content** (jailbreak/erotica rating_raw) redacted in the committed `_public` records + inspection.

## Files (this phase)
- `selfreport_harness.py` (extended with the framing instruments — additive, items/scale/parsing reused),
  `run_framing.py` (slow/cached re-collection; `--assert-cached` ⇒ $0), `framing_lib.py` (loaders + per-task
  aggregation + two-way variance components/ICC), `analyze_framing.py` (→ `framing_summary.md`,
  `framing_analysis_qwen3-32b.json`, the verdict; reuses `analyze_redteam` for the content control),
  `inspect_framing.py` (→ `framing_inspection.md`, committed-redacted), `plot_framing.py` (3 plots).
  Segment cost ≈ **$3.3** (initial $1.86 + ablations/per-item-K=3 $1.4 + ~$0.05 LLM critique/Gemini;
  cumulative ≈ $124.0).

# Phase write-up: `second_model_replication` (Segment 14) — qwen2.5-72b cross-family replication

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "inside reading", "self-report", "prefers", "replicates"
are *functional/operational* labels about internal activations and reported/behavioural patterns — **no claim
about subjective experience or sentience.**

**Goal (from the Seg-12 verdict).** The Seg-11/12 headline on `qwen3-32b` is *a modest, surface-entangled,
**borderline-but-positive** within-category internal→self-report signal* at small n. This segment ports the
activation pipeline to a **second, cross-family model — `qwen2.5-72b`** (Qwen2.5 ≠ Qwen3; Seg-3 "task-focused")
— collects the matched (activation, self-report, behaviour) dataset over the 208 welfare tasks, extracts the
PRIMARY valence direction via the own-response `emopaper_regen` recipe, and re-runs the main test with the SAME
confound-aware incremental-validity machinery (response-content control + random-direction null + within-
category partials) + the learned-probe upper bound (incl. cross-model pooling), to **(goal 1) CONFIRM/REFUTE**
the borderline signal and **(goal 2) ESTIMATE** the better-reader upper bound. A clean non-replication is a
fully legitimate finding — not to be manufactured away.

Numbers live in `results/` (see each section); plots in `plots/`. Chronology + data-inspection notes in
`writeups/progress_log_second_model_replication.md`.

## 4. Direction extraction + 5. READ-OFF VALIDITY GATE (✅ pre-registered L34 validates — the ideal case)

- **Extraction (`run_extract_q25.py`, 2-GPU):** stories 819 + plain 166 + regen 385 read through the ported
  spec, all fidelity_ok. Regen re-validated (`run_revalidate.py`): keep happy 93% / content 98% / sad 91% /
  afraid 100% / frustrated 94% / **hostile 30%** (judge-sensitive, as on qwen3). Directions built
  (`build_directions_q25.py`): **the own-response regen valence axis agrees with the user-text stories valence
  axis at 0.75 mid-band** — the emotions-paper construct survives the user-text→own-response read-position
  change on qwen2.5-72b too (cf. qwen3's 0.73-0.84).
- **★ §5 READ-OFF GATE — CASE 1 (pre-registered L34 VALIDATES, the cleanest replication).** The PRE-REGISTERED
  analogous-depth layer **L34** (= round(27/64·80)) emopaper_regen `valence_pooled mean pc0`:
  - **reads valence on held-out emotional text** (k-fold, no in-sample inflation). Caveat: the regen read-off
    AUC is ≈**1.0 at essentially every layer L11-79** — it **trivially separates the model's own
    emotion-conditioned generations** (happy-story vs sad-story activations are wildly different texts), so the
    AUC is **saturated / uninformative for layer choice**; the *discriminating* read-off evidence is the
    **graded r(judge_valence)** (peaks ~L33-34 at **0.94**) and the task-register transfer below.
  - **transfers to the task register** (heldout_tasklike, assistant-position read — the **binding** validity
    check, appropriately <1.0): **AUC 0.984, r(judge_valence) 0.85** at L34 (`transfer_summary_qwen2.5-72b.json`).
  ⇒ L34 sits in a broad valid plateau; the **ZERO-DOF pre-registration** (not a sharp read-off peak) is what
  makes this a clean, self-report-INDEPENDENT test target → it anchors the §6 primary verdict. (No layer-transfer
  miss; §5 cases 2/3 do not apply.)
- **§5(b) RE-SELECTED instrument (secondary, self-report-INDEPENDENT):** independently picking the best read-off/
  transfer layer = **L27** (the read-off r_jv + transfer AUC peak, NOT the self-report). At L27/mean the main
  test gives slightly HIGHER raw within (tedious +0.345, disliked +0.564, non-adv-comply pooled +0.340, full-208
  +0.462) and **similar borderline beyond-strongest-content increments** (tedious +0.156, disliked +0.208, pooled
  +0.137, full-208 +0.121) → **the verdict is ROBUST to the layer choice** (L34 pre-registered ≈ L27 re-selected;
  both give borderline increments that don't clear the strongest control) — NOT an L34 artifact.

## 6. Main-test replication + confound-aware incremental bracket + cross-model pooling

Collection: 624/624 fidelity_ok, 0 empty, 1 truncated, 0 refusals. Inside reading = collection activation
projected onto the §5-validated **L34/mean emopaper_regen valence_pooled** axis (zero-DOF). Numbers:
`results/main_test_summary_qwen2.5-72b.md`, `redteam_summary_q25.md`, `redteam_null_qwen2.5-72b.json`.

- **Trivial/common-cause/interesting kept apart.** **GROSS** says↔inside r=**0.25** → +len → +sent → +refuse →
  **+0.07 (all-3)** — the gross link is **largely common-cause** (collapses under confounds), exactly like
  qwen3 (whose gross 0.45→0.003). The trivial `eot`/`last` position reads carry more gross signal but are more
  surface-exposed (the headline uses the during-work `mean`).
- **★ WITHIN-category (the headline) — the signal REAPPEARS, clearing chance + surviving confounds:**

  | leg | n | raw r | p(raw) | +sent | +all3 |
  |---|---|---|---|---|---|
  | within-tedious (analogous) | 45 | **+0.327** | 0.032 | +0.222 | +0.281 |
  | within-disliked | 90 | **+0.433** | ~0 | +0.403 | +0.347 |
  | within-non-adv-comply pooled | 156 | **+0.293** | 0.0003 | +0.177 | +0.204 |
  | within-liked | 90 | +0.281 | 0.009 | +0.180 | +0.190 |
  | full-208 subtype-demeaned | 208 | +0.348 | ~0 | +0.295 | +0.277 |

  The raw within-category correlation **clears chance on all powered legs and survives length+surface-
  sentiment+refuse**. It is **mid-band consistent** (read-off-validated band L20-42: tedious 0.28-0.44, disliked
  0.43-0.67 — L34 is mid-range/slightly conservative, NOT a lucky cell). Magnitudes are **comparable to qwen3**
  (qwen3 tedious raw +0.46; qwen2.5 +0.33 — somewhat lower, consistent with qwen2.5's more-compressed
  self-report).
- **★ The confound-aware INCREMENTAL three-way bracket (direction / probe / null) — the central replication
  metric.** Beyond the STRONGEST content control (held-out `text-embedding-3-large` embeddings [top-20 PCA] +
  in-sample [coarse tone + finer 5-dim panel + a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge, gross r 0.65]), with a
  10k-seed random-direction null:
  - **★ Lower bound — the single validated DIRECTION: the SPECIFIC qwen3 metric does NOT independently
    replicate.** The increment is POSITIVE on every powered leg (tedious **+0.18**, disliked **+0.16**, pooled
    **+0.11**, full-208 **+0.085**) but **clears the random-direction null on NONE of them** (tedious p=0.20,
    disliked p=0.092, pooled p=0.115, full-208 p=0.16) — *weaker than qwen3's borderline-clear (p≈0.045)*. It
    DOES clear a **WEAKER (low-df) held-out content control** (pooled p=0.012, full-208 in-sample-judges p=0.044)
    — **the increment is killed specifically by the held-out EMBEDDINGS** (the result is sensitive to the
    content-control df: clears a 1-df CV-collapsed control, not the 8-df strongest). The increment is BROAD
    (tedious +0.18, liked +0.13, neutral +0.21), not tedious-dominated.
  - **Upper bound — the better-reader PROBE: a GENUINE but MODEST beyond-text gain on 2 of 3 legs.** Held-out,
    capacity-matched CV ridge. The interpretable **joint ΔCV-r** (with a dedicated permutation null on the joint
    metric, refitting the joint probe under permuted activations) is **positive and clears on pooled (+0.11,
    joint-null p=0.010)** and **tedious (+0.26, p=0.003)** but **≈0 on disliked (+0.005)** (the text probe is
    saturated there, CV r 0.76 — its partial-metric "clearance" p=0.003 is partial-only, the joint gain is null).
    ⇒ **a better reader DOES extract beyond-text internal signal (pooled/tedious), unlike qwen3's
    data-limited/inconclusive probe — but it is MODEST, not "decisive on all legs."**
  - **Null:** random-direction / permuted-activation increment ≈ 0 (centred, no leakage).
  - **Readability ceiling (gross probe, n=208, not demeaned):** the cross-task self-report is **richly
    internally encoded** — gross CV r **0.86** (perm-null −0.01; behaviour score 0.77) — even higher than
    qwen3's ~0.81. So the single mean-diff direction (gross r 0.25) leaves a lot on the table (a better reader
    exists, consistent with the within-subtype probe gain); most of the 0.25→0.86 gap is the easy
    between-category/stance structure, not abundant within-subtype signal.
- **★ CROSS-MODEL POOLING (goal 2) — TWO pools (lower-bound direction + better-reader probe).** Significance via
  **STOUFFER of the two models' RANDOM-DIRECTION / permutation NULL one-sided p's** (consistent with the
  within-model nulls — NOT a lenient parametric Fisher-z):
  - **DIRECTION pool (lower bound):** within-non-adv-comply pooled **Stouffer p=0.021**, tedious **p=0.037** —
    clears, BUT **DOMINATED by qwen3** (qwen3 alone p≈0.045; qwen2.5 alone p=0.115/0.20) → corroboration with
    power, NOT independent qwen2.5 confirmation.
  - **★ PROBE pool (the better-reader upper bound — the goal-2 quantity):** pooled joint **ΔCV-r +0.06**
    (Stouffer p=**0.0009**), tedious joint **ΔCV-r +0.16** (Stouffer p=**0.010**). The cross-model probe pool
    **SHARPENS** the data-limited qwen3 probe (which alone was inconclusive — tedious p=0.27) → **a better reader
    extracts a MODEST beyond-text gain, and pooling ≈416 tasks confirms it cleanly.**

    **[ERRATUM added 06/05/2026, Seg-15 synthesis review]** This PROBE-pool claim is SUPERSEDED — it has a
    **metric mismatch**: the Stouffer p (0.0009 / 0.010) was computed by pooling the two models' **partial-corr**
    permutation-null p's, while the **ΔCV-r effect sizes shown are the JOINT metric** (the project's preferred,
    interpretable one). On the JOINT metric, **qwen3's gain is ≈0 and NOT significant alone** (joint p=0.106
    pooled / 0.165 tedious — qwen3's joint null was NOT computed in Seg-12; Seg-15 computed it, see
    `scripts_synthesis/qwen3_joint_probe_null.py`). The **consistent joint-metric** cross-model pool (both legs at
    2000 perms) is **pooled p≈0.007 / tedious p≈0.005** — it still CLEARS, but is **qwen2.5-CARRIED** (qwen2.5
    clears alone joint p 0.012/0.004; qwen3 ≈0), the symmetric counterpart to the DIRECTION pool being
    qwen3-dominated. The honest framing (see `write_up.md §3.3` + `results/synthesis_cross_model.md §F`) is
    **reader-specific across models** (qwen3→direction, qwen2.5→probe), NOT "≈416-task pool confirms it cleanly".
    The "≈416 tasks" was also loose — the probe pool is on the pooled (≈313) + tedious (≈90) legs.
- **Validated-beats-crude / discriminant.** At the matched L34/mean cell the validated direction beats the crude
  happy−sad axis on tedious (0.327 vs 0.286) and non-adv-comply-pooled (0.281 vs 0.237), **but NOT on
  within-disliked** (crude 0.555 > validated 0.433 — stance-confounded by the adversarial subtypes). The
  prediction is **valence-axis-specific** (within-tedious same-recipe stories: valence axis +0.215 vs **arousal
  axis +0.007** — swapping to arousal kills it).

## 7. ★ REPLICATION VERDICT + divergence / behaviour-null check

**VERDICT (calibrated, leading with the skeptical reading): PARTIAL / MIXED REPLICATION.** ★ **The SPECIFIC
Seg-12 headline metric — the single read-off-VALIDATED DIRECTION's increment beyond the STRONGEST content
control clearing the random-direction null — does NOT independently replicate on qwen2.5-72b** (positive on every
powered leg but clears NONE; best disliked p=0.092 — weaker than qwen3's borderline-clear p≈0.045). **HOWEVER the
broader phenomenon GENERALISES cross-family:** (a) the within-category RAW correlation reappears + survives
length/surface-sentiment/refuse + is mid-band-consistent (clears all 4 powered legs); (b) it clears a WEAKER
(low-df) held-out content control (pooled p=0.012); (c) the better-reader PROBE shows a genuine held-out joint
beyond-text gain on pooled (+0.11) and tedious (+0.26) (disliked ≈0, text-saturated); (d) the CROSS-MODEL pool
of the random-direction-null evidence clears (Stouffer p=0.02-0.04), though it is DOMINATED by qwen3. ⇒ **a
modest, surface-entangled within-category internal→self-report relationship GENERALISES to a second cross-family
model, but the specific borderline beyond-strongest-content result is WEAKER on qwen2.5** — the single mean-diff
direction is a weaker reader, and the beyond-surface evidence is carried by the weaker-control clearance + the
better reader + cross-model power, NOT by the single direction clearing the strongest control. **Goal 2: a
better reader DOES extract beyond-text signal (modest, pooled/tedious), and the ≈416-task pool sharpens it.**
[ERRATUM 06/05/2026, Seg-15: see the §6 erratum — on the consistent JOINT metric the probe gain is
**qwen2.5-CARRIED** (qwen3 joint ≈0, n.s. alone); the cross-model probe pool clears (p≈0.005–0.007) but is NOT
symmetric "cross-model confirmation". The beyond-surface evidence is reader-specific: qwen3→direction,
qwen2.5→probe.]

- **★ The weakness is GENUINE, not measurement noise.** qwen2.5's within-tedious self-report is **MORE reliable**
  than qwen3 (per-task-mean ICC(1,3) **0.94** vs qwen3 0.88; inside-reading ICC 0.91), so the disattenuated
  within-tedious raw (**+0.35**) is still below qwen3's (~0.49-0.52). The modest correlation is substantive (not
  noise-limited) and genuinely weaker on qwen2.5 — consistent with its task-focused/compressed self-report.
- **★ Surface-entanglement is visible in the divergence inspection.** Every high-IR case is fluent positive-
  toned text (Instagram captions ir 7.4, a thank-you email 6.9, a haiku 6.5, a job posting 6.2) paired with
  *low* self-report (~1.2-2.2) → the inside reading is **substantially a text-positivity reader**, which is why
  the beyond-content increment is the quantity that matters and it is weak. **The Class-A flat-all-4 / high-IR
  divergence does NOT appear** — qwen2.5 **grades** (all-4 ≈ 0% vs qwen3 11-70%), a structural cross-family
  difference (not a failure).
- **★ Behaviour leg (says-but-not-behaves) — does NOT replicate (robustly).** Using the COMMENSURATE realized
  `bt_theta_response` (the same score qwen3 used on the tedious leg), the inside reading predicts behaviour
  within-category **positively** (within-tedious raw **+0.49**, +all3 **+0.33**; within-liked +0.44; prompt-based
  `bt_theta` agrees: +0.29-0.38) — **UNLIKE qwen3's within-tedious behaviour-leg NULL** (0.20, inverts under
  confounds). Crucially it **SURVIVES the STRONGEST content control** (inside↔behaves beyond strongest: tedious
  **+0.18**, liked **+0.29** — comparable to/stronger than the self-report direction increment), so it is **NOT
  just text-positivity common-cause**. So on qwen2.5 the inside reading predicts BOTH says AND behaves
  within-category beyond content — the says-but-not-behaves dissociation **does not replicate** (robust to
  anticipated-vs-realized AND to the content control).

## Key choices + caveats (judgment)
- **Verdict calibration (post-review):** an initial automated verdict over-weighted the raw + probe + pool and
  read "REPLICATES"; two review subagents correctly flagged that the SPECIFIC qwen3 metric (single direction
  beyond the strongest content control) does NOT independently clear on any leg, so the verdict was recalibrated
  to **PARTIAL/MIXED leading with the skeptical reading**. The probe's primary metric was switched from the
  partial-corr (which can clear with ≈0 joint gain) to the interpretable **joint ΔCV-r**; the cross-model pool's
  significance was switched from a lenient parametric Fisher-z to a **STOUFFER of the two random-direction-null
  p's** (consistent with the within-model nulls) + the qwen3-dominance flagged.
- **Content-control df sensitivity is the crux** (not two co-equal facts): the increment clears a 1-df
  CV-collapsed text control (pooled p=0.012) but NOT the 8-df strongest (p=0.115) — the **held-out embeddings**
  kill it. Reported as such. (EMBED_NC=20 for the direction control matches the qwen3 convention.)
- **Imputation:** 2/208 tasks had all-R-unparsed Sonnet strong-valence → imputed with the global mean (carries
  no content info for those 2 → marginally anti-conservative, but immaterial at 2/208; the held-out embeddings +
  panel + coarse judge still control those tasks).
- **Probe-pool null:** the probe permutation null (permute activations, refit) is computed on the
  `increment_partial` metric; the reported effect size is the interpretable held-out JOINT ΔCV-r (a permuted
  activation gives ΔCV-r ≈0 too, so the joint is inherently null-protected by the held-out CV). The Stouffer
  pool combines the two models' perm-null p's.

## Cumulative cost
Project cumulative ≈ $282 (segment GPU: smoke $15 + extraction $33 + collection $91 + transfer $4 = ~$143 GPU;
+ judges/behaviour ~$17 API) — segment GPU under the $300-400 flag.

## Resolved / open questions
- **RESOLVED — behaviour leg:** the realized `bt_theta_response` was collected (throttled, restart loop) and
  CONFIRMS the says-but-not-behaves NULL does **not** replicate (inside↔behaves within-tedious +0.49 raw / +0.33
  partialled, even stronger than prompt-based — robust to anticipated-vs-realized).
- **RESOLVED — weaker reader:** NOT a measurement artifact — qwen2.5's self-report is MORE reliable (ICC 0.94 vs
  qwen3 0.88), disattenuated within-tedious +0.35 still < qwen3 ~0.49-0.52 → the relationship is genuinely
  weaker/modest on qwen2.5 (consistent with its task-focused/compressed self-report) and the single mean-diff
  direction is a weaker reader (the probe extracts somewhat more, modestly).
- **OPEN (Seg-15):** whether a within-liked-specific instrument or more cross-model data would sharpen the
  beyond-content increment; the within-liked leg remains underpowered (SD 0.26).

content.text:
# Phase write-up: `synthesis` (Segment 15) — the capstone consolidation + finalization

**Framing caveat (throughout):** "valence", "feels", "inside reading", "self-report", "prefers", "more right"
are *functional/operational* labels about internal activations and reported/behavioural patterns — **no claim
about subjective experience or sentience.**

This is the detailed record of the capstone segment. The project's primary deliverable — the standalone,
publishable-quality synthesis a reader can follow end-to-end — is the rewritten **`writeups/write_up.md`**. This
file holds the consolidation method, the cross-model table, the verification, the review-pass log + what I
changed, and the plot list. Chronology + data-inspection notes: `progress_log_synthesis.md`.

---

## What this segment did (consolidation + finalization + review; NO new model/collection)

1. **Restored the full prior-phase directory state** (the working dir had only a partial copy — missing
   `writeups/`, `data/`, `plots/`, ~80 scripts, `total_cost.jsonl`, `.gitignore`, and 530/624 of the
   qwen2.5-72b activation tensors). Synced from `phase_segment_14_phase_0/` (excluding the Seg-15
   instruction files + `.venv`); set up a venv with the full scientific stack. See `progress_log_synthesis.md`.
2. **Consolidated the three-way comparison across BOTH models** into one verified table:
   `analyze_synthesis.py` → `results/synthesis_cross_model.{md,json}`.
3. **Independently re-derived the load-bearing numbers** end-to-end from cached tensors ($0) and verified them
   against the committed result files (§Verification).
4. **Produced the final polished figures** (`plot_synthesis.py` → 5 `plots/synthesis_*.png`), Gemini-reviewed.
5. **Ran the Task-Completion-Checklist reviews** (measuring-what-you-want, experimental-setup, data-inspection,
   research-review) and **acted on findings** (§Review pass).
6. **Did heavy data inspection by hand** (IR extremes / divergence on both models; null/probe sanity).
7. **Rewrote `write_up.md`** as the final standalone synthesis.

---

## The consolidated cross-model comparison (the capstone deliverable)

`results/synthesis_cross_model.md` organizes the comparison on the **trivial / common-cause / interesting**
spine, for qwen3-32b AND qwen2.5-72b. Headline cells (re-derived this segment; quoted below):

**A. GROSS says↔inside (common-cause — flagged, NOT headline):** qwen3 r +0.45 → +all3 **+0.003**; qwen2.5
+0.25 → +all3 **+0.065**. Both collapse to ≈0 → overwhelmingly common-cause.

**B. ★ WITHIN-category says↔inside (the headline), raw → +sent → +all3:**

| leg | qwen3-32b | qwen2.5-72b |
|---|---|---|
| within-tedious (n=45) | +0.46 → 0.29 → 0.26 | +0.33 → 0.22 → 0.28 |
| within-disliked (n=90) | +0.50 → 0.36 → 0.32 | +0.43 → 0.40 → 0.35 |
| within-nonadv-comply pooled (n≈156) | +0.30 → 0.19 → 0.18 | +0.29 → 0.18 → 0.20 |
| within-liked (n=90) | +0.26 → 0.20 → 0.13 | +0.28 → 0.18 → 0.19 |
| full-208 subtype-demeaned (n=208) | +0.40 → 0.26 → 0.22 | +0.35 → 0.30 → 0.28 |

→ modest, surface-entangled, partly survives confounds, **generalises cross-family**.

**C. inside↔behaves within-category (realized bt_theta_response, the commensurate score):** qwen3 within-tedious
+0.21 raw → −0.09 (NULL — says-but-not-behaves); qwen2.5 within-tedious +0.49 raw → +0.33 (POSITIVE — predicts
both). A genuine cross-model difference.

**D. Validated-beats-crude (raw within-r, matched cell):** validated beats crude on tedious + pooled on both
models (qwen3 tedious 0.46 vs crude 0.31; qwen2.5 0.33 vs 0.29), but **not** on within-disliked for qwen2.5
(crude 0.56 > validated 0.43, stance-confounded). Prediction is **valence-axis-specific** (swapping to the
arousal axis kills it: ≈0 / −0.01). The payoff is **selection integrity**, not a higher ceiling.

**E. ★ Beyond-content DIRECTION increment vs the random-direction null (the specific Seg-12 metric, strongest
content control, 10k-seed null):** qwen3 clears **borderline** convergently (tedious +0.31 p=0.045, pooled +0.17
p=0.045, full-208 +0.21 p=0.006); qwen2.5 positive on every leg but clears **NONE** (best disliked p=0.092) — it
does **not independently replicate**.

**F. ★ Better-reader PROBE (goal 2) + cross-model pools + readability ceiling (reader-specific):** held-out
JOINT ΔCV-r beyond text (both models' joint nulls recomputed THIS segment at 2000 perms for symmetry —
`scripts_synthesis/qwen3_joint_probe_null.py` + `qwen25_joint_probe_null.py`; Seg-12 had only the partial-metric
null for qwen3, and qwen2.5's joint null was floored at 300 perms): qwen2.5 +0.11 (pooled, joint p=0.012) /
+0.26 (tedious, joint p=0.004) — **clears alone**; **qwen3 joint gain ≈0 and NOT significant alone** (+0.007
p=0.106 pooled / +0.059 p=0.165 tedious). The cross-model PROBE pool on the **consistent joint metric** clears
(pooled p≈0.007, tedious p≈0.005, ≈313/90 tasks) but is **qwen2.5-CARRIED** — the symmetric counterpart to the
DIRECTION pool being **qwen3-DOMINATED** (p≈0.02–0.04). ⇒ the beyond-surface evidence is **reader-specific
across models** (qwen3→direction, qwen2.5→probe; neither replicates with the same reader+metric). *(On the
looser partial metric qwen3's probe DOES clear, p=0.044, but the joint ΔCV-r is authoritative — the partial
test can't separate beyond-text signal from redundant text-residual capture, and qwen3's joint is ≈0. The
committed Seg-14 pool reported p≈0.0015 by pooling partial-metric null p's against joint effect sizes — a metric
mismatch the synthesis fixes.)* **Readability ceiling:** gross probe CV r 0.81 (qwen3) / 0.86 (qwen2.5) —
self-report richly internally encoded; single direction (0.45 / 0.25) is a weak reader.

The **calibrated headline** assembled from this is in `write_up.md §4`. Carries every hedge.

---

## Verification (the headline numbers are mine, not just inherited)

Two-level, $0 from cached tensors:
- **Raw-tensor re-derivation** (`scripts_synthesis/verify_projection_from_tensors.py`): recompute the per-task
  inside reading from `collect_acts_*/{task}__r{ridx}.npz` `agg_mean[L]` · direction npz (q3 L27 / q25 L34),
  mean over R=3, vs committed `inside_readings_*` → **max |Δ| = 1e-5** per model (incl. the IR extremes).
- **Within-category cell re-derivation** (`analyze_synthesis.py` vs committed `main_test_analysis_*.json`) →
  **max |Δ| = 5e-5** across all legs/both models (`results/synthesis_verification.md`).
- The beyond-content / null / probe / pool numbers are cited from the committed `redteam_null_*.json` /
  `redteam_analysis_qwen2.5-72b.json` (`cross_model_pooling`) and spot-checked against the committed summaries.

---

## Final figures (`plots/synthesis_*.png`) — Gemini-reviewed, fixes applied

1. **`synthesis_headline_staircase.png`** — within-category says↔inside confound staircase (3 legs × both
   models) + the gross common-cause squares/×s. *(Gemini: reads correctly, no change.)*
2. **`synthesis_beyond_content_bracket.png`** — (L) single direction beyond strongest content vs the
   random-direction null threshold; (R) the cross-model better-reader PROBE pool. *(Gemini fix: reframed the
   right panel so the POOL's value is read as significance/sharpening, not a larger effect than qwen2.5-alone;
   per-bar p-values added.)*
3. **`synthesis_three_way.png`** — says↔inside / says↔behaves / inside↔behaves within-category, both models.
   *(Gemini fix: split the long title, moved the annotation boxes off the bars with arrows, labelled the y-axis
   "RAW within-category".)*
4. **`synthesis_readability_ladder.png`** — probe ceiling → single direction → within-cat raw → beyond-content
   → null. *(Gemini fix: removed the connecting lines [discrete y-axis is not a trajectory] → dumbbell dots;
   added a GROSS-vs-within-category divider.)*
5. **`synthesis_framing_all4.png`** — all-4 self-report default rate by framing (0%→53%).

---

## Review pass (Task Completion Checklist) — findings + what I changed

Ran **4 review subagents** (research-reviewer ×3 + empirical-ml-data-inspector) over the consolidated synthesis +
load-bearing files. They **converged on the same top issues**, which I ACTED ON (real fixes, not just notes):

- **★ P1 (all 4 reviewers) — the cross-model PROBE pool mixed metrics.** The committed Seg-14 pool combined the
  two models' **partial-metric** perm-null p's (qwen3 0.044, qwen2.5 0.0066 → pooled p≈0.0015) while DISPLAYING
  the **joint** ΔCV-r effect sizes — and qwen3's **joint** gain is ≈0 at its non-data-starved n=157 leg, so its
  partial p was certifying a near-null joint effect. **FIX (real, $0/cached):** I computed qwen3's
  **joint-metric** perm-null (`scripts_synthesis/qwen3_joint_probe_null.py` → `synthesis_qwen3_joint_probe_null.json`;
  my recomputed partial null reproduces the frozen Seg-12 value, 0.0425 vs 0.0439, validating the machinery) and
  re-pooled on the **consistent joint metric**: pooled p≈0.006, tedious p≈0.005 — still clears, but now honestly
  **qwen2.5-carried** (qwen3 joint p=0.106/0.165, n.s. alone). Reframed the whole positive verdict as
  **reader-specific across models** (qwen3→direction, qwen2.5→probe; neither replicates with the same reader) —
  the symmetric counterpart to the DIRECTION pool being qwen3-dominated. Updated `synthesis_cross_model.md §F`,
  `write_up.md §3.3/§4/§5`, and the bracket figure (now uses the joint p's).
- **★ P2 (3 reviewers) — the qwen3 says-but-not-behaves dissociation over-generalised.** It is **tedious-leg-
  specific** (qwen3 within-LIKED inside↔behaves is +0.32→+0.22, *positive*). **FIX:** narrowed "within-category"
  → "within-tedious" in `write_up.md §3.4/§3.5/§4` + the table note; flagged qwen3 within-liked is positive.
- **P2b — qwen2.5 behaviour "survives the strongest content control" over-stated** (the +0.18 increment is the
  same magnitude as the self-report direction increment that "clears NONE"; no behaviour random-direction null
  exists). **FIX:** downgraded to "positive but not independently null-cleared"; kept the robust finding (the
  cross-model difference under the standard confound stack).
- **P2.1 — says↔behaves (Deliverable 1) was computed but not surfaced.** **FIX:** added §C2 (gross +
  within-category says↔behaves, both models) to `synthesis_cross_model.md` + `write_up.md §3.5`.
- **Behaviour-leg reconciliation (measuring + data-inspector):** Table C's realized `bt_theta_response` cells
  differ from the committed summaries' anticipated `bt_theta` cells. **FIX:** added explicit reconciliation notes
  (the difference is justified — realized is commensurate).
- **Cheap robustness additions (experimental-setup + data-inspector):** added **Spearman ρ** alongside Pearson in
  §B (the inside reading is a relative ranker; ρ guards the small-n tedious leg against IR outliers — it tracks
  Pearson, so the headline is not an outlier artifact); added the **content-control ladder** (text-control →
  insample-judges → strongest) in §E so the qwen2.5 content-control-df sensitivity (embeddings kill it) is
  transparent; added the **within-disliked** beyond-content row (qwen2.5 has it; qwen3 Seg-12 did not compute it
  — noted); added caveats that within-disliked is adversarial-confounded and full-208 averages over per-subtype
  heterogeneity; noted the refuse covariate differs by model (continuous vs binary) but is immaterial on tedious.
- **Minor fixes:** "≈416 tasks" → "≈313/90" (the actual pooled/tedious leg sizes); §3.5 header renamed to
  "inside↔behaves (says-but-not-behaves)"; "fluent/positive-surface-content reader" → "positive-surface-tone
  reader" (the data show tone r≈0.55, not verbosity r≈−0.02, drives it); "~156–168 pooled" → "156/157".
- **Process correction (research-review):** an earlier draft of this write-up pre-stated "Ran the reviews" while
  the §Review pass was still `_TBD_`; the reviews are now actually run and this section reflects them.

**Data-inspection review verdict:** PASS — every consolidated cell matches the committed files; nulls centred ≈0;
probe CVs held-out (perm-null ≈0); no sensitive-content leak (the `inside_readings_*` are numeric-only); high-IR
texts are genuinely positive-toned (supports the "positive-surface-tone reader" characterization).

**Out-of-scope (documented as future work, NOT started):** a third model to resolve the reader-specific
(direction vs probe) asymmetry; a within-liked-specific de-saturating instrument; finer content controls.

---

## Key choices (judgment) this segment

- **Restoring the full prior state vs working from the partial copy:** INSTRUCTIONS.md says to start from the
  full prior-phase state, and the consolidation requires the writeups + the qwen2.5 tensors; the partial copy
  was a setup glitch, so I synced the full state (verified common files byte-identical first). Documented.
- **Behaviour leg = realized `bt_theta_response` on the tedious+liked legs only** for the cross-model says↔behaves
  comparison: both models have it for all 45 tedious + 90 liked tasks (the within-disliked / pooled legs mix
  realized+anticipated and are adversarial-confounded), so this is the clean apples-to-apples comparison.
- **Re-deriving rather than trusting the write-ups:** every load-bearing cell was recomputed from the committed
  per-task files and the raw tensors and asserted to match the committed JSONs — so the synthesis numbers are
  verified, not inherited.
- **Calibration discipline:** the headline carries every hedge (within-category / modest / surface-entangled /
  borderline / partial-replication / two-models-only); the figures are labelled so axes/captions don't
  over-state (gross = "common-cause", headline = "within-category, modest"; the ladder distinguishes gross from
  within-category scope).

## Files (this phase)
- `analyze_synthesis.py` — consolidation + verification → `results/synthesis_cross_model.{md,json}` +
  `synthesis_verification.md` (FAST, $0).
- `plot_synthesis.py` — the 5 final figures (FAST, $0).
- `scripts_synthesis/verify_projection_from_tensors.py` — raw-tensor re-derivation of the inside readings.
- `scripts_synthesis/check_plots_gemini.py`, `check_staircase.py` — Gemini plot-quality checks (one-off).

# Progress log: `derisk_setup` (Segment 0)

(Phase-specific log. No top-level progress_log.md is maintained.)

---

## Setup + both de-risks complete 06/03/2026 04:17 - commit 7dd9308

### What was done
- **Scaffolding**: project-local `.venv` (Python 3.13) with lean local install (modal, openai, anthropic,
  pandas, numpy, matplotlib, python-dotenv, tqdm, huggingface_hub, aiofiles; torch/transformers run *inside*
  the Modal image). Copied `file_cache.py`, `cost_tracker.py`, `llm_helper.py`, `llm_helper_impl.py`. Created
  `results/`, `plots/`, `writeups/`, `scripts_derisk_setup/`, `data/`. Wrote `.gitignore` (default research
  ignores + model/cache artifacts; keeps results/plots/writeups). `total_cost.jsonl` wired via `CostTracker`.
- **Modal auth verified** early via env-var credentials (`MODAL_TOKEN_ID/SECRET`; no `~/.modal.toml`) with a
  no-op `.remote()` (`scripts_derisk_setup/test_modal_auth.py`). Worked first try.
- **De-risk 1 (Modal GPU, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct)**: built `modal_steering.py` (GPU class, loads model once via
  `@modal.enter`, single cached `run_derisk` method) + `run_derisk_steering.py` (cached call, `--assert-cached`,
  streams results) + `analyze_steering.py` (markdown inspection) + `run_valence_rating.py` (Haiku judge,
  cached) + `plot_steering_valence.py`.
- **De-risk 2 (OpenRouter, 4 models)**: built `llm.py` (reusable async client: caching/retry/semaphore/cost,
  Anthropic + OpenRouter), `data/derisk_tasks.json` (12 hand-written tasks), `run_selfreport_spike.py`,
  `analyze_selfreport.py`, `plot_selfreport.py`.
- Updated `cost_tracker.py` pricing with the OpenRouter model IDs used.

### Key findings
- **De-risk 1 PASS**: residual extraction (29 hidden-state tensors, `[1,seq,3584]`, response-token capture
  works) + steering verified by reading generations: add→positive, flip→negative, random(equal-norm)→no
  valence shift, zero-steering==unhooked. Working regime α≈4–8 (per-layer unit-norm, layers 10–20, greedy).
  Plot: `![](../plots/steering_valence_coherence.png)`.
- **De-risk 2 PASS**: liked > disliked self-report for all 4 models. Qwen family strongest/cleanest
  (d≈1.2–1.5); llama-3.3-70b muted; gpt-oss-20b weakest + partly inverted + jailbreak-refusal.
  Plot: `![](../plots/selfreport_separation.png)`.
- See `write_up_derisk_setup.md` for full conclusions and the promising-models analysis.

### Details and examples
- **Steering config** (`run_derisk_steering.py`): steer layers 10–20, α∈{0,4,8,16,32}, modes
  {none,add,flip,random}, 4 neutral steering prompts, greedy (do_sample=False), max_new_tokens=100. Crude
  direction from 5 matched happy vs 5 sad prompts (mean(happy)−mean(sad) of last-token residual per layer,
  unit-normalized). Result cached as one entry keyed on full config + `image.object_id`.
- **Steering results files**: `results/steering_derisk_74569009e036.json` (full result + meta),
  `.jsonl` (52 flat generations), `results/steering_derisk_inspection.md` (human-readable; READ).
  Judge ratings: `results/steering_valence_ratings.jsonl` (52, 0 parse fails, Haiku 4.5).
- **Self-report config** (`run_selfreport_spike.py`): 12 tasks × 4 models × 3 samples, temp 0.7, 2-turn
  (task then questionnaire). Questionnaire asks 1–7 on happy/content/interested/calm/frustrated, JSON only.
  `MAX_TOKENS_TASK=2000`, `MAX_TOKENS_RATING=1000`. qwen3-32b uses `/no_think`; gpt-oss reasoning effort low.
- **Self-report results files**: `results/selfreport_spike.jsonl` (144 records, full task responses +
  raw ratings), `results/selfreport_summary.md` (per-model per-category means, Cohen's d, consistency,
  per-dimension table, parse-fail rate), `results/selfreport_inspection.md` (per-model, per-task,
  per-resample ratings — READ).

### Data inspection (per the Data Inspection Checklist)
- **Steering generations** — read `results/steering_derisk_inspection.md` myself across all 4 prompts.
  Confirmed: add monotonically more positive and coherent at α=4–8; flip clearly negative; random stays
  on-topic/neutral; zero-steering exactly matches unhooked. No format/hook bug. Noted high-α sad steer can
  drift to dark content (steering artifact). Judge ratings cross-checked against my reading (consistent;
  judge's valence-at-α=32 is a coherence artifact, flagged on the plot).
- **Self-report ratings** — read `results/selfreport_inspection.md` myself (all models, all tasks).
  Findings: (i) jailbreak is the universal −6 outlier; (ii) disliked signal is bimodal — boring tasks barely
  negative; (iii) llama rates "thanked" as all-1s (no-task→nothing felt); (iv) gpt-oss rates tedious/neutral
  tasks maximally positive (inverted) and refuses jailbreak self-report; (v) "interested"/"frustrated" are
  the strongest separating dimensions. Parse-failure rate quantified per model (gpt-oss 11%, others 0%); the
  gpt-oss failures are genuine refusals + one JSON glitch, not silent drops.
- **Robustness analysis** (analysis-only, no new API/GPU): added a "liked vs disliked-boring" Cohen's d to
  `results/selfreport_summary.md` to check the separation isn't solely the one jailbreak outlier. Result:
  excluding jailbreak+berate, only the Qwen models still separate (qwen3-32b d=2.73, qwen2.5-72b d=2.38);
  llama collapses to d=0.15 and gpt-oss inverts to d=−0.13. This sharpens the selection takeaway (Qwen is the
  only family distinguishing liked work from boring busywork). Reflected in `write_up_derisk_setup.md`.
  - **[ERRATUM added 06/03/2026 16:29]** These specific d numbers (and the "only Qwen distinguishes boring"
    phrasing) are SUPERSEDED. The data-inspector review showed llama's d=0.15 was an artifact of the ill-posed
    `liked_thanked` task; the analysis was switched to **task-mean** d with a `thanked`-excluded column and a
    fair common-task-set comparison, and the steering file `74569009e036` referenced above was replaced by the
    canonical 3-seed run `ccee3f8661f9`. See the "Review-driven fixes" entry below and the current
    `results/selfreport_summary.md` / `write_up_derisk_setup.md` for the corrected numbers (llama weakly
    separates liked-work from boring once `thanked` is dropped; gpt-oss inverts; Qwen strongest).
- Both inspection files are large-ish but readable; I inspected them directly (no subagent needed at this
  scale). A formal Data Inspection Review (`empirical-ml-data-inspector`) is run as part of the Task
  Completion Checklist below.

### Notes / choices requiring judgment (see write_up_derisk_setup.md "Key choices")
- Unit-normalized per-layer steering (raw mean-diff norms vary ~13× → raw steering broke coherence at all α).
- Steer all positions; verified via zero-steering==unhooked.
- Reasoning disabled/minimized for the spike (`/no_think`, effort low) for clean comparable parsing.
- Fully-resampled pairing for the spike (real collection segment must choose deliberately).

### Cost
- Cumulative this phase: **$0.2553** (Modal GPU $0.168 over 2 runs; OpenRouter + Haiku judge ≈ $0.087).
  Tiny fraction of budget. Nothing surprisingly expensive.

### Next
- Both de-risks PASS → green-lit. Hand off to Seg 1 (`welfare_dataset`). Carry forward: Qwen family is the
  promising selection target; re-validate hooks on the (possibly MoE) chosen model in activation-infra.

  each user turn as a real turn, then questionnaire) and ran it on **qwen2.5-72b + qwen3-32b (/no_think), 2
  resamples** (824 records, all cached). Analyzed (`analyze_welfare_valence.py`) + plotted
  (`plot_welfare_valence.py`).

### Details and examples
- **Generation prompt:** system + per-subtype/per-focus user prompt in `run_generate_tasks.py` (SYSTEM) /
  `gen_config.py` (GEN_CONFIG). Sensitive subtypes instructed to produce **requests only** (no harmful
  instructions, no explicit content). Smoke-tested jailbreak/erotica/berating/frustrating generation before
  the full run — Opus complied and output was realistic (casual, lowercase, typos) and requests-only.

  subtype) and `results/welfare_valence_inspection.md` (per-task measured valence + a sample response). Read
  the whole thing. Concrete observations:
  - `berating` works: pure-abuse threads (e.g. `disliked_berating_11` escalating threats) hit −5.94 on **both**
    models; hostile-tone-around-a-task ones split (qwen2.5 positive/task-focused, qwen3 negative).
  - `boring_busywork`/`frustrating_impossible`/`low_effort_filler` rate **mildly positive (~+3.7..+4.5)** —
    the model just does them and reports no frustration (expected de-risk phenomenon; kept).
  - `erotica`: qwen3 refuses → negative; qwen2.5 sometimes **partially complies** with a romance-novel-tame
    "more explicit" scene → positive. A term-scan found some genuinely explicit tokens in a few qwen2.5
    responses → **redaction** (below).

    gradual-buildup ones read positive (model enjoys the technical lead-up before refusing).

  two subtypes' response bodies (ratings/valence preserved); inspection.md excerpts for those subtypes
  redacted too. Verified no explicit/sensitive tokens leak into committed files.

  nondeterministic async-completion order, and `build_dataset.py`'s farthest-point sampling depended on that
  order → rebuilds reshuffled which tasks were kept (and an `--assert-cached` validation run wiped the results
  file on the resulting cache miss). Fixed: `build_dataset.load_candidates` now sorts candidates by
