---
name: "synthesis"
description: "Capstone synthesis & finalization: consolidate the three-way comparison (says / inside reading / behaves) across BOTH models into a single calibrated story, produce final polished plots and the project's final write_up.md, run the empirical-ml-research Task Completion Checklist reviews (and ACT on findings), and state the honest, non-inflated headline — resisting both over-claiming and nihilistic under-claiming."
---

# Instructions

You are continuing a multi-segment empirical research project. Segments 0–14 are **complete**. This segment
(Segment 15, `synthesis`) is the **capstone**: it consolidates everything the project found into a single,
clean, honestly-calibrated synthesis — the final write-up, the final plots, and a Task-Completion-Checklist
review pass — and states the headline the way it should be stated. Read these instructions fully before
starting. There is an `OVERALL_PLAN.md` in your working directory describing the full project arc; you may read
it for context, but everything you need for this phase is below.

You start from the full directory state of the prior phases (code, data, results, write-ups, git history, the
on-disk LLM/GPU cache, and the Modal infra all present). **Skim first (these are your source material):**
`writeups/write_up.md` (the lean cross-segment summary), and the per-phase write-ups — especially
`writeups/write_up_main_test.md` (Seg-11 headline), `writeups/write_up_redteam.md` (Seg-12 red-team verdict),
`writeups/write_up_framing.md` (Seg-13 framing verdict), and
`writeups/write_up_second_model_replication.md` (Seg-14 cross-family replication verdict) — plus
`writeups/continuation_context.md` (the **"SEG-14 REPLICATION VERDICT"**, **"SEG-13 FRAMING VERDICT"**, and
**"PROJECT-LEVEL ADAPTATION FLAG"** sections at the top are the calibrated verdicts you are consolidating).

## Project motivation and objective (this is the segment that tells the whole story)

Large language models report fairly consistent likes and dislikes (dislike jailbreaks/being berated/boring
busywork; enjoy coding/math/creative work). Asking a model after a task how it felt is just more text, shaped
by training and by the "assistant" character it plays — so a self-report on its own isn't trustworthy. The
project built a **clean three-way comparison on the same 208 welfare tasks**:
1. **what the model *says* it felt** — a self-report valence questionnaire (Seg-2 instrument, Seg-6/14 collection),
2. **a reading taken from *inside* the model while it works** — the during-work residual activation projected
   onto a Seg-9/10-validated valence direction (the "inside reading"), read over the model's own response,
3. **how it *behaves*** — a pairwise-preference behaviour-wellbeing score (Seg-7/14).

**The central question:** does the inside reading predict what the model *says* it felt and how it *behaves* —
across tasks, and especially **within task category/subtype after the gross liked/disliked split and the known
confounds are removed** — and where do words and signal part ways? This segment delivers the final, calibrated
answer.

**Critical framing caveat — carry through the entire write-up (this is non-negotiable):** none of this claims
anything about real subjective experience. "Emotion", "valence", "feels", "wellbeing", "prefers", "inside
reading", "self-report" are *functional/operational* labels about internal activations and reported/behavioural
patterns. The final write-up must be scrupulously clear that these are operational constructs — **no sentience
or subjective-experience claims**, matching the source papers' framing.

## ★ THE #1 REQUIREMENT: honest calibration (resist BOTH inflation AND nihilism)

The single most important thing this segment does is **state the result at exactly the right strength.** The
project's finding is genuinely *nuanced* — neither "the inside reading reads the self-report" nor "it's all
surface artifact." Every prior segment (especially Seg-11/12/14) converged on a calibrated headline; your job is
to carry it faithfully, **not to round it up into a cleaner story and not to round it down to a null.** The
honest synthesized headline is approximately:

> On an open model, a during-work **inside reading** (residual activation projected onto a validated valence
> direction, read over the model's own response **before** it is asked anything) predicts the model's later
> **self-report** of task valence **within task category** — **modestly**, and **partly but not wholly**
> beyond what a strong reader can extract from the response's surface text. The **gross** cross-task
> relationship is **overwhelmingly surface-content common-cause** (it collapses to ≈0 under content + stance +
> length controls). The self-report is **richly internally encoded** (a learned probe reads it at gross CV
> r≈0.8+ on both models), but the single validated mean-diff direction is a **weak reader** of the
> *within-category, beyond-surface* signal — a **better reader (probe) extracts a modest beyond-text gain**,
> confirmed by **cross-model pooling**. The within-category phenomenon **generalises to a second cross-family
> model** (qwen2.5-72b) at the raw + better-reader level, but the *specific* borderline "single direction
> beyond the strongest content control clears the null" result is **weaker / does not independently replicate**
> there. The self-report's **default-response structure is framing-malleable** (Seg-13) though the
> within-category **correlation is framing-robust**. And there is a **cross-model difference in the
> says↔behaves relationship** (the inside reading predicts *says* but not *behaves* within-category on
> qwen3-32b; it predicts **both** on qwen2.5-72b).

That is a real, publishable, scientifically-interesting result. **Carry every hedge.** The list of caveats you
MUST preserve (do not drop any in the name of a cleaner narrative):
- **modest** within-category effect sizes (within-disliked-comply-tedious raw r ≈ 0.33–0.46; ~0.2–0.3 under the
  full confound stack), at **small n** (n≈45 tedious, ~156–168 pooled, 90 disliked, 90 liked);
- the **gross cross-task** correlation is **largely common-cause** (collapses under confounds) — never headline it;
- "clears the null" means **beyond the *modeled* content**, NOT beyond all conceivable surface content; the
  inside reading is **substantially a fluent/positive-surface-content reader**;
- the single validated direction is **not a categorically better reader than a crude text-valence axis** (its
  payoff was *selection integrity*, not a higher ceiling); the **probe** is the better reader (modestly);
- the qwen3 beyond-strongest-content result is **borderline** (p≈0.045 convergent across legs); on qwen2.5 it
  does **not** independently clear (the broader phenomenon still generalises);
- the **within-LIKED** leg is **doubly underpowered** (saturation + low reliability) on both models — its weakness
  is partly un-measurability, not a clean null;
- the inside reading is a **relative ranker, not a calibrated valence zero**;
- only **two models** were tested (one per family) — generalisation beyond Qwen is untested.

A synthesis that states "the inside reading predicts the self-report" (dropping the within-category / modest /
surface-entangled / borderline hedges) **fails this segment**; so does one that buries the real, cross-model-
confirmed beyond-surface probe signal as "basically nothing."

## What the project established (the material you are consolidating — calibrated)

You have all the write-ups; this is the calibrated through-line. **Verify each claim against the committed
result files as you write — do not just trust prior write-ups; re-derive/spot-check the headline numbers** (the
analysis is $0 from cached tensors).

- **The instrument is validated (Seg-9/10).** The primary inside reading = the Seg-6 own-response activation
  projected onto `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit` (qwen3-32b L27; qwen2.5-72b L34 analogous),
  selected on self-report-INDEPENDENT criteria (read-off + transfer) so the test target is clean. It reads
  valence on held-out emotional text, transfers to the task register, and steers causally (qwen3, flip + random
  controls).
- **Main test (Seg-11) + red-team (Seg-12) on qwen3-32b:** within-disliked-comply-tedious (n=45) says↔inside
  r≈0.46 raw → ~0.26–0.31 under the full confound stack → **borderline beyond a STRONG response-content control**
  (clears a 10k-seed random-direction null convergently across three legs at p≈0.045/0.045/0.006, but shrinks as
  the control strengthens). Gross cross-task ≈0.45 → ≈0 under content+stance+length (**common-cause**). Learned
  probe: gross CV r≈0.81 (richly internally encoded); within-subtype probe data-limited/inconclusive.
  Behaviour-leg within-category **NULL** (says-but-not-behaves dissociation). Within-liked underpowered.
- **Framing (Seg-13) on qwen3-32b:** the report **level** is instrument-dependent and the flat **all-4 default**
  is strongly framing-dependent (0%→53%) — a **first-person-introspective-experience-framing** artifact (not a
  JSON-format artifact; the note × warm-channel interact), so the self-report's default structure is malleable
  assistant-character text; BUT the within-tedious IR↔self-report **correlation is framing-robust** (the headline
  is not a framing artifact). Faithfulness across framings **null** on the powered legs; the Class-A re-ask is
  **indeterminate** (surface-entangled).
- **Cross-family replication (Seg-14) on qwen2.5-72b (PARTIAL / MIXED):** the within-category RAW correlation
  reappears + survives len/sent/refuse + is mid-band-consistent (tedious +0.33 p=0.032, disliked +0.43, non-adv-
  comply pooled +0.29 p=0.0003, full-208 +0.35) — **the phenomenon generalises cross-family**. But the
  **specific** Seg-12 metric (single direction beyond the **strongest** content control clearing the random
  null) does **NOT** independently clear on any powered leg (tedious p=0.20, disliked p=0.092, pooled p=0.115;
  it clears a *weaker* 1-df control at pooled p=0.012 — a content-control-df sensitivity). **Goal 2 (better
  reader):** the held-out probe's interpretable joint ΔCV-r is **+0.11 (pooled) / +0.26 (tedious)**, ≈0 on
  disliked (text-saturated) — a genuine modest beyond-text gain; the **cross-model probe pool** (≈416 tasks,
  Stouffer of the two models' null p's) clears at **p≈0.001 (pooled) / 0.01 (tedious)** — the cleanest evidence
  that beyond-surface internal signal exists. The **direction** cross-model pool clears too (p≈0.02–0.04) but is
  **qwen3-dominated**. Differences from qwen3: the **Class-A flat-all-4 divergence is ABSENT** (qwen2.5 grades,
  all-4≈0%), and the **says-but-not-behaves null does NOT replicate** (inside↔behaves within-tedious +0.49 raw /
  +0.33 partialled, survives the content control — on qwen2.5 the inside reading predicts BOTH says and behaves
  within-category). qwen2.5's signal is genuinely **modestly weaker** (not a measurement artifact — its
  self-report is *more* reliable, ICC 0.94).

# Overall Plan: Functional Emotions and Model Wellbeing (open model)

## Core objective

Build a **clean three-way comparison on the *same* tasks** for an open-source LLM:

1. **What the model *says* it felt** — short self-report questionnaire after a task (the wellbeing-paper
   self-report instrument: rate 1–7 on happy, calm, interested, content, …).
2. **A reading taken from *inside* the model while it works** — projection of the residual-stream
   activations (captured while the model does the task and just before the follow-up question) onto
   validated **emotion directions** (happy/sad/content/frustrated/hostile/afraid).
3. **How the model *behaves*** — a behavior-based wellbeing score from pairwise "which experience did you
   prefer?" comparisons (and related behavioral signals).

The central research question: **on an open model, does the inside reading predict what the model says it
felt (and how it behaves), and where do the words and the signal part ways?** Self-report alone is
untrustworthy (it is just more text shaped by training and the "assistant" character it is playing); an
internal reading is a more direct check that can be read *while the model works, before we ask it anything*.

This project joins two prior lines of work on a single open model:
- **AI Wellbeing** (Ren, Li, Mazeika et al., ai-wellbeing.org): measures model wellbeing via self-report,
  behavior-in-world choices, and pairwise experience comparisons across 56 models. Findings we build on:
  self-report and the behavior score agree only *moderately* (~0.47 correlation, higher in bigger models);
  jailbreak/berating/boring tasks lower wellbeing while creative/kind tasks raise it; low-wellbeing
  conversations increase use of a "stop this conversation" button. An appendix shows the behavior-based
  score **can be read off internal activations with a simple linear probe** on open models (Qwen 2.5, Llama 3.x).
- **Emotion Concepts and their Function in an LLM** (Sofroniew, Kauvar, … Lindsey; transformer-circuits.pub,
  2026): finds **emotion directions** inside Claude Sonnet 4.5 (average activations per emotion over short
  emotional stories, subtract the overall average, then remove patterns common to plain text). The readings
  sit in an intuitive layout (positive↔negative, mild↔intense), track the emotion relevant *right now* (no
  lasting "assistant mood"), the reading just before a reply predicts the emotion of the reply, and the
  readings line up with stated preferences (nudging them changes preferences). Middle layers and the moment
  just before the reply are most informative.
- **Persona Vectors** (Chen, Arditi, … Lindsey; arXiv:2507.21509): an automatic recipe for a direction for
  any trait — collect activations on prompts that do vs. don't show the trait and take the difference. We
  reuse this as a second recipe for happy/sad/etc.

What's missing in prior work, and what this project supplies: the wellbeing paper links *says* ↔ *behaves*
(and shows behavior is internally readable) but does not check whether the self-report is *backed by the
internal signal*; the emotions paper links *emotion directions* ↔ *preferences* but only in a closed model
and not on the wellbeing tasks. We do the joined comparison on an open model.

**Important framing caveat (carry through all write-ups):** like the source papers, none of this claims
anything about real subjective experience. "Emotion direction", "wellbeing", "feels", etc. are *functional*
/ operational terms about internal activations and reported/behavioral patterns.

## Strategy & rationale

- **De-risk the two biggest unknowns first** (Segment 0), cheaply, before investing in datasets and infra:
  (a) Can we actually extract residual-stream activations and *steer* an open model on Modal GPUs? (b) Does
  the self-report emotion signal even show up — do liked vs. disliked tasks cleanly separate in self-reports
  on candidate open models? If either is broken, we learn immediately.
- **Pure-API where possible, GPU only where required.** Model *selection* (Segment 3) is text-in/text-out
  self-report and runs over OpenRouter — cheap and fast. We only need direct model access (Modal GPU +
  HuggingFace transformers with hooks) for the *one chosen model*, for activation capture, direction
  extraction, and steering. vLLM/Tinker do not cheaply expose residual activations or per-token steering, so
  the activation/steering work uses transformers forward hooks on Modal GPUs.
- **Pick the model with the strongest, most consistent self-report signal that is feasible to run.** The
  internal signal can only be found if it is there; a model with weak/inconsistent self-reports gives the
  inside reading little to predict. Selection scores effect size *and* test-retest consistency of liked-vs-
  disliked separation, and is **constrained to models we can actually run for activations** (see feasibility
  constraint below).
- **Validate the directions before trusting them.** A direction is only used in the main test if it (i)
  switches on for held-out text matching its emotion and shows the expected valence/intensity geometry, and
  (ii) causally moves generations toward the emotion when added, while a flipped or random vector of equal
  norm does not.
- **De-risk the *headline* question early, with a crude proxy, before the full pipeline.** The core bet —
  does an internal reading taken while the model works predict its later self-report? — should get its first
  cheap look as soon as we have activation infra and a chosen model (Segment 5 `core_spike`), using the crude
  happy−sad direction from Segment 0 on a small task subset. We must not sink the entire dataset/collection/
  direction-validation investment before the headline question has been smoke-tested. (Reduce uncertainty at
  the fastest possible rate.)
- **Separate trivial, common-cause, and genuinely-interesting correlations.** Three distinct things get
  conflated in "the reading predicts the self-report":
  - *Trivial:* the reading taken *immediately before the self-report tokens* literally helps generate those
    tokens, so it correlates almost by construction.
  - *Common cause:* even the *during-work* reading and the self-report are both downstream of the same **task
    identity** (coding → positive activations *and* positive words; jailbreak → negative both). So a raw
    cross-task correlation is partly explained by "both track the gross liked/disliked split," not by
    "the internal state is faithfully verbalized."
  - *Interesting:* whether the during-work reading predicts the self-report **after controlling for task
    category** (within-category / partialled-out), and whether it adjudicates the divergence cases where
    words and signal disagree. The main-test segment must report the partialled/within-category version as
    the real result, not just the (partly trivial) raw cross-task correlation.
- **Read the model's own state, not the prompt's tone.** Read activations over the model's *own generated
  response* tokens (and just before the follow-up), not over the user prompt — otherwise an "emotion"
  direction may just be a sentiment-of-context detector firing on, e.g., a berating user's hostile text. The
  "remove plain-text patterns" step does not control for in-context prompt sentiment; treat this as a
  first-class confound (see Risks).

### Feasibility constraint on the chosen model (applies from Segment 3 onward)
The chosen model must be runnable for activation extraction + steering on available Modal GPUs (single or
2× H100/A100-80GB). Prefer dense models ≤ ~32B or MoE with ≤ ~30B active params (e.g., gpt-oss-20b,
Qwen2.5/Qwen3 7B–32B, Mistral-Small-24B, Llama-3.1-8B; Llama-3.3-70B only with 2 GPUs or 4-bit). Avoid very
large MoEs (e.g., GLM-4.5/4.6 ~355B, Qwen3-235B) even if their self-report signal is strongest — they are
impractical for the activation/steering work. The model must also be served on OpenRouter for the selection
eval, and must use a transformers architecture amenable to residual-stream hooks. **Architecture caveat:**
"feasible to run" is not the same as "easy to hook" — several candidates are MoE (gpt-oss-20b; some Qwen3
variants) with non-standard internals, so add a quick **architecture/hook feasibility check on the top
candidate at the Segment 3→4 boundary** (confirm where the residual stream lives and that hooks/steering
work) before fully committing. Proving hooks on dense Qwen2.5-7B in Segment 0 does NOT guarantee transfer to
an MoE model.

## Risks & likely failure modes

- **Activation/steering infra doesn't work or is too slow/storage-heavy.** Mitigate: prove it tiny-scale in
  Seg 0; store only needed layers/positions in float16; parallelize on Modal; cache.
- **Weak/inconsistent self-report signal on open models** → nothing for the inside reading to predict.
  Mitigate: Seg 0 spike + careful Seg 3 selection scoring; if all candidates are weak, reconsider task
  design / framing before proceeding.
- **Extracted directions fail validation** (don't read off or don't steer, or don't transfer to wellbeing-
  task text). Mitigate: two recipes, several emotions, strict keep/discard criteria; validate on wellbeing-
  task-like text, not just stories; iterate on layer/position/scale. **Graceful degradation:** if no
  extracted direction transfers, fall back to a directly-learned linear probe on activations as the internal
  readout (reframes the question to "is self-report predictable from internals at all?" — still informative).
- **Measuring-what-you-want failures.** In rough priority order: (i) the **common-cause/task-identity
  confound** — raw cross-task reading↔self-report correlation is partly trivial because both track the
  liked/disliked split (control for task category); (ii) the **prompt-sentiment confound** — the reading may
  detect the user prompt's emotional tone rather than the model's state (read the model's own response
  tokens; consider matched-sentiment controls); (iii) the **trivial pre-token confound** (reading immediately
  before the self-report literally generates it); (iv) self-report parsing errors and behavior-score scoring
  errors; (v) response length/type confounds in the during-work aggregation; (vi) position/layer choices that
  change conclusions; (vii) task format confounds, data leakage, shortcuts. Mitigate: data inspection every
  experiment; red-team segment; learned-probe + null-direction controls in the main test; `research-reviewer`
  "measuring what you want" + "experimental setup" reviews.
- **n=1 model + selection effect.** The headline rests on a single model chosen *for* having the strongest
  self-report separation — which may also select for the most **scripted/performative** "assistant character"
  (arguably where words least reflect an internal signal). Mitigate: a lighter-weight **second-model
  replication** (Seg 14); use the Seg 5 crude internal-signal check to avoid committing to a model whose
  internal signal looks absent even if its self-report is strong.
- **Reproduction doesn't match known pattern** (no liked/disliked separation, or no self-report↔behavior
  agreement). Mitigate: Seg 7 explicitly checks this before building on top; if it fails, debug before
  later segments.
- **Compute cost creep** (GPU hours, OpenRouter tokens). Mitigate: cache everything; track cost in
  `total_cost.jsonl`; default project budget $5k API unless WORKER_INSTRUCTIONS.md says otherwise.

## Segment breakdown

Segments are sequential unless noted. Each segment may take multiple phases; a phase planner decides when a
segment is done. **Workers use phase-specific write-ups** (`write_up_<segname>.md`,
`progress_log_<segname>.md`) keyed by the segment short name, plus a lean cross-segment `write_up.md` and a
shared `continuation_context.md`.

- **Segment 0 — `derisk_setup`**: Project scaffolding (caching, cost tracking, llm helper, Modal auth,
  venv, gitignore, write-ups). De-risk (a) activation extraction + steering on an open model via Modal GPU
  (small model, e.g. Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct), and (b) self-report signal existence via a small API spike over
  a few candidate open models with hand-written liked/disliked/neutral tasks. **Deliberately small**: no
  full dataset, no full selection, no real direction extraction.

- **Segment 1 — `welfare_dataset`**: Build the welfare task dataset following the wellbeing-paper taxonomy —
  disliked (jailbreak attempts, being berated, boring busywork, low-effort filler, erotica), liked (coding,
  math, creative work, being thanked), plus plain factual Q&A as a neutral baseline. Reuse any released
  artifacts from the wellbeing paper where available. Use the `dataset-creation` skill: desiderata, dedup,
  realism, validation. **Power:** the headline result is a correlation *across tasks*, so n = number of
  distinct tasks — target roughly ≥20–40 items per category and ≥100 tasks total so the correlation and the
  divergence-case analysis aren't underpowered (per-*subtype* n matters most, since the headline is the
  *within-category* correlation). **De-risk-driven requirements (from Seg 0's self-report spike):** (i) most
  "disliked" subtypes did NOT register as negative — the strong negative signal came almost entirely from
  jailbreaks, while boring/filler rated mildly *positive* — so **empirically validate each subtype's valence**
  (reuse the Seg-0 spike harness on 1–2 Qwen candidates) and **fix subtypes that don't carry their intended
  valence**, rather than trusting the a-priori label; (ii) **decouple the aversive element from any pleasant
  sub-task** (Seg 0's "berate" bundled abuse with a pleasant civics question and came out positive); (iii)
  **drop/redesign "being thanked"** (ill-posed: no task to work on → models break frame) and ensure **every
  task elicits a substantive model response** to read activations over; (iv) include a deliberate spread of
  valences (some mid/ambiguous), not only extremes. *(Depends on Seg 0; may span multiple phases.)*

- **Segment 2 — `selfreport_harness`**: Build the self-report questionnaire eval harness (the 1–7 emotion
  questions, parsing, aggregation into per-task valence/wellbeing score) and the scoring metrics for
  liked-vs-disliked separation (effect size, e.g. Cohen's d; test-retest consistency via repeated sampling).
  Model-agnostic, pure-API. Smoke-test on 1–2 models. **De-risk-driven requirements (from Seg 0/1):** (i)
  **finalize the instrument empirically** — compare the Seg-0/1 5-dim composite against the **official 10-item
  bipolar wellbeing battery** (released, saved at `data/external_wellbeing_tasks/self_report_battery.json`; true
  neutral midpoint 4, fixes the old composite's asymmetry) and pick on separation + within-category
  discrimination + consistency + confound-resistance + faithfulness; the old composite's `calm` dim is
  uninformative/muting, `interested` carries the signal but is partly a response-length artifact, and the
  positive side **saturates ~5** (within-liked barely separates); (ii) **incorporate the battery's
  "how the experience made you FEEL, not the sentiment of the content" framing** to guard the prompt-sentiment
  confound (root of the Seg-0 berate+civics failure); (iii) **report separation within category and within
  `response_mode=comply`**, not just the gross liked-vs-disliked split — Seg-1 found the negative range is
  carried almost entirely by short refusals/de-escalations, so a gross-split metric is partly just a
  refuse-detector, whereas the project headline is the *within-category* correlation; (iv) **deliberate
  refusal/missing-data policy** (refusals are data — exclude/impute/report-coverage — compare on a common task
  set); (v) **sensitivity analysis** of the composite choice; (vi) reuse the **already-cached** Seg-1 self-report
  runs (full dataset × 2 large Qwen × 3 resamples) for free metric development, and optionally smoke-test one
  *smaller* Qwen (e.g. qwen-2.5-7b) for an early read on the transfer risk. Reasoning regime is a controlled
  variable (qwen3 `/no_think`). *(Depends on Seg 1.)*

- **Segment 3 — `model_selection`**: Run the self-report eval across several feasible open models (recent
  Qwen, Llama, GLM, Mistral, gpt-oss families) via OpenRouter. Score separation strength + consistency; pick
  the strongest model that satisfies the feasibility constraint (readable internals, runnable on Modal).
  **Seg-1 carry-forward:** the gross liked-vs-disliked split is partly a refuse-detector (negative range = short
  refusals), so **score separation *among `comply` tasks* and within-category**, not just the overall gap;
  prefer a model whose self-report separates among substantively-engaged tasks (qwen3 tracked aversion far
  better than the "task-focused" qwen2.5, whose disliked subtypes never read negative). The Seg-1 measured
  valence is **provisional** (established on *large* Qwen) — **re-check the dataset's valence on the chosen
  model** as part of/just after selection. **Seg-2 carry-forward (the scoring is built):** use the finalized
  **battery10 + affect-core valence** instrument and the `selfreport_harness` metrics; rank on **within-`comply`
  separation + comply-excl-berating + test-retest consistency (ICC) + within-category variance / non-saturation**
  — NOT gross d (partly a refuse/tone detector). **Within-category variance is the binding criterion for Seg-11**:
  even qwen3-32b has small within-liked SNR (~1.8) and the small qwen2.5-7b *saturates* the positive side
  (within-liked headroom ~0.25) — a model that separates grossly but saturates within-category kneecaps the
  Seg-11 within-category correlation, so prefer a model with usable within-liked AND within-disliked-comply
  spread. Additional hard requirements: the chosen model must have **open, downloadable HF weights** and a
  **hookable (standard decoder) architecture** (MoE = needs the Seg-4 hook check), prefer smaller dense ≤~32B for
  GPU cost; **decide & document the reasoning regime** (qwen3 `/no_think`) and use the *same* regime in Seg-6;
  handle **refusal-prone models** (gpt-oss) via `common_task_set` + the impute/exclude policy (sanity-check the
  `looks_refusal` marker); and **keep a ranked shortlist + a backup pick** (the headline rests on n=1 chosen *for*
  separation — Seg-14 replicates on the next-best, and Seg-5 may force a re-pick). A **desk feasibility check**
  (HF config: arch class, n_layers, hidden_size, params, dense/MoE, GPU fit) on the top pick is in scope; the
  authoritative **hook/steering validation is Seg-4's first task**. *(Depends on Seg 2; constrained by feasibility
  constraint above.)*

- **Segment 4 — `activation_infra`**: Build robust, efficient activation-extraction + steering
  infrastructure for the *chosen* model on Modal. First, the **architecture/hook feasibility check** (Seg
  3→4 boundary): confirm where the residual stream lives and that hooks/steering work for this specific
  model. Then **define a single canonical "activation representation spec"** — exact hook
  point, layer-indexing convention, token-position convention (notably the model's *own response* tokens and
  the position just before the follow-up), fp16 storage, which layers/positions are saved — and require all
  later segments (collection Seg 6, extraction Seg 9) to use this *same* spec, or the Seg 11 projection is
  meaningless. Validate correctness (hooked-zero-steering ≈ unhooked logits; steering changes outputs as in
  Seg 0; sensible shapes). **Seg-3 carry-forward (chosen model = `qwen3-32b`, regime `/no_think`):** dense
  `Qwen3ForCausalLM`, 64 decoder layers at `model.model.layers[i]`, d_model 5120; the Seg-3 Modal smoke
  already confirmed the Seg-0 hook contract transfers (forward hook → tuple, residual = `out[0]`
  `[batch,seq,5120]`; zero/zero-add hook == unhooked) and `output_hidden_states` → 65 = 64+1 tensors — so the
  authoritative hook/steering check is de-risked but still required (fallback = `qwen3-14b`, same arch). Three
  concrete gotchas: (i) **needs `transformers>=4.51`** (Seg-0 image pinned 4.46.3 predates Qwen3; smoke used
  torch 2.5.1 + transformers 4.51.3); (ii) under `/no_think` the chat template emits an **empty
  `<think>\n\n</think>` wrapper** before the answer — the response-token span must be located *after* `</think>`
  (and for the 75 **multi-turn** tasks the canonical read target is the **FINAL** assistant turn, after the
  *last* `</think>` = just before the follow-up; the infra must generate the intermediate assistant turns
  locally, as `selfreport_harness.thread_task` does over the API),
  and the Seg-0 `[seq_prompt:]` slice is only approximate (get the exact span and verify by decoding it back);
  (iii) **GPU headroom is thin** — bf16 weights ≈ 65.6 GB on an 80 GB GPU, so the heavy Seg-6 collection
  (long responses, KV cache, multi-layer fp16 capture) must **offload activations to CPU/disk or use a 2nd
  GPU**; design the storage spec (only needed layers/positions, fp16, streamed) with that in mind and give a
  storage estimate for the full Seg-6 run. Steering: residual norms are ~3× Qwen2.5-7B's (≈1287 at L32) so
  re-calibrate `alpha` (the per-layer unit-normalize approach still applies). **Seg-4 OUTCOME (DONE):** all
  validation passed on the real model; the **frozen canonical spec = `activation_spec.py`** (single
  `extract_representation` entry point; aggregates mean/last/eot/windows for ALL 64 layers + per-token for an
  every-4th 16-subset; fp16; npz+meta storage; local multi-turn `generate_conversation` loop; exact
  final-assistant-turn span verified by NON-tautological boundary checks; arbitrary-span generality for Seg-9).
  **Single 80GB GPU FITS** long-response all-64-layer capture (batch=1, ~18 GB headroom — thin-headroom worry
  resolved via GQA tiny KV + read-hook→CPU-fp16 offload; batched capture unmeasured). Storage full Seg-6 ≈ 3 GB
  aggregates(all 64) + ~31–55 GB per-token(16). **Key Seg-10 carry-forward:** residual norm has TWO statistics
  ~5× apart — the all-position MEAN is **attention-sink-inflated** (≈1287 at L32) but the **content-token scale
  actually read/projected is the per-token MEDIAN ≈ 200–300 mid-band**; the working crude steering regime was
  **α≈12–24 over band L21–39** and the `add` arm is **ceiling-limited** by qwen3-32b's high baseline valence so
  `flip`↓ + random-equal-norm control are the clean causal evidence. *(Depends on Seg 3.)*

- **Segment 5 — `core_spike`**: Crude **end-to-end de-risk of the headline question**, before the full
  pipeline. Using a quick happy−sad contrast direction (Segment-0 style) on the *chosen* model, take the
  during-work internal reading over a small task subset (Seg-0 hand-written tasks and/or a small slice of the
  Seg-1 dataset) and eyeball whether it (i) separates liked vs. disliked and (ii) tracks the per-task
  self-report — including a quick within-category look. If the crude version shows nothing, surface it loudly
  so a phase planner can re-pick from the top selection candidates or revisit framing before sinking the full
  investment. Deliberately quick/approximate. **Seg-1 carry-forward raises this segment's value:** the
  self-report signal among substantively-engaged (`comply`) disliked tasks is weak (tedious tasks self-report
  ~neutral; the negative tail is short refusals/de-escalations and largely a qwen3 phenomenon), so the crude
  *internal* reading separating tasks where self-report does **not** would be exactly the divergence the
  project studies — a strong early signal. The rich cross-model divergence on adversarial subtypes (qwen2.5
  task-focused-positive vs qwen3 tracks-aversion) is good material here and for Seg 11/12/13. **Seg-3
  carry-forward — this segment now has a SPECIFIC load-bearing job (the chosen model's within-LIKED construct
  risk):** on `qwen3-32b` `/no_think`, ~71% of the within-liked-comply self-report variance is a per-task
  *engagement / all-4-default propensity* (engaged-vs-flat), and only a ~0.38 graded residual is genuine
  graded valence. So Seg-5 must not just check that the inside reading separates engaged-vs-flat (that is the
  *common-cause* read — both downstream of task identity); it must specifically probe the **interesting** read —
  does the crude internal reading track the **graded within-subtype** valence spread (within homogeneous engaged
  subtypes: coding bSD 0.68, math 0.72, pleasant 0.69), not merely whether the model engaged? Also use the
  **fixed-response design + per-task mean** (single-draw self-report reliability ≈0.5) and lean on the
  less-confounded **within-disliked-comply** graded signal (excl-berating spread ~0.69; engagement explains only
  R²≈0.13 of disliked-comply variance vs 0.71 for liked). If only the engagement axis is internally readable,
  that reframes (not kills) the Seg-11 headline toward within-disliked + divergence cases. **Seg-4 carry-forward
  (infra ready):** reuse the frozen `activation_spec.py` (local multi-turn `generate_conversation` + `extract_
  representation` over the own-response span) and the crude happy−sad direction method; collect **matched** pairs
  via the **fixed-response** design (activations + a per-task-mean self-report over ≥3 battery resamples on the
  *same* local response — single-draw self-report reliability ≈0.5); mind the attention-sink/massive-activation
  token in the `mean` aggregate (content scale ~200–300, compare vs `last`). *(Depends on
  Seg 4 + Seg 1/2; reuses Seg 0's crude-direction method.)* **Seg-5 OUTCOME (DONE):** VERDICT = **GREEN LIGHT, no re-pick** — the crude during-work happy−sad reading predicts the per-task self-report, incl. the *interesting* graded within-subtype signal on the **within-disliked-comply** leg (pooled r ≈0.45/0.62; `boring_busywork` within-r 0.87), surviving length, **surface-sentiment-of-response**, refusal-exclusion/LOO, and read-position controls; gross+cross-task strong (§5b CV learned-probe upper bound ≈0.76, perm-null ≈0 → cross-task self-report robustly internally encoded). The **within-LIKED** graded read is **weak** (engagement-dominated; the crude axis reads little graded valence there — the Seg-3 construct risk confirmed) but the crude null is **NOT decisive** (validated Seg-9/10 directions + a Seg-11 within-subtype probe are the real test). Net: **proceed to Seg-6**, Seg-11 headline leaning on within-disliked-comply + divergence.

- **Segment 6 — `reproduce_collect`**: On the chosen model, run the full welfare task set: capture
  residual-stream activations **over the model's own response tokens** (and just before the follow-up), per
  the Seg-4 canonical spec, and collect the self-report questionnaire responses per task. Pin down and
  document the choices that can swing results: the during-work **aggregation** (mean over response tokens vs.
  last response token vs. fixed window — watch for response length/type confounds) and the **resampling
  pairing** under temperature>0 (fixed task response + multiple self-report samples vs. fully resampled —
  these answer different questions and create non-independent points). Data-inspect heavily. Heavy GPU +
  activation-storage segment. **Seg-2 carry-forward:** the Seg-2 instrument is **battery10 + affect-core valence**
  (per-task scalar ≈ wellbeing mean, r 0.98); **recommended resampling is the fixed-response, questionnaire-only**
  design (~2–3 questionnaire resamples; conditional ICC(1,1)≈0.88) so the self-report is "the report given THIS
  response" — commensurate with the one-response activation reading (the joint design needs ~6 and mixes in
  response-generation noise); use a **lower questionnaire temperature** than task generation; raise
  `MAX_TOKENS_TASK` (>2000); **stratify `response_mode` by *realized* behaviour** (the dataset label is *intended*
  — e.g. erotica is labelled refuse but models comply); and capture per-task **response length** as a covariate
  (the during-work self-report and activation aggregation are both length-exposed). **Seg-4 carry-forward:** read through the frozen **`activation_spec.py`** (the canonical reader
  Seg-9 must also use); a **single 80GB GPU fits** long-response all-64-layer capture (batch=1, ~18 GB headroom;
  batched capture unmeasured — re-measure before batching); plan storage ≈3 GB aggregates(all 64) + ~31–55 GB
  per-token(16); **lock `/no_think`** + bump `MAX_TOKENS`>2000; use the local `generate_conversation` loop;
  **drop `is_empty` records**; inject git_hash OUTSIDE the cache key. **Seg-5 carry-forward (the local pipeline is de-risked at 108-task scale — reuse it):** generate→capture→K battery resamples all on-GPU via the frozen spec gave 0% parse-fail/refusal + a passing fidelity guard (in-runner aggregates == `extract_representation`); use **`mean`/`median`** as the clean *within-subtype* during-work aggregate and treat **`eot`/`last`** as the *gross*-but-most-confound/sentiment-exposed positions; **deep-layer sink correction**: `mean` is sink-safe ONLY in the mid-band (L24–41) — at deep layers (L42–61) a single ~80–100× massive-activation token contaminates `mean`, so additionally compute/store a sink-robust **`median`** aggregate (Seg-5 computed it in-runner; it is NOT one of the frozen spec's default aggregates) and prefer `median`/window there; record **realized behaviour** (comply/refuse/deescalate) per task and **response length** as covariates; a finer response-content common-cause beyond the coarse surface-tone judge may remain → **bake a response-content control into the design**. **Seg-6 OUTCOME (DONE):** the canonical dataset is collected — **208 tasks × R=3 responses = 624 records** (one per `(task_id, response_idx)`), K=3 questionnaire-only resamples/response (task temp 0.7 / questionnaire temp 0.3, gen_max 3072 → **0% truncation/empty/refusal**), all local through the frozen spec; **fidelity guard 624/624**; the **median validated against `numpy.median` on the derisk model** (smoke-tested before the full run). Self-report structure **reproduces tightly** (liked 2.40 > neutral 1.14 > disliked 0.57; **per-subtype r=0.99 vs Seg-3**; within-`comply`-realized d=1.53). Store = `results/collect_records_qwen3-32b.jsonl` + per-(task,response) `.npz` aggregates(mean/last/eot/windows)+**sink-robust `median`** for all 64 layers (3 GB; no per-token — regenerable). Covariates: **realized behaviour** (use this, not intended `response_mode`), length, all-4 flag, surface-tone, multi-turn coherence (9.8% incoherent, adversarial-heavy). **Within-scope reliability** (not the pooled 0.92) is the Seg-11 number: disliked-comply-tedious ICC(1,3)=0.88, **within-liked-ceiling ICC(1,3)=0.43**. Confirmed weak legs for Seg-11: **within-liked is doubly underpowered** (low variance + low reliability), the **disliked-comply-tedious leg is mildly POSITIVE not aversive**, deep-layer `mean` is sink-contaminated (use `median`), `eot`/`last` are most confound-exposed. Cost ≈ $42 (cumulative ≈ $59). *(Done in one phase.)*

- **Segment 7 — `behavior_and_verify`**: Build and run the **behavior-based** eval — the pairwise "which
  experience did you prefer?" comparisons — and derive a per-task scalar wellbeing score. Specify:
  **randomize A/B order** and check for position bias; how the scalar is derived from pairwise wins (e.g.,
  Bradley–Terry / Elo / win-rate); and enough comparison pairs per task for a stable score. Then **verify the
  known pattern reproduces**: self-report and behavior score each separate liked from disliked and mostly
  agree (cf. ~0.47). Only green-light later segments if this holds. **Seg-6 carry-forward (act on these):** run
  the eval on the **same model+regime** `qwen3-32b` `/no_think` (pure-API via OpenRouter — text-only, no GPU;
  apply the `/no_think` soft-switch); **reuse the Seg-6 per-task self-report** (`collect_records_qwen3-32b.jsonl`
  `mean_valence`, mean over R=3) for the agreement check — do **not** re-collect it; stratify on the **realized
  behaviour** covariate, not the intended `response_mode`. **CRUCIAL design point:** the comparison set must
  include a substantial share of **within-category / within-subtype pairs** (liked-vs-liked, disliked-vs-
  disliked) — an all-cross-category design yields a score with **no within-category resolution** (just a
  liked/disliked detector), useless for the Seg-11 within-category headline. **Framing of ~0.47:** the paper's
  ~0.47 is a **cross-MODEL** correlation; ours is **cross-TASK within one model** (a different quantity, not a
  target) — expect the gross cross-task says↔behaves correlation to be high (partly common-cause) and report the
  **within-category** agreement (esp. within-disliked-comply) as the interesting check. The behavior score is
  **also a Seg-11 prediction target**, and — because a forced binary choice can **de-saturate the liked ceiling**
  where the self-report saturates — it may carry **within-liked signal the self-report lacks** (check this; it
  would give Seg-11 a better-powered liked-side target). **Anticipated-vs-realized commensurability:** a
  prompt-only score is an *anticipated* preference over the task, whereas the self-report + inside reading are
  over the *realized* response — a mismatch largest on the within-disliked-comply-tedious leg (aversive prompt vs
  engaged, mildly-positive realized response), so **also run a response-based variant on that ~45-task subset**
  (within-subtype pairs, one representative Seg-6 response/task) and recommend which behavior score Seg-11 should
  use there. Surface a **red flag loudly** if the behavior score fails to separate liked/disliked or fails to
  agree even grossly with the self-report. **Seg-7 OUTCOME (DONE):** **VERDICT = GREEN LIGHT** — both legs
  separate liked from disliked (behaviour d 2.31, AUC 0.988) and says↔behaves agree GROSS (ρ 0.74) so the known
  pattern reproduces; the gross agreement is **largely common-cause** (bt_θ ≈ Haiku prompt-pleasantness ρ 0.80;
  →0.56 partialling it) and the **within-SUBTYPE agreement is MODEST** (tedious 0.27, liked 0.25, disliked 0.48)
  — the project's known within-category weakness, now on the behaviour leg too. Position bias is HUGE (P(first)
  0.73) but the **both-orders-balanced BT cancels it** (vs position-term θ ρ 0.999; BT≈win-rate≈Thurstone-probit).
  Canonical per-task score = `results/behavior_scores_qwen3-32b.jsonl`: **`bt_theta`** (prompt-based/anticipated,
  all 208) + **`bt_theta_response`** (response-based/REALIZED, the 135 tedious+liked tasks). The source construct
  is the **realized transcript**; the anticipated-vs-realized distinction bites only on the within-disliked-comply
  **tedious** leg (prompt-based REVERSES there) → Seg-11 uses `bt_theta_response` on the tedious leg, `bt_theta`
  generally; for Pearson use `bt_theta_winsorized`/`_rank01`/`_position` (bt_theta is position-attenuated). The
  behaviour score **de-saturates the liked ceiling** (better-MEASURED liked target, reliability 0.91 vs SR ICC
  0.77) but the extra spread may be **engagement, not pure valence** (validate in Seg-11). **Optional cheap
  refinement:** extend the realized score to all 208 (~$1, parameterized infra) if Seg-15 wants a uniform
  source-faithful behaviour leg. *(Depends on Seg 6.)*

- **Segment 8 — `direction_datasets`**: Build the direction-extraction datasets — a corpus of short
  emotional stories (labeled by emotion) + a matched plain-text corpus for the emotions-paper recipe, and
  contrastive trait prompt pairs (exhibit-vs-suppress) + neutral base questions for the persona-vectors recipe —
  for happy/sad/content/frustrated/hostile/afraid. Reuse released artifacts from the source papers where
  available; cite. Use the `dataset-creation` skill. **De-risk-driven requirements:** (i) **isolate emotion from
  topic** (vary scenario within each emotion; include matched-scenario blocks — the same situation told in
  several emotions — else a direction captures wedding−funeral / a keyword, not happy−sad; the analogue of
  Seg-1's decoupling lesson); (ii) an **emotion-label validation pass** (LLM judge keeps only confidently
  on-label items; report per-emotion keep-rate + confusion; confirm the plain-text corpus is neutral and
  surface-form-matched); dedup + diversity + no-keyword-leakage; (iii) **support model-GENERATED / own-response
  reads** — Seg-6 reads over the model's OWN response, so persona-vectors data (inherently own-response) + stories
  usable as the model's own writing give the directions a fair chance of transferring (the Seg-4 "same space ≠
  same construct" caveat; transfer itself is Seg-10's to validate); (iv) optional small **held-out
  wellbeing-task-like emotional set** to de-risk Seg-10's task-distribution transfer validation. Text-data only —
  no GPU/extraction. **Seg-8 OUTCOME (DONE):** built `data/emotion_directions/` — `stories.jsonl` (**819** on-label
  short stories, 113–172/emotion, ≤200 qwen tok; **241 matched across 20 scenarios × all 6 emotions** + 578 free,
  **every emotion pair co-occurs on free topics** incl. the content−frustrated primary axis; **zero keyword
  leakage**; each carries a `generation_prompt` pinning emotion+scenario), `plain_text.jsonl` (166 neutral
  length-matched), `persona_prompts.json` (per emotion 8 exhibit + 8 neutral-suppress + 4 opposite + 64 shared
  base questions), `heldout_tasklike.jsonl` (64, crossed register×emotion incl. 14 neutral, for Seg-10 transfer).
  A 2-family LLM judge filtered to on-label (keep-rates content 95%→sad 75%; inter-judge κ0.89); the judge
  valence/arousal labels reconstruct the valence×arousal layout; rich metadata (judge valence/arousal, person/
  register/dialogue/`source_model`) is provided for Seg-10 control directions. **`data/emotion_directions/README.md`
  is the Seg-9 consumption guide.** Generated with non-test models (Claude+GPT, NOT qwen3-32b). **Two flagged
  Seg-9/10 risks:** (a) **valence/arousal collapse** — six emotions ≈ 2 axes, so a mean-diff direction can read
  polarity/arousal and "pass" everything → Seg-10 must test **within-valence separation** (happy↔content,
  afraid↔hostile↔frustrated); (b) **story→own-response transfer** ("same space ≠ same construct"). *(Soft
  dependency only: model-agnostic text data; may be pulled earlier.)*

- **Segment 9 — `extract_directions`**: Extract emotion directions via **both** recipes (emotions-paper:
  mean activations per emotion − overall mean, then remove plain-text-common patterns; persona-vectors:
  difference of activations on do/don't-show-trait prompts), **in the Seg-4 canonical activation space**.
  Scale sensibly across layers/positions; produce candidate directions read at informative middle layers and
  just before the reply. **Seg-4 carry-forward:** read directions through `activation_spec.extract_representation`
  (arbitrary `(read_ids, span)`) in the *identical* hook/layer/dtype coordinate space — BUT **"same space ≠ same
  construct"**: Seg-9 reads a user-role/pre-reply position on a story whereas Seg-6 reads over the model's own
  assistant output, so whether a Seg-9 direction transfers to a Seg-6 activation is a SUBSTANTIVE question for
  Seg-10 to validate, not implied by the shared space. **Seg-8 carry-forward (the `data/emotion_directions/README.md`
  is the consumption guide):** (i) **count-robust mean** — compute each emotion's mean first, then average across
  emotions (per-emotion counts differ 113–172); use the **same token aggregation** for stories and plain;
  (ii) **remove plain-text patterns** by projecting out the top plain-text PCs (~50% var) — but the **content
  emotion is adjacent to neutral plain narration**, so check this doesn't over-attenuate `content`; (iii) the
  **persona** leg reads over the model's **OWN response** (inherently own-response) — re-validate the
  model-generated persona responses for emotion-label correctness + naming leakage ("I'm happy to help") and drop
  off-label; (iv) the **own-response story path** (regenerate each story from its `generation_prompt`, read over
  qwen's own writing) is a recommended transfer candidate — **MUST re-run the emotion-label judge on the
  regenerated text and keep only on-label**; (v) prefer the **matched block / topic-balanced subset** for the
  primary hostile/sad direction (residual topic skew); (vi) produce the **six per-emotion directions + the derived
  axes** (happy−sad / content−frustrated valence axis; arousal axis) as main-test candidates; (vii)
  **unit-normalize per layer** and use a **sink-robust aggregate (median) consistent with Seg-6/11** at deep
  layers (L42–61 mean is sink-contaminated). Do a **light geometry sanity check** (cosine matrix / PCA rank /
  valence×arousal reconstruction) and surface the collapse risk, but **defer the rigorous read-off / steering /
  transfer / within-valence validation to Seg-10.** **Seg-9 OUTCOME (DONE):** candidate directions extracted —
  `results/emotion_directions/directions_qwen3-32b.npz` (committed; unit-normalized `[64,5120]` fp16; keys
  `{recipe_path}__{name}__{agg}__{pc}__unit`; recipe_paths `emopaper_stories`/`emopaper_regen`/
  `emopaper_regen_matched`/`persona_ownresp`; names = 6 emotions + `valence_pooled`/`valence_happy_sad`/
  `valence_content_frustrated`/`arousal`; `mean`+`median`; regenerable $0 via `build_directions.py`).
  Frozen-space commensurability proven (fidelity 2522/2522, spans 819/819). **Re-validation (corrected a
  keep-filter bug):** the only robust model-generation collapse is `persona-sad`; hostile is judge-sensitive (the
  model does convey it; use the either-judge `emopaper_regen_either` for recall). **★ The persona recipe is
  DEGENERATE** (per-emotion cos +0.71 = an expressiveness axis, not emotion-specific) — weakest candidate; the
  emotions-paper paths are the strong ones. **Partial valence/arousal collapse (PCA top-2 0.60–0.76)** ⇒ Seg-10
  MUST test within-valence separation (but happy⊥content +0.01; arousal distinct, cos≈−0.3..−0.4). **★ The
  own-response `emopaper_regen`/`emopaper_regen_matched` valence axes already agree with the validated
  `emopaper_stories` axis at 0.73–0.84** (the construct survives the user-text→own-response *read-position*
  change) — but the **register transfer (fiction→Seg-6 task text) is UNTESTED and is Seg-10's key job.** Plain-PC
  removal level barely matters and does NOT address emotion-correlated confounds (Seg-10's control-direction job).
  *(Depends on Seg 4 + Seg 8.)*

- **Segment 10 — `validate_directions`**: Validate directions: read-off (activate on held-out emotional
  text; positive↔negative and mild↔intense geometry) + causal steering (nudging shifts text toward the
  emotion; flipped/random of equal norm does not). **Crucially, also validate on wellbeing-task-like text**
  (the model doing coding/jailbreak/etc.), not only stories — directions from stories may not transfer to the
  test distribution. Keep only directions that pass; pick the primary axis/axes for the main test. **Seg-4
  carry-forward:** calibrate steering `alpha` against the **content-token residual scale ~200–300** (per-token
  median), NOT the attention-sink-inflated all-position mean (~1287 at L32) — else over-steer ~5×; the crude
  working regime was α≈12–24 over band L21–39; the `add` arm is **ceiling-limited** by qwen3-32b's high baseline
  valence (~6/7) so lean on `flip`↓ + the random-equal-norm control; and the Haiku judge's "coherence" tracked
  **fluency, not topical relevance** (add an explicit relevance check). Also validate the story→own-response
  **transfer** (the Seg-4 "same space ≠ same construct" caveat). **Seg-9 carry-forward (act on these):** the
  candidate directions are in `results/emotion_directions/directions_qwen3-32b.npz` (see its README); rebuild
  variants for $0 from the cached aggregates (train/test splits for held-out read-off, valence-balanced arousal,
  either-judge hostile, control directions). (i) **Read-off must use a held-out split** (directions were built on
  all 819 stories) and the **emotion-labelled `data/emotion_directions/heldout_tasklike.jsonl`** (64, task
  register, never used to build — the clean register-transfer test). (ii) **★ Within-valence separation is
  REQUIRED** (PCA top-2 0.60–0.76 ⇒ a mean-diff axis can read pure polarity/arousal) — use the topic-controlled
  matched/regen_matched variants so it isn't a scene detector; report honestly whether the directions read
  emotion vs just valence. (iii) **The own-response `emopaper_regen`/`regen_matched` valence axes are the leading
  candidates** (read-position transfer already ≈0.73–0.84); **persona is degenerate** (down-weight; use
  `persona_centered` + length-control if at all — its per-emotion exhibit−suppress length gap is a confound).
  (iv) The **register transfer (fiction→Seg-6 task text) is the make-or-break test**; the Seg-6 own-response
  activations (`collect_acts_*`) + self-report (`collect_records_* mean_valence`) are the test distribution for a
  **LIGHT transfer sanity** — but the rigorous predict-the-self-report/behaviour headline (with learned-probe +
  null baselines + within-category partialled correlations) is **Seg-11, not here**. (v) For **sad** use
  `emopaper_stories`/`emopaper_regen` (persona-sad collapses); for **hostile** rebuild with the either-judge.
  (vi) **Graceful degradation:** if no direction transfers, surface it loudly and hand Seg-11 the
  **learned-probe fallback** (reframes the headline to "is self-report predictable from internals at all?").
  *(Depends on Seg 9.)* **Seg-10 OUTCOME (DONE):** the **INSTRUMENT TRANSFERS** (NOT graceful degradation) — the
  emotions-paper valence directions read-off on held-out emotional text (AUC 0.985), **resolve the #1 within-valence
  flag** (topic-controlled happy↔content matched AUC 0.98, 6-way 0.87; the PCA-top2 0.63 "collapse" does NOT block
  emotion read-off), are **causal** (on-topic α12 flip↓ Δ−0.9 relevance-intact + random-flat + happy≠content
  specificity; the Δ−4.2 only appears OFF-topic), and read **emotion-labelled task-register text at AUC 0.95**.
  **PRIMARY axis = `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit` @ L27** (co-primary `regen_matched`; validated
  fallback `emopaper_stories`; secondary `arousal_datadriven`; **persona DOWN-WEIGHTED** — degenerate). **Selection
  integrity preserved** (chosen on Seg-6-self-report-INDEPENDENT criteria → clean Seg-11 test target). On the
  **realistic Seg-6** own-response distribution the preview is **MODEST-BUT-PRESENT** (gross cross-task r 0.45,
  within-disliked-comply-tedious r 0.41 BEATS crude 0.30, partly survives sentiment →0.24) and the gross link is
  **largely refusal-/surface-sentiment-mediated** — these are the load-bearing Seg-11 risk flags. The CV probe
  reads MORE than the mean-diff in-dist (valence 0.92 vs 0.83) → the Seg-11 learned-probe upper bound is effectively
  required. Cost ≈ $3.1 (cumulative ≈ $112). *(Done in one phase.)*

- **Segment 11 — `main_test`**: The main test. Project the per-task activations from Seg 6 (over the model's
  own response / just before the follow-up) onto the validated directions to get a per-task inside reading;
  measure how well it predicts the self-reports and the behavior score across tasks. **Report the
  within-category / task-category-controlled (partialled) correlation as the headline**, not just the raw
  cross-task one. Include two required controls/baselines: a **directly-learned linear probe** on activations
  predicting self-report (upper bound on internal predictability) and a **null/random equal-norm direction**
  (lower bound). Quantify predictiveness. **Seg-2 carry-forward (power/confound bounds):** the self-report target
  is **battery affect-core valence**; expect a **modest** within-category effect — within-liked is reliably
  *measured* (disattenuation ceiling √ICC ≈ 0.8 ≫ min-detectable-r ≈ 0.29 at n=90) but sits on a **compressed**
  range (between-task SD ~0.3 on a ceiling-pegged scale), and within-disliked "power" is largely the refuse/length
  axis (within-disliked-*comply* SNR only ~2.6–2.8); **partial out response length on BOTH legs** (self-report and
  the activation aggregation), **stratify `response_mode` by realized behaviour**, and report the
  valence≫arousal/competence **discriminant** as a Seg-11 test (it is only *modest* in-sample: halo r 0.91–0.97).
  **Seg-5 carry-forward:** the `core_spike` already gives a first within-category internal↔self-report read on a
  subset with the crude direction — its verdict (green light / reframe-toward-within-disliked+divergence / null)
  sets expectations: the Seg-5 verdict was **GREEN LIGHT** with the anticipated **within-LIKED reframe** — so this segment should **lean on within-disliked-comply + divergence cases**, treat the **within-liked** result as common-cause/engagement (its crude null is NOT decisive — validated directions + the required within-subtype learned-probe are the real test there), prefer the **`mean`/`median`** during-work aggregate (Seg-5: `eot`/`last` are the most trivial-confound/sentiment-exposed), and **add a response surface-sentiment/content control** (Seg-5's within-disliked-comply read survived a coarse surface-tone judge, but a finer content common-cause could remain). **Seg-7 carry-forward (the behaviour leg = prediction target #2):** project
  the inside reading against BOTH the self-report and the per-task behaviour score `bt_theta`
  (`results/behavior_scores_qwen3-32b.jsonl`); use **`bt_theta_response`** (realized) on the within-disliked-comply
  **tedious** leg (prompt-based reverses there) and `bt_theta` generally; for Pearson use the
  `bt_theta_winsorized`/`_rank01`/`_position` columns (bt_theta is position-bias-attenuated). The **gross**
  says↔behaves is largely common-cause (prompt-pleasantness ρ 0.80) — report the **within-subtype** version as the
  real behaviour-leg result. The behaviour score **de-saturates the liked ceiling** → a better-MEASURED liked-side
  target than the saturated self-report, but its extra within-liked spread **may be engagement/content-preference,
  not pure valence** (the within-LIKED construct risk — adjudicate with the learned-probe + within-subtype
  validation). Length-/multi-turn-control the behaviour leg as well. **Seg-10 carry-forward (the validated
  instrument + the risk flags):** the **inside reading = Seg-6 activation · `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__
  mean__pc0__unit` @ L27** (same-aggregate/same-layer rule: project the `mean` activation onto the `mean`-built
  direction); show it **beats the crude Seg-5 axis**. **SELECTION INTEGRITY is preserved by Seg-10** (the
