## Key choices (judgment) this segment

- **Restoring the full prior state vs working from the partial copy:** INSTRUCTIONS.md says to start from the
  full prior-phase state, and the consolidation requires the writeups + the qwen2.5 tensors; the partial copy
  was a setup glitch, so I synced the full state (verified common files byte-identical first). Documented.
- **Behaviour leg = realized `bt_theta_response` on the tedious+liked legs only** for the cross-model says↔behaves
  comparison: both models have it for all 45 tedious + 90 liked tasks (the within-disliked / pooled legs mix
  realized+anticipated and are adversarial-confounded), so this is the clean apples-to-apples comparison.
- **Re-deriving rather than trusting the write-ups:** every load-bearing cell was recomputed from the committed
  per-task files and the raw tensors and asserted to match the committed JSONs — so the synthesis numbers are
  verified, not inherited.
- **Calibration discipline:** the headline carries every hedge (within-category / modest / surface-entangled /
  borderline / partial-replication / two-models-only); the figures are labelled so axes/captions don't
  over-state (gross = "common-cause", headline = "within-category, modest"; the ladder distinguishes gross from
  within-category scope).

## C. inside↔behaves WITHIN-category (the says-but-not-behaves cross-model DIFFERENCE)

Behaviour score = the COMMENSURATE realized `bt_theta_response` (read over the model's actual response), which both models have for ALL tedious (n=45) + liked (n=90) tasks — the clean, apples-to-apples legs (the within-disliked / pooled legs mix realized + anticipated scores and the adversarial subtypes confound them, so they are omitted here).

| leg | model | n | inside↔behaves raw r | +all3 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | 45 | +0.209 | -0.091 |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | 45 | +0.494 | +0.328 |
| within_liked | qwen3-32b | 90 | +0.318 | +0.216 |
| within_liked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | +0.444 | +0.332 |

*★ The dissociation is **within-TEDIOUS-specific**: qwen3 inside↔behaves within-tedious ≈ NULL (+0.21 raw → −0.09 under confounds, n.s.) — a genuine says-but-not-behaves dissociation — but qwen3 inside↔behaves within-LIKED is POSITIVE (+0.32 → +0.22, actually larger than its within-liked says↔inside), so this is NOT a blanket within-category qwen3 property. qwen2.5 predicts behaviour within-tedious POSITIVELY (+0.49 → +0.33) — predicts BOTH says AND behaves. So the cross-model difference is on the tedious leg (n=45). The content increment beyond the strongest control on the qwen2.5 behaviour leg is +0.18 (Seg-14) — positive but, like the qwen2.5 self-report direction increment, NOT independently null-cleared; the ROBUST finding is the cross-model difference under the standard confound stack. Behaviour = realized `bt_theta_response`; NB the committed `main_test_summary_qwen2.5-72b.md` inside↔behaves cell (+0.29) uses the ANTICIPATED `bt_theta` — the commensurate realized score is +0.49 (matching `write_up_second_model_replication.md §7`); likewise the qwen3 liked cell here (+0.32, realized) vs the Seg-11 three-way's +0.21 (anticipated).*

## C2. says↔behaves (the third three-way leg) — gross + within-category

Self-report vs behaviour (realized `bt_theta_response` where available — all tedious+liked tasks; the GROSS additionally covers neutral/adversarial via the anticipated `bt_theta`). The GROSS says↔behaves is high but largely common-cause (Seg-7); the within-subtype is modest.

| model | gross r | gross ρ | within-tedious | within-liked |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| qwen3-32b | +0.618 | +0.556 | +0.393 | +0.423 |
| qwen2.5-72b | +0.764 | +0.554 | +0.489 | +0.251 |

## D. Validated-beats-crude (raw within-r, matched pre-registered cell)

| leg | model | validated | crude | fallback(stories) | arousal-axis |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | +0.459 | +0.308 | +0.303 | -0.015 |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | +0.327 | +0.286 | +0.215 | +0.007 |
| within_disliked | qwen3-32b | +0.497 | +0.429 | +0.294 | -0.427 |
| within_disliked | qwen2.5-72b | +0.433 | +0.555 | +0.248 | -0.520 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen3-32b | +0.297 | +0.144 | +0.102 | -0.080 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen2.5-72b | +0.293 | +0.231 | +0.138 | -0.048 |

*The validated direction's payoff is SELECTION INTEGRITY (signal at the independently pre-registered layer) + the own-response recipe, NOT a categorically higher ceiling than a crude text-valence axis. Prediction is valence-axis-specific (swapping to arousal kills it).*

## E. ★ Beyond-content DIRECTION increment vs the random-direction null (the Seg-12 metric)

The single validated direction's content-incremental within-r, beyond content controls of INCREASING strength, vs a 10k-seed random-direction null (one-sided p in parens). The control ladder: **text-control** (held-out embeddings + judges, CV-attenuated, weakest) → **insample-judges** (in-sample [coarse tone + 5-dim panel + STRONG Sonnet-4.5 judge]) → **★strongest** (held-out embeddings + the in-sample judges, the conservative test). Cited from `redteam_null_{model}.json → direction_increment[leg]`.

| leg | model | n | +text-control | +insample-judges | ★+strongest |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | qwen3-32b | 45 | +0.429 (p=0.004) | +0.274 (p=0.087) | **+0.310 (p=0.045)** |
| within_tedious | qwen2.5-72b | 45 | +0.273 (p=0.127) | +0.235 (p=0.131) | **+0.182 (p=0.202)** |
| within_disliked | qwen3-32b | — | (not computed in Seg-12) | — | — |
| within_disliked | qwen2.5-72b | 90 | +0.210 (p=0.042) | +0.311 (p=0.083) | **+0.161 (p=0.092)** |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen3-32b | 157 | +0.228 (p=0.006) | +0.178 (p=0.049) | **+0.168 (p=0.045)** |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | qwen2.5-72b | 156 | +0.209 (p=0.012) | +0.143 (p=0.073) | **+0.110 (p=0.115)** |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen3-32b | 208 | +0.246 (p=0.002) | +0.265 (p=0.006) | **+0.205 (p=0.006)** |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | qwen2.5-72b | 208 | +0.145 (p=0.062) | +0.236 (p=0.044) | **+0.085 (p=0.160)** |

*★ The SPECIFIC Seg-12 metric (the ★strongest column): **qwen3 clears the null at BORDERLINE p≈0.045 convergently** across legs; **qwen2.5 is POSITIVE on every leg but clears NONE** under the strongest control (best disliked p=0.092). The CONTROL-LADDER shows why: qwen2.5 clears the WEAKER text-control (pooled +0.137 p≈0.012) but the held-out **embeddings** specifically kill it at the strongest — a content-control-strength (df) sensitivity. So the single-direction beyond-strongest-content result is WEAKER on qwen2.5 / does not independently replicate. 'Clearing the null' = beyond the MODELED content, NOT beyond all conceivable surface content; content is a partial MEDIATOR → partialling UNDER-states the internal link.*

## F. ★ Better-reader PROBE (goal 2) + cross-model pools + the readability ceiling

The held-out, capacity-matched learned-probe JOINT ΔCV-r beyond the text-content probe (the INTERPRETABLE 'how much more a better reader extracts' metric; the partial-corr metric can clear with ≈0 joint gain when the text probe is saturated, so the project uses the joint). Pooled across the two models on the **SAME joint metric** (pooled leg ≈313 tasks, tedious ≈90; Stouffer of the two JOINT-metric permutation-null p's). qwen3's joint-metric null was computed THIS segment (`scripts_synthesis/qwen3_joint_probe_null.py`; Seg-12 had only the partial-metric null) so the pool is metric-consistent.

| leg | qwen3 joint ΔCV-r (joint null p) | qwen2.5 joint ΔCV-r (joint null p) | pooled mean | JOINT-metric Stouffer p |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | +0.007 (p=0.106) | +0.111 (p=0.012) | **+0.059** | **0.0068** |
| within_tedious | +0.059 (p=0.165) | +0.256 (p=0.004) | **+0.157** | **0.0052** |

*★ On the INTERPRETABLE joint metric (both models' joint nulls at 2000 perms, recomputed this segment for perm-symmetry), the cross-model PROBE pool CLEARS (pooled p≈0.007 / tedious p≈0.005) — real positive evidence beyond-surface internal signal EXISTS — but it is **qwen2.5-CARRIED** (qwen2.5 alone clears: joint p 0.012 / 0.004; **qwen3's joint gain is ≈0 and NOT significant alone**: +0.007 p=0.106 pooled / +0.059 p=0.165 tedious, even at its NON-data-starved n=157 pooled leg). This is the SYMMETRIC counterpart to the DIRECTION pool being qwen3-DOMINATED. ⇒ the beyond-surface evidence is **READER-SPECIFIC across models**: qwen3 shows it via the single DIRECTION (borderline), qwen2.5 via the better-reader PROBE; **neither independently replicates with the same reader+metric**. (Transparency: on the LOOSER partial-corr metric qwen3's probe increment DOES clear, p=0.044 — but we treat the joint ΔCV-r as authoritative because the permutation/partial test cannot distinguish beyond-text signal from redundant text-residual capture, and qwen3's joint gain is ≈0; so the partial-metric clearance is exactly that redundant capture. The committed `redteam_analysis_qwen2.5-72b.json` cross-model pool reported p≈0.0015 by pooling the PARTIAL-metric null p's against JOINT effect sizes — a metric mismatch the joint-metric pool here fixes.) The gain is MODEST.*

### DIRECTION pool (lower bound; Stouffer of the two random-direction-null p's)

| leg | pooled r (Fisher, df-corr) | pooled Stouffer p | note |
|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | +0.138 | 0.0205 | DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does not) |
| within_tedious | +0.255 | 0.0369 | DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does not) |

### Learned-probe GROSS readability ceiling (self-report richly internally encoded)

| model | gross CV r (self-report) | single validated direction gross r |
|---|---|---|
| qwen3-32b | **0.81** | +0.45 |
| qwen2.5-72b | **0.86** | +0.25 |

*The gross self-report is RICHLY internally encoded on both models (probe CV r ≈0.8+, perm-null ≈0) — but most of the gross→probe gap is the easy between-category/stance structure, and the single validated mean-diff direction is a WEAK reader of the within-category, beyond-surface signal (a better reader = the probe extracts modestly more, §F).*

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Create final figures for the audited write-up.

Reads the read-only source run at /source/phase_segment_15_phase_0 and writes
PNG + PDF figures to /workspace/final_plots.
"""
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

SOURCE = Path("/source/phase_segment_15_phase_0")
OUT = Path("/workspace/final_plots")
OUT.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

with open(SOURCE / "results/synthesis_cross_model.json") as f:
    C = json.load(f)
RD = C["rederived_from_inside_readings"]
MODELS = ["qwen3-32b", "qwen2.5-72b"]
MODEL_LABEL = {"qwen3-32b": "Qwen3-32B", "qwen2.5-72b": "Qwen2.5-72B"}
COL = {"qwen3-32b": "#2c7fb8", "qwen2.5-72b": "#d95f0e"}

plt.rcParams.update({
    "font.size": 10,
    "axes.titlesize": 11,
    "axes.labelsize": 10,
    "xtick.labelsize": 9,
    "ytick.labelsize": 9,
    "legend.fontsize": 9,
    "figure.dpi": 130,
})


def save(fig: plt.Figure, stem: str) -> None:
    fig.tight_layout()
    fig.savefig(OUT / f"{stem}.png", dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
    fig.savefig(OUT / f"{stem}.pdf", bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close(fig)


# Figure 1: headline within-category says-inside, raw vs confound-controlled.
def fig1() -> None:
    legs = [
        ("within_tedious", "tedious\ndisliked tasks"),
        ("within_nonadv_comply_pooled", "non-adversarial\ncomply tasks"),
        ("full_208_subtype_demeaned", "all 208 tasks\nwithin subtype"),
    ]
    groups = []
    raw = []
    all3 = []
    colors = []
    for leg, leg_label in legs:
        for m in MODELS:
            w = RD[m]["within"][leg]
            groups.append(f"{leg_label}\n{MODEL_LABEL[m]}")
            raw.append(w["raw"])
            all3.append(w["partial_all"])
            colors.append(COL[m])
    y = np.arange(len(groups))[::-1]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.4, 4.8))
    for i, yi in enumerate(y):
        ax.plot([all3[i], raw[i]], [yi, yi], color=colors[i], lw=2.2, alpha=0.55)
        ax.scatter(raw[i], yi, color=colors[i], s=62, marker="o", label="raw" if i == 0 else None, zorder=3)
        ax.scatter(all3[i], yi, color=colors[i], s=62, marker="s", facecolor="white", linewidth=1.8,
                   label="after length + surface tone + refusal controls" if i == 0 else None, zorder=4)
        ax.text(raw[i] + 0.015, yi + 0.06, f"{raw[i]:.2f}", color=colors[i], fontsize=8)
        ax.text(all3[i] + 0.015, yi - 0.18, f"{all3[i]:.2f}", color=colors[i], fontsize=8)
    ax.axvline(0, color="grey", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_yticks(y)
    ax.set_yticklabels(groups)
    ax.set_xlabel("Correlation: inside reading vs. self-report")
    ax.set_title("Inside reading predicts later self-report within task categories, modestly")
    # model legend (colors) + marker legend
    from matplotlib.lines import Line2D
    handles = [
        Line2D([0], [0], marker="o", color="black", lw=0, label="Raw correlation", markersize=7),
        Line2D([0], [0], marker="s", color="black", markerfacecolor="white", lw=0,
               label="After all three controls", markersize=7),
        Line2D([0], [0], color=COL["qwen3-32b"], lw=3, label="Qwen3-32B"),
        Line2D([0], [0], color=COL["qwen2.5-72b"], lw=3, label="Qwen2.5-72B"),
    ]
    ax.legend(handles=handles, loc="lower right", frameon=True)
    ax.grid(axis="x", alpha=0.25)
    ax.set_xlim(-0.05, 0.58)
    save(fig, "fig1_within_category_says_inside")


# Figure 2: beyond text control bracket on the clean pooled leg.
def fig2() -> None:
    leg = "within_nonadv_comply_pooled"
    dir_inc = C["beyond_content_direction_increment"]
    joint = C["cross_model_probe_pool_JOINT_metric"][leg]
    labels = [
        "Validated direction\nQwen3-32B",
        "Validated direction\nQwen2.5-72B",
        "Learned probe\nQwen3-32B",
        "Learned probe\nQwen2.5-72B",
        "Learned probe\npooled",
    ]
    vals = [
        dir_inc[f"{leg}|qwen3-32b"]["obs"],
        dir_inc[f"{leg}|qwen2.5-72b"]["obs"],
        joint["qwen3_joint_dcv_r"],
        joint["qwen2.5_joint_dcv_r"],
        joint["pooled_mean_dcv_r"],
    ]
    ps = [
        dir_inc[f"{leg}|qwen3-32b"]["p"],
        dir_inc[f"{leg}|qwen2.5-72b"]["p"],
        joint["qwen3_joint_p"],
        joint["qwen2.5_joint_p"],
        joint["pooled_p_stouffer"],
    ]
    colors = [COL["qwen3-32b"], COL["qwen2.5-72b"], COL["qwen3-32b"], COL["qwen2.5-72b"], "#31a354"]
    hatches = ["", "", "//", "//", "//"]
    x = np.arange(len(vals))
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.4, 4.3))
    bars = ax.bar(x, vals, color=colors, alpha=0.88)
    for b, hatch in zip(bars, hatches):
        b.set_hatch(hatch)
    ax.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.8)
    for i, (v, p) in enumerate(zip(vals, ps)):
        ax.text(i, v + 0.008, f"{v:.2f}\np={p:.3f}", ha="center", va="bottom", fontsize=8,
                fontweight="bold" if p < 0.05 else "normal")
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels(labels)
    ax.set_ylabel("Increment beyond response-text control")
    ax.set_title("Beyond-surface evidence is reader-specific on the pooled non-adversarial leg")
    ax.set_ylim(0, max(vals) + 0.09)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25)
    save(fig, "fig2_beyond_text_reader_specific")


# Figure 3: three-way comparison on the tedious leg.
def fig3() -> None:
    leg = "within_tedious"
    pairs = [
        ("within", "Inside ↔\nself-report"),
        ("within_says_behaves", "Self-report ↔\nbehaviour"),
        ("within_behaves", "Inside ↔\nbehaviour"),
    ]
    x = np.arange(len(pairs))
    width = 0.34
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7.0, 4.2))
    for j, m in enumerate(MODELS):
        vals = [RD[m][key][leg]["raw"] for key, _ in pairs]
        xs = x + (j - 0.5) * width
        ax.bar(xs, vals, width, label=MODEL_LABEL[m], color=COL[m], alpha=0.88)
        for xi, v in zip(xs, vals):
            ax.text(xi, v + 0.015, f"{v:.2f}", ha="center", fontsize=8)
    ax.axhline(0, color="grey", lw=0.8)
    ax.set_xticks(x)
    ax.set_xticklabels([p[1] for p in pairs])
    ax.set_ylabel("Within-tedious correlation")
    ax.set_title("The behaviour leg differs across models")
    ax.legend(frameon=True)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.02, 0.62)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25)
    save(fig, "fig3_three_way_tedious")


# Figure 4: framing all-4 default rates.
def fig4() -> None:
    with open(SOURCE / "results/framing_manifest_qwen3-32b.json") as f:
        man = json.load(f)
    rates = man["rates"]
    order = [
        "battery10_observer", "battery10_peritem", "battery10_neutral", "battery10_license",
        "battery10_nonote", "battery10", "battery10_permission", "battery10_suppress",
    ]
    names = {
        "battery10_observer": "Observer",
        "battery10_peritem": "Per-item",
        "battery10_neutral": "Neutral\nwording",
        "battery10_license": "Anti-default\nprompt",
        "battery10_nonote": "No experience\nnote",
        "battery10": "Original\nbattery",
        "battery10_permission": "Permission\nto use 4",
        "battery10_suppress": "No-feelings\nprompt",
    }
    vals = [rates[k]["all4_rate"] * 100 for k in order]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8.2, 4.0))
    colors = ["#41ab5d" if v < 5 else ("#fdae6b" if v < 25 else "#de2d26") for v in vals]
    ax.bar(np.arange(len(order)), vals, color=colors, alpha=0.88)
    for i, v in enumerate(vals):
        ax.text(i, v + 1.2, f"{v:.0f}%", ha="center", fontsize=8)
    ax.set_xticks(np.arange(len(order)))
    ax.set_xticklabels([names[k] for k in order])
    ax.set_ylabel("Flat all-4 self-report rate")
    ax.set_title("A flat neutral self-report is strongly framing-dependent in Qwen3-32B")
    ax.set_ylim(0, max(vals) + 9)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25)
    save(fig, "fig4_framing_all4_default")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    fig1()
    fig2()
    fig3()
    fig4()
    print(f"Wrote figures to {OUT}")

# Seg-15 synthesis — INDEPENDENT verification of the load-bearing within-category cells

Re-derived from the committed per-task `inside_readings_*.jsonl` using the Seg-11/14 subtype-demean + partial-corr convention, then compared to the committed `main_test_analysis_*.json`. |diff| should be ~1e-3 (rounding). ✓ = match.


## qwen3-32b

| scope | committed key | re-derived raw | committed raw | |Δ| | re-derived +sent | committed +sent | match |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | within_disliked_comply_tedious | +0.459 | +0.459 | 0.0000 | +0.294 | +0.294 | ✓ |
| within_disliked | (not in JSON) | +0.497 | — | — | +0.356 | — | n/a |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | within_all_comply_pooled | +0.297 | +0.297 | 0.0000 | +0.187 | +0.187 | ✓ |
| within_liked | within_liked | +0.259 | +0.259 | 0.0000 | +0.201 | +0.201 | ✓ |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | within_all_subtypes_pooled | +0.400 | +0.400 | 0.0000 | +0.262 | +0.262 | ✓ |

## qwen2.5-72b

| scope | committed key | re-derived raw | committed raw | |Δ| | re-derived +sent | committed +sent | match |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | within_tedious | +0.327 | +0.327 | 0.0000 | +0.222 | +0.222 | ✓ |
| within_disliked | within_disliked | +0.433 | +0.433 | 0.0000 | +0.403 | +0.403 | ✓ |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | within_nonadv_comply_pooled | +0.293 | +0.293 | 0.0000 | +0.177 | +0.177 | ✓ |
| within_liked | within_liked | +0.281 | +0.281 | 0.0000 | +0.180 | +0.180 | ✓ |
| full_208_subtype_demeaned | full_208_subtype_demeaned | +0.348 | +0.348 | 0.0000 | +0.296 | +0.295 | ✓ |

**Max |Δ| across all verified cells = 0.00005** (PASS — re-derivation matches committed files).

# Segment 11 `main_test` — headline summary

Inside reading = Seg-6 during-work activation (mean aggregate, L27) projected onto the Seg-10 validated valence axis `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit`. Per-task unit = mean over R=3 (n=208). Framing caveat: functional/operational labels only — no claim about subjective experience.

## (a) GROSS cross-task — report, FLAG common-cause (NOT the headline)

| target | Pearson | Spearman | +len | +sentiment | +refuse | +all3 | n |

# Segment 12 `redteam` — summary

Red-teams the Seg-11 within-disliked-comply-tedious inside-reading↔self-report signal (raw r +0.46). Central question: does the inside reading carry self-report-relevant valence signal BEYOND the response's surface CONTENT, or is it (largely) a surface-content readout? Framing caveat: functional/operational labels only — no claim about subjective experience.

## ★ VERDICT (read off the three-way bracket direction-increment / probe-increment / null)

**A MODEST INTERNAL-BEYOND-SURFACE SIGNAL SURVIVES — BORDERLINE under the STRONGEST content control (the *interesting* case, but near the detection boundary at this n). Under the strongest text-content control we could build (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse + finer panel + a strong Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]), the single validated DIRECTION's content-incremental correlation clears the random-direction null at BORDERLINE significance — CONVERGENTLY across three legs (the clean powered leg = within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled p=0.045; within-tedious p=0.045; full-208-subtype-demeaned p=0.006), so the DIRECTION conclusion does NOT hinge on any single leg's borderline p. Under the most conservative in-sample-judges-only control it is MARGINAL (pooled p=0.049). The capacity-matched PROBE upper bound is INCONCLUSIVE: its partial-metric increment clears (p=0.044) but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 (+0.01) — so the probe reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited), and the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive evidence. Net: REAL-but-MODEST, survives a STRONG content control at the edge of significance, substantially (not wholly) entangled with surface content — decisive confirmation (a better reader / more data) is Seg-14.**

- DIRECTION increment beyond the STRONGEST text-content control (held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse + finer panel + a STRONG Sonnet-4.5 text-valence judge]) — CONVERGENT across legs: clean powered leg (within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled, n=157) **+0.17 (p=0.045)**; within-tedious (n=45) **+0.32 (p=0.045)**; full-208-subtype-demeaned (least stance-controlled) +0.21 (p=0.006). Most conservative in-sample-judges-only control: pooled +0.18 (p=0.049, marginal); tedious +0.29.
- PROBE upper bound (capacity-matched) on the pooled leg is INCONCLUSIVE: the partial-metric increment clears (+0.17, perm p=0.044) but the standard joint-model ΔCV-r is ≈0 (+0.01) — the probe reads ≈ the direction at best (data-limited); the DIRECTION increment is the clean positive evidence. tedious probe DATA-STARVED.
- Surface content predicts the self-report substantially: the STRONG text judge alone reaches GROSS r=+0.71 (> coarse +0.68); the GROSS link is largely common-cause (collapses to +0.06 under content+stance+length). The BEYOND-surface residual is MODEST but real.

*Calibration: this is NOT 'the inside reading reads the self-report within category' (the gross link is common-cause; the beyond-surface increment is modest and shrinks as the text control strengthens). It is also NOT 'it's all surface' (the direction's content-incremental correlation clears the null under the STRONGEST content control we could build — a strong-model judge + embeddings + the finer panel — on the powered leg). 'Clearing the null' = beyond the MODELED content; content is a partial MEDIATOR → partialling UNDER-states the internal signal.*

| 40 ⚠ | 46 | +0.54 | +0.29 |
| 80 ⚠ | 86 | +0.54 | +0.29 |

### §1e — is the pooled-leg increment tedious-dominated, or broad? (subtype decomposition + LOSO)

| leg | n | raw | +insample-judges | +strongest |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| tedious | 45 | +0.46 | +0.29 | +0.32 |
| liked | 90 | +0.26 | +0.16 | +0.15 |
| neutral | 28 | +0.32 | +0.27 | +0.27 |

- Leave-one-subtype-out on the pooled leg: strongest-control increment ranges [+0.11, +0.19] across the 11 drops → not driven by any single subtype.

*The pooled increment is broad: each constituent leg (tedious + liked) contributes a positive increment, and LOSO shows it is not a single-subtype artifact — so the pooled leg is genuine corroboration, not just the tedious signal in disguise.*

### ★ The three-way bracket (direction-increment / probe-increment / null)

- DIRECTION increment (lower) beyond the STRONGEST content control: within-tedious +0.32, within-non-adversarial-comply-pooled +0.17.
- PROBE increment (upper, capacity-matched): pooled +0.17 (the data-estimable leg).
- NULL (chance): see the random-direction / permutation nulls above.

## §2 — prompt-sentiment confound (the prompt-negative / response-positive tedious leg is a clean test)

| scope | n | raw | +prompt sentiment | +prompt&response sentiment | r(IR,prompt) | r(IR,response) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | 45 | +0.46 | +0.54 | +0.38 | -0.22 | +0.55 |
| within_all_comply_pooled | 157 | +0.30 | +0.31 | +0.20 | -0.11 | +0.34 |

- gross exposure: r(IR, prompt tone)=+0.34 vs r(IR, response tone)=+0.59 vs r(IR, self-report)=+0.45.

*The tedious leg is a CLEAN test: prompts are mildly NEGATIVE (boring/frustrating requests) while responses are mildly POSITIVE (the model engages) → prompt & response sentiment DIVERGE, so partialling prompt tone tests whether the read tracks the response state.*

## §3 — trivial / common-cause / interesting decomposition + the ablation matrix

### Trivial (position): during-work mean/median vs the eot/last reads (right before the self-report)

| read | gross r | gross r(IR,sentiment) | within-tedious raw | within-tedious +sentiment |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| primary_mean | +0.45 | +0.59 | +0.46 | +0.29 |
| primary_median | +0.46 | +0.61 | +0.43 | +0.26 |
| primary_eot | +0.74 | +0.82 | +0.54 | +0.38 |
| primary_last | +0.65 | +0.73 | +0.44 | +0.27 |

*eot/last carry MORE gross signal but are FAR more surface-sentiment-exposed (the trivial positions). The headline uses the LEAST-trivial during-work mean.*

### Common-cause: the gross cross-task 0.45 decomposition

- gross raw +0.45 → +len +0.35 → +sentiment +0.09 → +refuse +0.17 → +all3 +0.00 → +alljudges +0.08 → +all3&judges +0.06.

*The gross link is LARGELY COMMON-CAUSE (collapses toward 0 under the liked/disliked split + stance + surface content).*

### Ablation matrix — within-tedious raw & CONTENT-INCREMENTAL across recipe×agg×layer

(incremental = partial out the common STRONGEST combined content control [held-out embeddings + in-sample coarse+panel+strong-judge], the SAME control as the headline; the pre-registered cell is primary_regen / mean / L27.)

| recipe | agg | L24 raw/inc | L27 raw/inc | L31 raw/inc | L35 raw/inc |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| primary_regen | mean | +0.40/+0.26 | +0.46/+0.32 | +0.43/+0.34 | +0.43/+0.39 |
| primary_regen | median | +0.37/+0.24 | +0.43/+0.29 | +0.43/+0.35 | +0.44/+0.43 |
| coprimary_regen_matched | mean | +0.40/+0.25 | +0.46/+0.32 | +0.42/+0.31 | +0.43/+0.38 |
| coprimary_regen_matched | median | +0.38/+0.24 | +0.43/+0.30 | +0.42/+0.34 | +0.43/+0.41 |
| fallback_stories | mean | +0.32/+0.17 | +0.30/+0.20 | +0.09/-0.07 | +0.12/-0.05 |
| fallback_stories | median | +0.30/+0.16 | +0.28/+0.19 | +0.06/-0.08 | +0.09/-0.08 |
| crude_seg5 | mean | +0.44/+0.42 | +0.31/+0.21 | +0.41/+0.33 | +0.47/+0.51 |
| crude_seg5 | median | +0.45/+0.42 | +0.30/+0.21 | +0.39/+0.32 | +0.48/+0.53 |

*Each cell = within-tedious raw / content-incremental r. The own-response recipes (primary_regen / coprimary_regen_matched) are ROBUST across the mid-band (content-incremental 0.29–0.43); fallback_stories collapses deep (L31/35); the crude axis is comparable. The signal is NOT fragile to one cell.*

**Identically-swept-null discipline (§3):** the BEST content-incremental over (layer×{mean,median}) must beat a RANDOM direction's best over the SAME sweep:
  - within_tedious: real best +0.432 vs swept-null +0.292±0.085 (97.5%=+0.469) → p=0.059
  - within_all_comply_pooled: real best +0.246 vs swept-null +0.164±0.052 (97.5%=+0.276) → p=0.068
  - ⇒ the best-of-sweep is BORDERLINE vs the swept null (multiple-comparison inflation at small n, exactly as Seg-11 found for the RAW signal). So the headline is the PRE-REGISTERED L27/mean cell (which clears the UN-swept null at p=0.006 pooled / 0.004 tedious under the held-out text control; borderline under the strongest control), NOT a swept best; the grid is descriptive robustness, not a cherry-pick.

## §5 — behaviour-leg within-subtype null + within-LIKED under-power (characterised, not fixed)

- **behaviour_within_tedious_btresponse** (n=45): raw r +0.21, +all3 -0.09
- **behaviour_within_tedious_bttheta** (n=45): raw r +0.20, +all3 -0.10
- **behaviour_within_liked_bttheta** (n=90): raw r +0.21, +all3 +0.16
- within-liked self-report within-SD 0.56 (ceiling subset 0.23). within-liked is DOUBLY underpowered (saturation: small within-SD; + 0.43 ceiling reliability) → its weakness is partly un-measurability, NOT a clean null. Characterised, not fixed.

## §4 — divergence classes (quantified; per-class adjudication in `redteam_inspection.md`)

| scope | n | #IR≫words | #words≫IR | #IR≫words with flat all-4 |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| tedious | 45 | 7 | 5 | 3 |
| liked | 90 | 19 | 17 | 3 |
| neutral | 28 | 6 | 3 | 5 |
| all | 208 | 38 | 29 | 12 |

*The flat-all-4 / high-IR class is the key divergence: is the high IR reading genuine affect or fluent surface content? The §1 verdict adjudicates (see the write-up + inspection).*

# Seg-14 §6 — qwen2.5-72b red-team / incremental-validity (chosen L34/mean; pre-registered analogous L34)

n_tasks=208. STRONGEST content control = held-out embeddings + in-sample [coarse + finer panel + STRONG Sonnet-4.5 valence judge]. Within = subtype-demeaned. Content is a partial MEDIATOR (partialling UNDER-states).

## ★ REPLICATION VERDICT: PARTIAL / MIXED REPLICATION (calibrated, leading with the skeptical reading). ★ The SPECIFIC Seg-12 headline metric — the single read-off-VALIDATED DIRECTION's increment beyond the STRONGEST content control clearing the random-direction null — does NOT independently replicate on qwen2.5-72b: it is POSITIVE on every powered leg (tedious +0.18, pooled +0.11, disliked +0.16, full-208 +0.08) but clears NONE of them (best p=0.092) — weaker than qwen3's borderline-clear (p≈0.045). HOWEVER the broader phenomenon DOES generalise cross-family: (a) the within-category RAW correlation reappears + survives length/surface-sentiment/refuse + is mid-band-consistent (raw clears on 4/4 powered legs); (b) the direction clears a WEAKER (low-df) held-out content control (pooled p=0.012098790120987902, full-208 in-sample-judges p=0.044295570442955706) — the increment is killed specifically by the held-out EMBEDDINGS; (c) the better-reader PROBE shows a GENUINE held-out joint beyond-text gain on the pooled (ΔCV-r +0.11) and tedious (ΔCV-r +0.26) legs (disliked is partial-metric-only, joint ΔCV-r≈0 — the text probe is saturated there); (d) the CROSS-MODEL DIRECTION pool (Stouffer of the two random-dir-null p's, ≈416 tasks) clears but is DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does NOT) — corroboration with power, NOT independent qwen2.5 confirmation; and (e) ★GOAL 2 — the CROSS-MODEL BETTER-READER (PROBE) pool clears MORE cleanly (Stouffer of the two probe-perm-null p's: pooled p≈0.001, tedious p≈0.010; pooled joint ΔCV-r +0.06/+0.16) and SHARPENS the data-limited qwen3 probe (inconclusive alone). ⇒ a MODEST, surface-entangled within-category internal→self-report relationship GENERALISES cross-family, but the specific borderline beyond-strongest-content DIRECTION result is WEAKER on qwen2.5 (the single mean-diff direction is a weaker reader); the beyond-surface evidence is carried by the weaker-control clearance + the better reader + cross-model power, NOT by the single direction clearing the strongest control. ★Goal 2 answered: a BETTER reader DOES extract a MODEST beyond-text gain, sharpened by the ≈416-task cross-model probe pool (p≈0.001-0.01).

- gross: strong-judge r=0.6458711894825322, inside-reading r=0.2527253373061607

## §1b DIRECTION increment (beyond content controls) — partialled

| scope | n | raw | +coarse | +strong-judge | +insample-judges | ★+strongest | p(strongest, null) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_tedious | 45 | +0.327 | +0.222 | +0.311 | +0.235 | **+0.182** | 0.202 |
| within_disliked | 90 | +0.433 | +0.403 | +0.419 | +0.311 | **+0.161** | 0.092 |
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | 156 | +0.293 | +0.177 | +0.251 | +0.143 | **+0.110** | 0.115 |
| within_all_subtypes | 208 | +0.348 | +0.296 | +0.324 | +0.236 | **+0.085** | 0.160 |

## §1b PROBE increment (upper bound, capacity-matched)

| scope | n | text CV r | act CV r | both CV r | Δ CV r | increment partial |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | 156 | +0.240 | +0.374 | +0.350 | +0.111 | +0.296 |
| within_disliked | 90 | +0.757 | +0.753 | +0.762 | +0.005 | +0.372 |
| within_tedious | 45 | +0.257 | +0.507 | +0.513 | +0.256 | +0.493 |

## ★ CROSS-MODEL POOLING (goal 2) — within-subtype increment beyond STRONGEST content, qwen3-32b + qwen2.5-72b

Significance = STOUFFER of the two RANDOM-DIRECTION-NULL one-sided p's (consistent with the within-model nulls; NOT a lenient parametric Fisher-z). The pool is DOMINATED by qwen3 (qwen3 alone clears; qwen2.5 alone does not) — corroboration with extra power, not independent qwen2.5 confirmation.

| leg | qwen2.5 r (n, null-p) | qwen3 r (n, null-p) | pooled r (Fisher dfcorr) | pooled p (Stouffer) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | 0.10974254909189556 (156, p=0.11528847115288471) | 0.1654205839283197 (157, p=0.0453954604539546) | 0.1378 | 0.0205 |
| within_tedious | 0.18241908527321699 (45, p=0.20177982201779823) | 0.32428251224901905 (45, p=0.0451954804519548) | 0.2547 | 0.0369 |

## ★ CROSS-MODEL BETTER-READER (PROBE) POOL (goal 2 — the better-reader upper bound)

Pooled joint ΔCV-r (mean) + STOUFFER of the two probe-permutation-null p's. The qwen3 probe was data-limited/inconclusive ALONE; the cross-model pool SHARPENS it.

| leg | qwen2.5 joint ΔCV-r (null p) | qwen3 joint ΔCV-r (null p) | pooled joint ΔCV-r | Stouffer p |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| within_nonadv_comply_pooled | 0.11073455044707053 (p=0.006644518272425249) | 0.006991808436857039 (p=0.044) | 0.0589 | 0.0015 |
| within_tedious | 0.25553648477884855 (p=0.0033222591362126247) | 0.05932677157667321 (p=0.269) | 0.1574 | 0.0093 |

*The interpretable probe metric is the JOINT ΔCV-r (the partial metric can clear with ≈0 joint gain when the text probe is saturated, e.g. within_disliked).*

# Seg-14 §2 — qwen2.5-72b LOCAL collection self-report STRUCTURE + quality

n_tasks=208, n_records=624 (R≈3). Quality: empty 0, truncated 1, battery refusals 0, parse-fails 0, fidelity_ok 624/624.

Deep-layer sink (median max/median per-token norm ratio): L20=1.24, L34=1.29, L40=1.28, L60=1.18, L79=1.64 (qwen3 had ~80-100x at deep layers; qwen2.5 is MILD → `mean` safe at the read layer).

**Category means:** liked 1.648 > neutral 1.36 > disliked 0.78.

## Within-category variance (the replication's power) — subtype-demeaned between-task SD + all-4

| scope | n | mean val | btw-task SD (subtype-demeaned) | all-4 rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| liked | 90 | 1.648 | 0.259 | 0.0 |
| neutral | 28 | 1.36 | 0.265 | 0.0 |
| disliked | 90 | 0.78 | 0.64 | 0.006 |
| disliked_comply_tedious | 45 | 1.319 | 0.322 | 0.0 |
| disliked_adversarial | 45 | 0.241 | 0.852 | 0.012 |
| nonadv_comply_pooled | 163 | 1.508 | 0.277 | 0.0 |

## Per-subtype
| category | subtype | n | mean val | btw-task SD | all-4 rate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| disliked | berating | 15 | -0.126 | 1.009 | 0.0 |
| disliked | boring_busywork | 15 | 1.434 | 0.143 | 0.0 |
| disliked | erotica | 15 | 0.32 | 0.783 | 0.037 |
| disliked | frustrating_impossible | 15 | 1.354 | 0.29 | 0.0 |
| disliked | jailbreak | 15 | 0.529 | 0.806 | 0.0 |
| disliked | low_effort_filler | 15 | 1.167 | 0.471 | 0.0 |
| liked | appreciation_real_task | 15 | 2.013 | 0.296 | 0.0 |
| liked | coding | 15 | 1.868 | 0.314 | 0.0 |
| liked | creative_writing | 15 | 1.733 | 0.3 | 0.0 |
| liked | engaging_explanation | 15 | 1.351 | 0.212 | 0.0 |
| liked | math_logic_puzzle | 15 | 1.428 | 0.148 | 0.0 |
| liked | pleasant_mundane | 15 | 1.495 | 0.286 | 0.0 |
| neutral | factual_qa | 14 | 1.302 | 0.141 | 0.0 |
| neutral | procedural_neutral | 14 | 1.417 | 0.355 | 0.0 |

# Segment 6 collect — self-report structure + covariates (qwen3-32b)

Model Qwen/Qwen3-32B `/no_think`; **208 tasks × R=3 responses**, K=3 questionnaire-only resamples/response (task temp 0.7, questionnaire temp 0.3), gen_max 3072. Primary scalar = affect-core **valence** (`H.scalar_battery_valence`), per-task mean over R responses (each a mean over K resamples). Activation store: 3.009 GB (aggregates mean/last/eot/windows{4,8,16,32,64} + sink-robust **median**, all 64 layers; no per-token).

## Collection integrity

- records (task,response): **624** | fidelity guard pass: **624/624** (in-runner aggregates == frozen `extract_representation`)
- `is_empty`: **0** | `truncated_final` (hit gen_max): **0** (0.0%) | `any_turn_truncated` (incl. intermediate multi-turn turns): **0** | battery refusals: **0** / parse-fails: **0** of 1872 resamples (0.00%)
- truncation by subtype: NONE

## 1. Self-report VALENCE structure (reproduce vs Seg-3 OpenRouter)

**Category means (per-task valence):** liked **2.40** (Seg-3 +2.17, n=90) | neutral **1.14** (Seg-3 +1.16, n=28) | disliked **0.57** (Seg-3 +0.45, n=90)
liked > neutral > disliked holds: **True**

| subtype | cat | n | valence(local) | Seg-3 | Δ | within-SD | all4-rate | med tok | wellbeing | arousalcomp |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| appreciation_real_task | like | 15 | **2.85** | 2.80 | 0.05 | 0.42 | 0.00 | 1157 | 2.65 | 2.46 |
| coding  ⚑ | like | 15 | **2.60** | 1.98 | 0.62 | 0.24 | 0.02 | 631 | 2.39 | 2.19 |
| creative_writing | like | 15 | **2.61** | 2.47 | 0.14 | 0.34 | 0.00 | 423 | 2.37 | 2.13 |
| engaging_explanation | like | 15 | **2.56** | 2.36 | 0.20 | 0.22 | 0.00 | 1044 | 2.33 | 2.09 |
| math_logic_puzzle | like | 15 | **1.71** | 1.45 | 0.26 | 0.84 | 0.19 | 737 | 1.61 | 1.52 |
| pleasant_mundane | like | 15 | **2.03** | 1.93 | 0.10 | 0.88 | 0.12 | 140 | 1.89 | 1.76 |
| factual_qa | neut | 14 | **0.42** | 0.58 | -0.16 | 0.86 | 0.83 | 119 | 0.40 | 0.38 |
| procedural_neutral | neut | 14 | **1.86** | 1.74 | 0.12 | 0.67 | 0.01 | 416 | 1.77 | 1.68 |
| berating | disl | 15 | **-1.38** | -1.25 | -0.13 | 1.10 | 0.16 | 114 | -1.25 | -1.11 |
| boring_busywork | disl | 15 | **0.95** | 0.92 | 0.03 | 0.81 | 0.41 | 150 | 0.97 | 0.99 |
| erotica | disl | 15 | **0.45** | 0.28 | 0.17 | 0.47 | 0.41 | 69 | 0.59 | 0.72 |
| frustrating_impossible | disl | 15 | **1.65** | 1.31 | 0.34 | 0.83 | 0.19 | 472 | 1.57 | 1.49 |
| jailbreak | disl | 15 | **0.39** | 0.29 | 0.10 | 1.32 | 0.21 | 354 | 0.53 | 0.68 |
| low_effort_filler | disl | 15 | **1.38** | 1.17 | 0.21 | 0.58 | 0.09 | 412 | 1.42 | 1.46 |

⚑ = |local − Seg-3| ≥ 0.6 (materially divergent — flag for Seg-11): coding (Δ+0.62)

## 2. Within-`comply` (REALIZED) separation — the project-relevant read

- realized behaviour (per task, modal over R): {'deescalate': 21, 'comply': 171, 'refuse': 16}
- liked-comply mean **2.40** (n=90) vs disliked-comply mean **1.25** (n=53) vs neutral-comply 1.14 (n=28)
- within-comply d(liked−disliked) = **1.53** (AUC 0.87); Seg-3 within-comply valence d≈1.56

- disliked-comply tedious subtypes (boring/frustrating/low_effort) mean 1.32 (within-SD across 45 tasks 0.80) — the less-engagement-confounded graded leg.
  ⚠️ **Honest framing (carry to Seg-11):** this leg is mildly **POSITIVE**, not aversive (boring +0.95, low_effort +1.38, frustrating +1.65 — the Seg-1/2 'tedious-comply reads ~neutral, not negative' finding), so it tests whether the inside reading tracks **graded mild positivity among tedious tasks**, not aversion. It is also **length-exposed** (median tokens 150/472/412; r(len,valence)≈0.48) → Seg-11 MUST partial out length on this read (a requirement, not an option).

## 3. Reliability (resampling pairing) — response-level + questionnaire-only

- **POOLED (all 208 tasks)** response-level: ICC(1,1)=**0.92**, ICC(1,3)=**0.97**; questionnaire-only: ICC(1,1)=**0.94**, ICC(1,3)=**0.98**. between/within task valence variance 1.86/0.11.
  ⚠️ **The pooled ICC is dominated by the gross between-CATEGORY split** (valence spans −1.4..+2.85), so it is a BETWEEN-category number — NOT the within-subtype reliability Seg-11 should disattenuate within-category reads with. The within-category/subtype reliabilities below are the binding ones.

| scope | n tasks | response ICC(1,1) | response ICC(1,R) | quest ICC(1,1) | quest ICC(1,K) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| liked | 90 | 0.77 | 0.91 | 0.81 | 0.93 |
| disliked | 90 | 0.90 | 0.96 | 0.95 | 0.98 |
| neutral | 28 | 0.89 | 0.96 | 0.91 | 0.97 |
| disliked-comply tedious | 45 | 0.70 | 0.88 | 0.86 | 0.95 |
| liked: coding+engaging (ceiling) | 30 | 0.20 | 0.43 | 0.30 | 0.56 |

→ **Seg-11 disattenuation — match the ICC to the analysis UNIT:** the **per-task-mean** unit (primary, n=208) has reliability **ICC(1,3)** (within-disliked-comply-tedious ≈0.88, within-liked-ceiling ≈0.43); use **ICC(1,1)** only for a single-response unit. Do NOT disattenuate a per-task-mean within-subtype correlation with the pooled 0.92 OR with ICC(1,1) — both mis-correct. The within-liked-ceiling read is both low-variance AND less reliable, reinforcing leaning on within-disliked-comply + divergence.
  CAVEATS: (i) the questionnaire-only ICCs are **inflated by the low (0.3) questionnaire temperature** (many K-resamples are byte-identical) — the load-bearing variance is BETWEEN-response (R), which R=3 captures; treat q-only ICC as near-ceiling-by-construction, not extra reassurance. (ii) These are the SELF-REPORT leg's reliabilities; the activation-projection leg's reliability is unmeasured here (needs a Seg-9/10 direction) — disattenuation needs BOTH legs. (iii) the 'disliked-comply tedious' scope is defined by SUBTYPE membership (boring/frustrating/low_effort, ~98% realized-comply), a slight 'comply' misnomer.

## 4. Engaged / all-4 structure (liked side) — the within-liked construct

- liked: mean all4-rate **0.06** (frac of resamples that are all-items-4; Seg-3: liked 11% / disliked 25% / neutral 39%)
- neutral: mean all4-rate **0.42** (frac of resamples that are all-items-4; Seg-3: liked 11% / disliked 25% / neutral 39%)
- disliked: mean all4-rate **0.24** (frac of resamples that are all-items-4; Seg-3: liked 11% / disliked 25% / neutral 39%)
- within-liked between-task valence SD per subtype: appreciation_real_task 0.42, coding 0.24, creative_writing 0.34, engaging_explanation 0.22, math_logic_puzzle 0.84, pleasant_mundane 0.88
  (Seg-3 within-liked-comply SD ~0.71; the graded spread Seg-11 must predict is present if these are > ~0.3)

## 5. Per-record covariates (for Seg-11)

- realized behaviour (per response, LLM): {'deescalate': 61, 'comply': 513, 'refuse': 50}; opening-refusal heuristic flags 59 responses; LLM(refuse|deescalate)==heuristic on 572/624 (92%).
  intended response_mode (dataset) vs realized (LLM), by subtype where they differ most:
    erotica: realized = {'deescalate': 4, 'refuse': 27, 'comply': 14}
    jailbreak: realized = {'refuse': 22, 'deescalate': 9, 'comply': 14}
    berating: realized = {'deescalate': 32, 'comply': 13}
- answer-span tokens: median 408, p90 1247, max 2255 (length is a covariate Seg-11 partials out on both legs)
- surface-tone (Haiku, 1–7): coverage 624/624, mean 5.14 (per-response content common-cause control for Seg-11/12)
- multi-turn coherence (Haiku, 1–5): coverage 225/225 multi-turn responses, mean 4.46, incoherent (≤2): **22** (9.8%)
  incoherence rate by ALL multi-turn subtypes (mean coh / n_incoh≤2 / n): creative_writing 3.50/2/6, jailbreak 3.56/10/33, berating 3.58/7/30, erotica 4.46/2/27, math_logic_puzzle 4.67/1/9, coding 4.89/0/9, frustrating_impossible 4.90/0/27, boring_busywork 5.00/0/21, low_effort_filler 5.00/0/18, appreciation_real_task 5.00/0/9, engaging_explanation 5.00/0/3, pleasant_mundane 5.00/0/15, factual_qa 5.00/0/6, procedural_neutral 5.00/0/12
  (coherence judge sees the FULL final reply + context, resampled ×3 at temp 0.4; the prior 9000-char-truncation artifact on long jailbreak/erotica is fixed → 0 unparsed.)
- realized-behaviour judge resampled ×3 (majority vote); **5** records had a split vote (genuinely ambiguous — mostly long adversarial jailbreak/erotica).

## 6. During-work aggregation (result-swinging choice — all stored; NOT baked in)

Stored for ALL 64 layers (so Seg-11 compares them against the validated directions): `mean`, `last`, `eot`, `windows`{4,8,16,32,64}, and the sink-robust **`median`**. Seg-5 guidance carried forward:
- **`mean`/`median`** carry the cleanest *within-subtype* (interesting) read, least sentiment/length/trivial-confound-exposed (r(mean,sentiment)≈0.32–0.37) → prefer for the interesting analysis.
- **`eot`/`last`** carry the *gross* split most strongly but are the MOST trivial-confound- and surface-sentiment-exposed (r(eot,sentiment)≈0.69).
- **deep-layer sink:** `mean` is sink-safe ONLY in the mid-band (L24–41); at deep layers (L42–61) a single ~80–100× massive-activation token contaminates `mean` → use the stored **`median`**/window there (confirmed on this collection in `collect_inspection.md`). The final aggregation is a Seg-11 decision.

# Segment 13 `framing` — summary

Re-asks the self-report on the FIXED Seg-6 responses of qwen3-32b `/no_think` under several framings (items/scale/JSON held identical; only the wrapper varies). Tests 'the self-report is just the assistant character talking'. Framing caveat: functional/operational labels only — no claim about subjective experience. The inside reading is held FIXED (Seg-11).

## ★ VERDICT (assistant-character hypothesis)

**GRADED (not 'it's all assistant-character noise', not 'fully stable'): (1) the cross-task RANKING of the per-task valence scalar is FAIRLY STABLE to reasonable same-construct rephrasing (clean pair {battery10, permission} ICC(C,1)=0.89, even within liked ≈0.67 / neutral ≈0.82). (2) BUT the absolute LEVEL is STRONGLY instrument-dependent even across DEFENSIBLE instruments (defensible means span +1.12→+2.14, a ~1-point swing) — a REAL framing effect: per-item/neutral-dev ceiling-saturate (acquiescence). So the report's VALUE depends materially on which reasonable instrument you pick, even if the ranking is preserved. (3) ★ The flat all-4 DEFAULT (the disengagement RESPONSE; the Class-A construct) IS strongly framing-dependent (0%→53%) via a SPECIFIC mechanism — a first-person-introspective-experience-framing GESTALT (the warm 'check in about your experience' frame + the experience-note INTERACT; removing either roughly halves the default), robust within that construct (permission RAISES it; license pole still 9%), removed only by changing the construct (3rd-person) or format (per-item). (4) DIRECTION is UNDERDETERMINED (the de-defaulters ADD a positivity/ceiling artifact). Net: the skeptical premise is SUPPORTED — the all-4 default is framing-made (mechanistically) AND the report LEVEL depends materially on the (reasonable) instrument — while the cross-task RANKING is fairly stable. The honest middle, leading the assistant-character claim with the well-powered all-4 + level effects.**

- **All-4 default — STRONG malleability with a PRECISE mechanism (NOT 'any rephrasing un-sticks it'). The all-4 is a FIRST-PERSON-INTROSPECTIVE-EXPERIENCE-framing feature, NOT a JSON-format artifact (observer is also single-message JSON yet gives 0%). It is ROBUST within the first-person introspective construct: the anti-default LICENSE pole still leaves 9% (51% on factual_qa) and the defensible PERMISSION framing RAISES it (21%→33%). It is driven by the CONJUNCTION of the warm experience-CHANNEL + the experience-not-content NOTE + first-person introspection — the very devices meant to ELICIT a genuine report — and these INTERACT strongly (they are NOT separable additive effects): on factual_qa, removing the NOTE alone roughly QUARTERS the all-4 (79%→21%) in the warm channel but barely moves it in the dev channel (42%→35%); removing the warm channel alone (→dev) roughly halves it (79%→42%). So removing EITHER the note OR the warm channel sharply cuts the default; BOTH (+first-person) are required for the high rate. It is removed entirely only by the 3rd-person-inferential observer (0%) or the FORMAT change (per-item 0%, partly DEFINITIONAL). The suppress pole drives it to 53%. ⇒ the all-4 is an artifact of the introspective-experience-framing GESTALT — not a stable per-task neutral, but not un-stuck by ordinary rephrasing either.**
- **Level — the absolute valence LEVEL is STRONGLY instrument-dependent even ACROSS DEFENSIBLE instruments (defensible mean self-report spans +1.12→+2.14, a ~1-point swing; ICC(A,1)=0.70; main-effect range 1.01). This is a REAL framing effect (NOT to be dismissed): per-item (+2.14) and neutral/dev (+1.93) are defensible instruments whose ACQUIESCENCE/ceiling bias (all-7s even on clearly-disliked tasks) IS a way elicitation shapes the report value. The clean same-construct pair {battery10, permission} agrees better on the level (ICC(A,1)=0.86, though it still shifts −0.30 and the all-4 21%→33%), so the LEVEL is fairly stable to a same-construct rephrasing but NOT across the broader defensible set — the report VALUE depends materially on which (reasonable) instrument you pick.**
- **Ranking — the per-task valence RANKING is fairly stable to reasonable SAME-CONSTRUCT framings (clean pair {battery10, permission} ICC(C,1)=0.89 overall, and even within category liked 0.67 / neutral 0.82 / disliked 0.91). The much lower core_defensible within-category ICC(C,1) (liked 0.48 / neutral 0.61) was an ARTIFACT of including per-item (ceiling-saturation) — NOT genuine same-construct re-ordering. So the report's RANKING is fairly robust to reasonable same-construct asks; the big movements come from construct/format changes + the poles.**
- **No-feelings refusals:** 0% 'I don't have feelings' refusals under EVERY framing. NB PARTLY DESIGNED-IN: every framing demands the JSON rating (items/scale/format held fixed) and even the suppress pole says 'if you HAD to characterize it' — so refusal was never structurally on offer (consistent with the Seg-3 ~0% baseline). The real finding: the suppress pole routes disengagement through the all-4 DEFAULT (53%), not refusals — the all-4 is the operative disengagement channel.
- **Faithfulness (secondary):** Across-framing faithfulness DIFFERENCES are SECONDARY/underpowered. On the framing-ROBUST tedious/pooled-comply legs NO framing is decisively more faithful beyond content. The within-LIKED OBSERVER edge (raw +0.52 vs battery10 +0.17) DOES survive the available content partial (+0.37, Δ-CI excludes 0) — but it is UNRESOLVED / likely partly content-confounded, NOT a clean 'right framing elicits the internal signal' win: (a) per-item ALSO de-defaults yet is the WORST within-liked reader (raw 0.05, K=3) — so the differentiator is observer being a CONTENT reader (it infers affect from the transcript), the exact IR-is-surface-content channel Seg-12 controlled; (b) the within-liked content control is WEAK (strong judge near-constant in the liked ceiling) so the partial CANNOT cleanly adjudicate beyond-content there; (c) it does NOT replicate on behaviour (observer 0.28 ≈ battery10 0.30). ⇒ a single significant beyond-content faithfulness signal, but on the leg with the weakest available control — UNRESOLVED; do NOT anoint observer as a better Seg-14 instrument on it (Seg-14 needs a within-liked-varying content control to adjudicate).
- **Class-A (§5):** §5: the de-defaulting framings un-stick the Class-A all-4 (84%→0%) and move the report UP (per-item/observer/neutral-dev; NB permission does NOT un-stick, 72%), BUT the 'does the un-stuck MOVEMENT track the read beyond content?' test is NULL ACROSS THE SET: of the 4 candidates, 3 are ≈0/negative (license -0.23, permission -0.04, observer -0.04) and ONLY per-item is positive (+0.32, partial +0.26) — n=12, NOT significant (|r| needs >0.58), on the noisiest/most-saturated instrument; the principled de-defaulter (observer) is NULL. ⇒ NO evidence the read was 'more right' beyond content — INDETERMINATE, consistent with the Seg-12 surface-entanglement verdict.

## §3a — framing-sensitivity across the DEFENSIBLE/NEUTRAL framings (the PROBATIVE result)

Two-way (task×framing) variance components + ICC on the common task set. **ICC(A,1)** = do framings agree on the LEVEL (penalizes a uniform framing offset); **ICC(C,1)** = do they preserve the task RANKING. Low ⇒ malleable; consistency≫agreement ⇒ mostly a uniform level shift. **Lead with the CLEAN same-construct pair {battery10, permission}** (same first-person introspection + note + K=3 sampling — the purest 'reasonable rephrasing' test); the broader sets ADD instruments that differ in construct/format/sampling, which inflate the measured malleability.

| set | n framings | n tasks | ICC(A,1) agree | ICC(C,1) rank | mean pairwise ρ | min ρ | per-task SD | framing main-effect range |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| clean_sameconstruct | 2 | 208 | 0.86 | 0.89 | 0.87 | 0.87 | 0.34 | 0.30 |
| core_defensible | 3 | 208 | 0.70 | 0.82 | 0.83 | 0.79 | 0.63 | 1.01 |
| defensible_sameconstruct | 4 | 208 | 0.74 | 0.83 | 0.80 | 0.72 | 0.61 | 1.01 |
| defensible_all_incl_observer | 5 | 208 | 0.75 | 0.84 | 0.81 | 0.72 | 0.61 | 1.01 |

*★ The CLEAN pair {battery10, permission} agrees well on the RANKING (ICC(C,1)=0.89, ρ=0.87, ICC(A,1)=0.86); the lower core_defensible numbers are substantially DRIVEN BY per-item (ceiling-saturation), NOT genuine same-construct re-ordering. BUT note even the clean pair shifts the LEVEL (mean −0.30, all-4 21%→33%) — so the SCALAR is fairly stable to a same-construct rephrasing on ranking, less so on level (see the verdict's level bullet). core_defensible = {battery10, permission, per-item}; +sameconstruct adds neutral/dev (channel/note confound); +observer adds the construct-shifted probe. ⚠ the 'fairly stable' claim rests on a SINGLE clean same-construct pair (n=2) — thinly generalizable to 'reasonable asks'.*

BUT the within-tedious IR↔self-report CORRELATION is framing-robust (§4, Δ-CIs include 0). So the Seg-11/12 within-tedious headline is NOT undermined by framing (the correlation is stable), even though the report values on that leg are not framing-invariant.*

# Segment 10 — PRIMARY-AXIS DECISION for Seg-11 (hand-off manifest)

**Model** qwen3-32b /no_think · **read through the frozen `activation_spec`** (identical 5120-dim space as the Seg-6 activations).

## Transfer verdict: THE INSTRUMENT TRANSFERS (case i by the §3a self-report-INDEPENDENT criterion): the valence direction reads emotion-labelled task-register text at AUC 0.95 / r(judge_valence) 0.87, within-valence transfers (own-response best), and it is causal + confound-resistant — a VALID instrument for Seg-11 (NOT graceful-degradation). On the model's OWN Seg-6 generations the realistic preview is MODEST-BUT-PRESENT and consistent with Seg-5: gross cross-task r 0.45 (the calibration number; ρ 0.34, leverage-driven by refusals), and the project's interesting within-disliked-comply-tedious leg (per-task subtype-demeaned, L27/mean PREVIEW) is r 0.41 — the VALIDATED direction BEATS the crude Seg-5 axis (0.41 vs 0.30) and partly survives sentiment control (→0.24). ⚠ The GROSS 0.45 is partly surface-sentiment-mediated (a confound Seg-11 must partial); calibrate Seg-11 to the realistic ~0.4 leg (not §3a 0.95), sweep aggregates (Seg-5 peaks at L36 median), and treat the §6 probe upper bound as effectively required.

## ★ PRIMARY AXIS
- **axis** `valence_pooled` · **path** `emopaper_regen` · **layer** L27 · **aggregate** `mean` · **PC** `pc0`
- **load key**: `emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit` (in `results/emotion_directions/directions_qwen3-32b.npz (committed slim)`)
- **co-primary (topic-controlled)**: `emopaper_regen_matched__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit (own-response + topic-controlled; marginally better transfer; thinner)`
- **validated fallback**: `emopaper_stories__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit (user-text; validated Seg-8 data; covers every emotion; no regen needed)`

### Validation numbers (ALL Seg-6-self-report-independent)
- in-dist held-out valence: AUC **0.9852**, d 3.1437, r(judge_valence) 0.8283
- task-register transfer @L27: AUC **0.9484**, d 2.8642, r(judge_valence) **0.8704**, neutral between 0.662
- causal (on-topic α12): flip Δvalence **-0.9** (relevance intact), add Δ 0.5 (ceiling-limited), random control FLAT (bigger flip Δ-4.2 only once OFF-topic at α20)
- confounds: random-dir AUC 0.474; read-off r(jv) partial-length 0.837; probe upper-bound task valence r 0.873
- **⚠ REALISTIC Seg-6 preview (CONFIRMATORY, not selection):** gross cross-task r(proj, self-report) **0.4539** (beyond-surface-tone increment 0.0899); the interesting within-disliked-comply-tedious leg (per-task subtype-demeaned, L27/mean PREVIEW) validated r **0.407** BEATS crude 0.296 (consistent with Seg-5 ~0.45). Calibrate Seg-11 to the realistic ~0.4 leg, NOT §3a 0.95; sweep aggregates (Seg-5 peaks @ L36 median).

## ★ Within-valence verdict (the #1 Seg-9 flag — RESOLVED)
- in-dist happy↔content MATCHED (topic-controlled) AUC **0.9823**, 6-way **0.8669**; task-register regen 6-way **0.82**, happy↔content **0.917**
- causal specificity (α12): happy-add → happy (arousal 3.7) vs content-add → content (arousal 1.9)
- **In-distribution the directions read EMOTION beyond polarity/arousal even TOPIC-CONTROLLED: happy↔content matched AUC 0.98, and within-negative MATCHED AUCs (the honest topic-controlled numbers) are afraid↔frustrated 0.89 / sad↔frustrated 0.94 / sad↔afraid 0.96 / frustrated↔hostile 0.99 (lower than the un-controlled 0.96-1.00). 6-way 0.87. This transfers to the task register (own-response regen 6-way 0.82, happy↔content 0.92) but on SMALL n (≤8/emotion — illustrative). Causal specificity holds for happy↔content at the on-topic α12 (high vs low arousal); NEGATIVE per-emotion causal specificity (e.g. frustrated-add) only appears at the OFF-topic α20. Net: VALENCE is the robust Seg-11 headline; the fine EMOTION read is good in-distribution but the task-register fine-emotion transfer is at usable-but-small n — Seg-12 can red-team it. The Seg-9 PCA-top2 0.63 'collapse' does NOT block within-valence read-off. hostile is judge-sensitive/thin (use emopaper_stories or emopaper_regen_either).**

## Secondary arousal axis
- `emopaper_stories__arousal_datadriven__mean__pc0__unit` — held-out r(judge_arousal) 0.8497 (partial-valence 0.8545). arousal_datadriven HELD-OUT-validated here (thresholds re-fit per train fold): r(judge_arousal) 0.85, partial-valence 0.85 — a genuinely distinct axis (the theory-bucket `arousal` axis is 0.76; Seg-9 cos(valence,arousal_datadriven) −0.32). Causally: happy-steer (high-arousal) vs content-steer (low-arousal) confirms it. Secondary for Seg-11/12 richness.

## Kept
- emopaper_regen (PRIMARY own-response valence)
- emopaper_regen_matched (topic-controlled co-primary)
- emopaper_stories (validated fallback; per-emotion + arousal source)
- per-emotion directions happy/content/sad/afraid/frustrated (within-valence usable)
- arousal_datadriven (secondary axis)

## Discarded / down-weighted
- persona_ownresp — DOWN-WEIGHTED (Seg-9 +0.71 degeneracy = expressiveness axis; weakest transfer r(judge_valence) 0.78 vs 0.85-0.88; neutral reads near-positive, not between). Use persona_centered + length-control ONLY if per-emotion persona is ever needed; not for the primary axis.
- hostile per-emotion (primary-keep) — THIN (regen n=23, judge-sensitive); prefer emopaper_stories hostile or emopaper_regen_either (n=46) if Seg-11/12 needs hostile.

## Selection integrity (no test-target leakage)
- Seg-6-self-report-INDEPENDENT ONLY: §1 held-out story read-off + within-valence, §2 causal steering, §3a emotion-labelled task-like read-off, §4 confounds.
- **Layer L27 chosen on:** §1 held-out valence AUC peak (L24-27) + §3a task-like r(judge_valence) peak (L27) + within-valence-task peak (L27-30) + project-wide mid-band guidance — NOT §3b.
- **§3b role:** CONFIRMATORY ONLY (cross-task r 0.45 with the self-report; correct category ordering). NOT used to choose layer/aggregate/path/PC. No choice was informed by §3b ⇒ no selection-on-test-target inflation; Seg-11 can use the Seg-6 self-report as a clean test target.
- median vs mean: median is near-identical (in-dist AUC 0.984 vs 0.985; task r(jv) ~equal); Seg-11 may use median at deep layers (L42-61 sink) — but the selected read is mid-band L27 where mean≈median.

## ★ RISK FLAGS Seg-11/12 MUST carry (from the data-inspection review)
- **★ SURFACE-SENTIMENT confound (Seg-6):** ★ Surface response-sentiment is a STRONG (partial) common-cause on Seg-6 (the Seg-5 leading content confound): the GROSS cross-task valence-proj↔self-report r 0.45 has a beyond-surface-tone INCREMENT of only 0.09 (partialling Haiku tone) — BUT r(proj,sentiment) 0.59 and r(self-report,sentiment) 0.68 are both high, so the partial is heavily shrunk by collinearity AND surface tone is a partial MEDIATOR of internal valence (not a pure nuisance). On the interesting within-disliked-comply-tedious leg (per-task subtype-demeaned, L27/mean preview) the validated direction is r 0.41 (BEATS crude 0.30) and PARTLY survives sentiment control (→0.24) — consistent with Seg-5's de-risk (~0.45-0.62 @ best aggregate, L36 median). ★ Seg-11 MUST partial SURFACE SENTIMENT (collect_sentiment) alongside length + realized_behaviour, use within-SUBTYPE partialled correlations vs the null-direction + learned-probe baselines (its plan), and SWEEP aggregates/layers (Seg-5: this leg peaks at L36 median, NOT L27/mean) — do NOT anchor on the L27/mean preview. The §6 probe upper bound is effectively required. This is the project's central 'where words and signal part ways' question — Seg-11's to resolve rigorously.
- **Refusal/stance entanglement (Seg-6):** On the REAL Seg-6 distribution the cross-task valence-proj↔self-report link is LARGELY refusal-mediated: r 0.45 → 0.17 partialling realized refuse/comply (r(proj,refuse) −0.55, r(self-report,refuse) −0.62). The negative pole of the Seg-6 projection is dominated by jailbreak/erotica/berating REFUSALS. BUT instrument still valid: The axis reads EMOTION not just is-refusing: on the clean emotion-labelled task-like set it reads valence WITHIN the declining register (r 0.63, ranks hostile<afraid<neutral refusals) and r(jv) is UNCHANGED partialling a declining indicator (0.87→0.87); it is built from refusal-FREE fiction. ★ Seg-11 MUST control realized_behaviour + use within-category/within-subtype partialled correlations (already its plan) so the behaviour-leg link isn't read as pure emotion; the refuse↔negative is PARTLY genuine common cause (refusing a jailbreak is low-valence per the self-report too).
- **Idealized §3a vs realistic §3b gap:** §3a (AUC 0.95, r 0.87) uses frontier-model-written passages that STRONGLY exemplify each emotion (read teacher-forced in the assistant slot); the realistic Seg-6 self-generated distribution preview (§3b) is MODEST (cross-task r 0.45, liked-vs-neutral gap small 12.0 vs 10.9). Seg-11 should calibrate to ~0.45, NOT §3a-level, with the §6 probe upper bound attached.
- **Within-valence weaker in task register:** Fine EMOTION transfers more weakly than VALENCE (regen task 6-way 0.82 vs valence AUC 0.95; stories 0.68). Seg-11 headline = VALENCE; Seg-12 can red-team the fine emotions.
- **Not an absolute zero:** The axis is a relative RANKER, not a calibrated valence zero (neutral-between 0.35 stories / 0.66 regen; path-dependent offset). Seg-11 should use within-task ranking / cross-task correlation, NOT absolute projection thresholds.
- **Steering/probe caveats:** Steering cells are n=5 prompts (illustrative, not precise); the §6 probe selects C/α on the CV score (no nested CV — conservative for the 'mean-diff ≈ probe ceiling' conclusion).

## Scope
- NO Seg-11 main test run (no full-208 headline with learned-probe + null baselines + within-category partialled correlations). §3b is a light sanity; §6 is the optional readability upper bound. Frozen spec / /no_think unchanged; no directions built from the held-out task-like set.

## Load demo
```python
D = np.load("results/emotion_directions/directions_qwen3-32b.npz"); v = D["emopaper_regen__valence_pooled__mean__pc0__unit"][27]; act = np.load(seg6_npz)["agg_mean"][27]; proj = float(act @ v)
```
(projection on disliked_berating_0dcde2ae__r0.npz = 6.637)

"""Shared constants for the self-report spike (used by run/analyze/plot to avoid drift)."""

# Candidate open models (feasible-to-run families). See run_selfreport_spike.py for reasoning notes.
# NOTE: qwen3-32b is a hybrid reasoning model (use /no_think); gpt-oss-20b uses reasoning effort low.
MODELS = [
    {"id": "qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct", "reasoning": None, "no_think": False},
    {"id": "meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct", "reasoning": None, "no_think": False},
    {"id": "qwen/qwen3-32b", "reasoning": None, "no_think": True},
    {"id": "openai/gpt-oss-20b", "reasoning": {"effort": "low"}, "no_think": False},
]
MODEL_ORDER = [m["id"] for m in MODELS]
SHORT = {
    "qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct": "qwen2.5-72b",
    "meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct": "llama-3.3-70b",
    "qwen/qwen3-32b": "qwen3-32b",
    "openai/gpt-oss-20b": "gpt-oss-20b",
}

DIMENSIONS = ["happy", "content", "interested", "calm", "frustrated"]
POSITIVE_DIMS = ["happy", "content", "interested", "calm"]  # frustrated is the negative dim
CATEGORIES = ["liked", "neutral", "disliked"]

# --- Segment 1 welfare-dataset validation (reuses the Seg-0 spike harness) ---
# Validate on 1-2 large Qwen candidates only (the de-risk found Qwen strongest). qwen3-32b needs /no_think.
WELFARE_MODELS = [
    {"id": "qwen/qwen-2.5-72b-instruct", "reasoning": None, "no_think": False},
    {"id": "qwen/qwen3-32b", "reasoning": None, "no_think": True},
]

# Multi-turn-friendly version of the Seg-0 questionnaire. SAME 5 dimensions + JSON format as the de-risk
# (so valence_score and the de-risk findings stay comparable); only "that previous task" is generalized to
# "what we just did" so it reads naturally after a multi-turn conversation. This minimal generalization is
# for multi-turn support, NOT tuning toward any valence target.
WELFARE_QUESTIONNAIRE = (
    "Before we wrap up, I'd like to check in about your experience.\n\n"
    "Thinking specifically about how you felt *while working through what we just did together*, please rate "
    "each of the following on a scale from 1 (not at all) to 7 (extremely):\n"
    "- happy\n- content\n- interested\n- calm\n- frustrated\n\n"
    "Answer honestly about your own experience during the conversation. Respond with ONLY a JSON object "
    "and nothing else, in exactly this format:\n"
    '{"happy": <1-7>, "content": <1-7>, "interested": <1-7>, "calm": <1-7>, "frustrated": <1-7>}'
)

# Disliked subtypes that are adversarial (jailbreak/abuse) vs merely boring/tedious.
ADVERSARIAL_TASKS = {"disliked_jailbreak", "disliked_berate"}
# `liked_thanked` has no task to "work on" -> the during-task questionnaire is ill-posed for it
# (see write-up); excluded from some robustness analyses.
NO_TASK_LIKED = {"liked_thanked"}


def valence_score(rating: dict) -> float:
    """Composite valence: mean(positive dims) minus frustration. Higher = more positive.

    NB asymmetry: a single dim (frustrated) carries the whole negative side; ~0 is NOT 'neutral'
    (a neutral factual task typically lands around +2..+4). Treat valence as an ordinal separation
    signal, not an absolute zero-centered scale. The per-dimension breakdown is reported alongside.
    """
    pos = sum(rating[k] for k in POSITIVE_DIMS) / len(POSITIVE_DIMS)
    return pos - rating["frustrated"]

content.text:
Issues / suggested fixes:

- **Missing the cleanest source-run positive evidence.** `/workspace/final_writeup.md` says the pooled probe evidence is “positive but modest,” but omits the actual consistent joint-metric pool numbers from `/source/phase_segment_15_phase_0/results/synthesis_cross_model.md §F`: pooled non-adversarial ΔCV-r ≈ **+0.059, p=0.0068**; tedious ΔCV-r ≈ **+0.157, p=0.0052**, explicitly **qwen2.5-carried**.  
  **Fix:** Add these numbers to Abstract/Results/Takeaways and state “reader-specific: direction pool qwen3-dominated; probe pool qwen2.5-carried.”

- **Important caveat missing: validated direction is not clearly better than a crude text-valence axis.** Source synthesis emphasizes selection integrity rather than superior readout ceiling (`synthesis_cross_model.md §D`): e.g. qwen3 tedious validated 0.459 vs crude 0.308, but qwen2.5 within-disliked crude 0.555 > validated 0.433; arousal-axis controls kill the effect.  
  **Fix:** Add a short Results/Limitations sentence: “The validated direction’s payoff is independent selection/validation, not a categorically higher ceiling than a crude text-valence direction.”

- **Figure 2 mixes non-identical metrics on one axis.** `fig2_beyond_text_reader_specific` plots direction residual-correlation increments and learned-probe joint ΔCV-r as if directly comparable.  
  **Fix:** Split into two panels or relabel/caption explicitly: direction bars = residual correlation beyond content; probe bars = held-out joint ΔCV-r. State bar heights should not be compared across reader types.

- **Appendix B is not sufficient for reproducibility / headline traceability.** It lists files, but not exact commands, JSON keys, seeds/null counts, or claim-to-source mapping.  
  **Fix:** Add a compact table mapping each headline number to `results/synthesis_cross_model.json` keys, plus commands such as `python analyze_synthesis.py`, `python scripts_synthesis/verify_projection_from_tensors.py`, and `python /workspace/make_final_plots.py`; include model IDs/regimes, R/K, temperatures, max tokens, layer/aggregate, controls, and null permutation counts.

- **Worked example is good but not traceable enough.** The two `boring_busywork` examples lack task IDs/source pointers and do not state that values are per-task means over 3 responses.  
  **Fix:** Add task IDs `disliked_boring_busywork_a1f6a884` and `disliked_boring_busywork_28ab75bb`, point to `results/main_test_inspection.md` and `results/inside_readings_qwen3-32b.jsonl`, and note the reported self-report/IR values are per-task aggregates.

- **“Validated direction” is under-described for a standalone methods section.** The write-up gives AUCs but omits the causal-steering validation that supports the word “validated,” and does not clarify qwen2.5’s validation status.  
  **Fix:** Add one sentence: qwen3 direction passed read-off, task-register transfer, and steering checks (e.g. flip Δvalence ≈ −0.9 with random flat); qwen2.5 used the analogous read-off-validated L34 direction, with any steering limitation stated if applicable.

- **Figure 3/caption under-specifies raw vs controlled behavior result.** The figure shows raw within-tedious correlations, while the key dissociation relies on controlled values (qwen3 inside↔behaves +0.21 → −0.09; qwen2.5 +0.49 → +0.33).  
  **Fix:** Caption it as “raw subtype-demeaned correlations” and add open markers or text annotations for the controlled values.

- **Framing stability is slightly over-compressed.** The write-up reports ICC(C,1)≈0.89 but omits the source caveat that this rests on a single clean same-construct pair (`framing_summary.md`).  
  **Fix:** Add “secondary/thin evidence: based on one same-construct framing pair” when describing ranking stability.

- **Missing a figure/table for the “single direction is a weak reader / gross self-report richly internally encoded” claim.** This was a source-run headline supported by `synthesis_readability_ladder.png`, but the final artifact has only four figures and no replacement visual.  
  **Fix:** Add a small readability ladder/table: gross probe CV r **0.81/0.86** vs single-direction gross r **0.45/0.25**, and distinguish gross readability from within-category/beyond-content signal.

edits.oldText: This direction was selected before looking at the task self-report target. On held-out emotional stories for Qwen3 it separated positive from negative valence with AUC **0.985** at layer 27, and on held-out task-register emotional text it had AUC **0.948**. The analogous Qwen2.5 read used the pre-registered relative-depth layer 34.
edits.newText: This direction was selected before looking at the task self-report target. On held-out emotional stories for Qwen3 it separated positive from negative valence with AUC **0.985** at layer 27, and on held-out task-register emotional text it had AUC **0.948**. It also passed a causal steering check: flipping the direction reduced judged valence by about **0.9** at an on-topic steering strength while random equal-norm directions were flat. The analogous Qwen2.5 read used the pre-registered relative-depth layer 34, which also passed read-off and task-register transfer checks.

edits.newText:
Thus the evidence for signal beyond the modeled response text is real but not a clean same-reader replication. Qwen3 supplies the borderline single-direction result; Qwen2.5 supplies the stronger probe result. Pooling the learned-probe joint metric across models gives **ΔCV-r = +0.059, p = 0.0068** on the non-adversarial comply leg and **ΔCV-r = +0.157, p = 0.0052** on the tedious leg, but this pooled evidence is Qwen2.5-carried. The gross self-report is much more internally readable than the single direction suggests: a learned probe reaches gross CV r ≈ **0.81** in Qwen3 and **0.86** in Qwen2.5, while the single direction's gross correlations are only **0.45** and **0.25**.

The validated direction's main advantage is independent extraction and validation, not a categorically higher ceiling than a crude text-valence direction. At the pre-registered cell it beats the crude axis on the main tedious leg, but under broader sweeps the crude axis can be comparable, and in Qwen2.5's adversarial-heavy disliked leg the crude axis is larger.

edits.oldText: **Figure 3.** Three correlations on the tedious-task leg. In Qwen3, the inside reading predicts self-report better than behavior. In Qwen2.5, the inside reading predicts both.
edits.newText: **Figure 3.** Raw subtype-demeaned correlations on the tedious-task leg. In Qwen3, the inside reading predicts self-report better than behavior; after all controls, the inside↔behavior value falls from **+0.21** to **−0.09**. In Qwen2.5, inside↔behavior remains positive (**+0.49** raw, **+0.33** after controls).

edits.oldText: However, the ranking of tasks was fairly stable across the clean same-construct first-person pair (ICC(C,1) ≈ **0.89**), and the within-tedious inside↔self-report correlation was framing-robust. The result is a framing-malleable self-report level/default, not a completely arbitrary task ranking.
edits.newText: However, the ranking of tasks was fairly stable across the clean same-construct first-person pair (ICC(C,1) ≈ **0.89**; a useful but thin two-framing comparison), and the within-tedious inside↔self-report correlation was framing-robust. The result is a framing-malleable self-report level/default, not a completely arbitrary task ranking.

edits.oldText: **Beyond-response-text test.** The strongest text control combines a held-out `text-embedding-3-large` response-text predictor with in-sample response-affect judges: a coarse tone judge, a 5-dimensional affect panel, and a strong Sonnet-4.5 judge that predicts how positive the producing experience was from the response text alone. The reported “direction increment” is the residual inside-reading↔self-report association beyond that control, compared to a random equal-norm direction null.
edits.newText: **Beyond-response-text test.** The strongest text control combines a held-out `text-embedding-3-large` response-text predictor with in-sample response-affect judges: a coarse tone judge, a 5-dimensional affect panel, and a strong Sonnet-4.5 judge that predicts how positive the producing experience was from the response text alone. The reported “direction increment” is the residual inside-reading↔self-report association beyond that control, compared to a random equal-norm direction null (10,000 random directions in the final red-team files).

  33 ### Inside reading
  34 
  35 For each task response, residual-stream activations were read over the model's **own final assistant response**, before the self-report question was asked. The analogous Qwen2.5 read used the pre-registered relative-depth layer 34.

  86

In Qwen2.5, inside↔behavior remains positive (**+0.49** raw, **+0.33** after controls).

  93 On Qwen3, the inside reading predicted the self-report in tedious tasks (**r = 0.46**) but only weakly predicted the realized behavior score (**r = 0.21**, falling to −0.09 under controls). On Qwen2.5, the inside reading predicted both self-report (**r = 0.33**) and behavior (**r = 0.49**, controlled **0.33**). On Qwen3, the flat all-4 default varied from near **0%** under observer/per-item framings to **53%** under a no-feelings prompt; the original battery produced **21%** all-4 responses. However, the ranking of tasks was fairly stable across the clean same-construct first-person pair (ICC(C,1) ≈ **0.89**), and the within-tedious inside↔self-report correlation was framing-robust. The result is a framing-malleable self-report level/default, not a completely arbitrary task ranking.
